Architecture foundation, repository skeleton, Dockerized startup, and requirement traceability

Goal of This Phase

Create the repository foundation so the rest of the system can be built incrementally without structural drift. This phase exists to lock in architecture, Docker startup, module boundaries, requirement traceability, and audit-readiness before feature implementation begins.

Scope Boundaries

Do in this phase:

create full repo structure
create Docker Compose startup path
establish backend/frontend app skeletons
define domain modules and route map
define configuration, migration, seed, and storage strategy
write requirement traceability docs and implementation plan docs
create placeholder-safe but real compile-oriented scaffolding where appropriate

Do not do yet:

no full business feature implementation
no fake endpoints claiming completion
no unfinished mock dashboards pretending to satisfy requirements
no shallow auth; only skeleton/stubs if needed for compile path
no partial feature docs that imply completed functionality

Preserve these assumptions:

offline-only deployment
Dioxus + Rocket + MySQL
local file storage
strict RBAC and masking requirements
static-only audit expectations

Instructions:

You are building the initial foundation of a production-grade, fully offline scholarly resources and teaching operations system.

This phase is architecture-first. Do not implement the whole product yet. Create a statically reviewable repository foundation that is clearly designed to satisfy the project requirements without hidden setup or architectural ambiguity.

Non-negotiable requirements for this phase:
1. The project must be startable with one command only: `docker compose up`.
2. All services and ports must be explicitly declared in `docker-compose.yml`.
3. No manual migration, seed, or setup step may be required outside container startup.
4. The repository must be structured like a real engineering project, not a demo.
5. The repository must be statically understandable by a strict reviewer who will not run the code.

Implement the following in this phase:

A. Repository structure
Create a clean root layout with at least:
- README.md
- docker-compose.yml
- .gitignore
- .dockerignore
- run_tests.sh
- docs/
- backend/
- frontend/
- unit_tests/
- API_tests/

B. Backend skeleton (Rocket / Rust)
Create a modular Rocket backend skeleton with clear directory separation such as:
- src/api/
- src/application/
- src/domain/
- src/infrastructure/
- src/config/
- src/errors/
- src/lib.rs / src/main.rs
- migrations/
- seeds/
- scripts/ or entrypoint files needed for automated startup

Define module placeholders and route namespaces for:
- auth
- users / roles / permissions
- journals
- teaching_resources
- courses
- sections
- attachments
- checkins
- metrics
- dashboards
- reports
- audit_logs
- admin_config
- retention

Do not implement fake business logic. If a route is scaffolded now, it must be clearly marked as not yet implemented and not documented as complete.

C. Frontend skeleton (Dioxus)
Create a professional Dioxus web app structure with semantic organization such as:
- src/components/
- src/pages/
- src/layouts/
- src/hooks/ or services/
- src/api/
- src/state/
- src/types/
- src/routes/

Define role-aware navigation structure for:
- dashboard
- library serials/resources
- courses/sections
- imports/exports
- check-in
- reports
- audit logs
- admin settings

Navigation must be declarative and role-filterable, even if route internals are not fully implemented yet.

D. Docker and startup automation
Create a working Docker Compose architecture with:
- mysql service
- backend service
- frontend service

Requirements:
- explicit ports
- named volumes for MySQL data and local attachment storage
- environment variables declared in Compose
- automated backend startup flow that includes waiting for DB, running migrations, running deterministic seeds, then starting the server
- no undeclared host dependency
- no private registries

E. Data and architecture documentation
Create documentation in docs/ that statically maps the task requirements into engineering design:
- docs/architecture.md
- docs/domain_model.md
- docs/api_surface.md
- docs/requirement_traceability.md
- docs/security_model.md

These docs must include:
- core domains and relationships
- role matrix and visibility rules
- versioning strategy for journals/courses/metric definitions
- local file storage strategy
- check-in anti-duplicate strategy
- audit log immutability and chained hash strategy
- retention and deletion strategy
- report scheduling strategy
- masking/encryption assumptions
- requirement-to-module mapping

F. Initial database migration plan
Create initial SQL migration files or migration scaffolding that clearly define the planned tables for:
- users
- roles
- user_roles
- sessions
- failed_login_attempts or equivalent lockout model
- departments / scopes
- journals and journal_versions
- teaching_resources and resource_versions
- courses and course_versions
- sections and section_versions if needed
- attachments metadata
- checkin_events
- metric_definitions and metric_definition_versions
- dashboard definitions if needed
- reports and report_runs
- audit_logs
- audit_hash_chain
- retention_policies

At this phase, migration files can be incomplete on columns if necessary, but table intent and naming must be clear and serious. Do not invent irrelevant tables.

G. Seed strategy
Add deterministic seed planning and initial seed files for:
- at least one user per role
- department/scope examples
- basic configuration defaults
- README-aligned credentials placeholders to be refined later

H. README
Write a real README that accurately describes the current state of the project and explicitly says which features are scaffolded vs implemented. It must include:
- project overview
- chosen stack
- architecture summary
- folder structure
- docker startup instructions
- exposed ports
- current phase status
- constraints and assumptions

I. Audit-readiness requirements
Throughout the code and docs:
- do not use giant monolithic files
- do not use mock business outcomes
- do not document unimplemented features as done
- keep naming professional
- add TODO markers only where bounded and specific
- ensure a reviewer can see where each major requirement will live

J. Testing scaffolding
Create the test folder structure and initial harness setup:
- unit_tests/
- API_tests/
- backend unit test configuration
- backend integration/API test configuration
- placeholder test files only if they assert something real (for example startup config validation or route registration)
- run_tests.sh must exist and call both suites, even if some tests are placeholders at this stage

At the end of the phase, provide a concise implementation summary in docs/phase_1_summary.md that lists:
- what was created
- what remains intentionally unimplemented
- where the next phase should continue

Do not silently simplify any requirement from the original task. If something is ambiguous, choose the safer production-grade interpretation and document it.

Deliverables Expected at End of Phase

docker-compose.yml
.gitignore
.dockerignore
README.md
run_tests.sh
backend/ skeleton with modular folders
frontend/ skeleton with role-aware route/nav structure
initial migration scaffolding
seed scaffolding
docs/architecture.md
docs/domain_model.md
docs/api_surface.md
docs/requirement_traceability.md
docs/security_model.md
docs/phase_1_summary.md

Static Review Checklist for This Phase

Is the repo structure professional and decomposed?
Is docker compose up clearly the intended startup path?
Are services, ports, volumes, and env vars explicit?
Is there clear requirement-to-module traceability?
Are unimplemented areas honestly marked?
Are test folders present and wired?
Does README match the current repo state?

Stop Condition / Hand-off Rule

Stop when the architecture, Docker startup path, docs, and module scaffolding are in place and reviewable, but before implementing real auth or business features.
I'll start by checking the current state of the repository, then build this foundation systematically.

{"file_path": "/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/run_tests.sh", "content": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"

echo "========================================="
echo "  Scholarly - Test Suite Runner"
echo "========================================="

FAILED=0

# ------------------------------------------
# 1. Backend unit tests
# ------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "-----------------------------------------"
echo "  Running backend unit tests..."
echo "-----------------------------------------"
cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/backend"
if cargo test --lib 2>&1; then
    echo "[PASS] Backend unit tests passed."
else
    echo "[FAIL] Backend unit tests failed."
    FAILED=1
fi

# ------------------------------------------
# 2. Backend integration tests
# ------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "-----------------------------------------"
echo "  Running backend integration tests..."
echo "-----------------------------------------"
if cargo test --test '*' 2>&1; then
    echo "[PASS] Backend integration tests passed."
else
    echo "[FAIL] Backend integration tests failed."
    FAILED=1
fi

# ------------------------------------------
# 3. Frontend tests
# ------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "-----------------------------------------"
echo "  Running frontend tests..."
echo "-----------------------------------------"
cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/frontend"
if cargo test 2>&1; then
    echo "[PASS] Frontend tests passed."
else
    echo "[FAIL] Frontend tests failed."
    FAILED=1
fi

# ------------------------------------------
# 4. API tests (requires running services)
# ------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "-----------------------------------------"
echo "  API tests..."
echo "-----------------------------------------"
if [ -d "$SCRIPT_DIR/API_tests" ] && ls "$SCRIPT_DIR/API_tests"/*.sh 1>/dev/null 2>&1; then
    for test_file in "$SCRIPT_DIR/API_tests"/*.sh; do
        echo "  Running: $(basename "$test_file")"
        if bash "$test_file" 2>&1; then
            echo "  [PASS] $(basename "$test_file")"
        else
            echo "  [FAIL] $(basename "$test_file")"
            FAILED=1
        fi
    done
else
    echo "  [SKIP] No API test scripts found yet."
fi

# ------------------------------------------
# Summary
# ------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "========================================="
if [ $FAILED -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "  ALL TESTS PASSED"
else
    echo "  SOME TESTS FAILED"
fi
echo "========================================="

exit $FAILED
"}

content.arguments:
{"file_path": "/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/docker-compose.yml", "content": "version: "3.9"

services:
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────
  # MySQL 8.0 — primary data store
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────
  mysql:
    image: mysql:8.0
    container_name: scholarly-mysql
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "3306:3306"
    environment:
      MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD: scholarly_root_pass
      MYSQL_DATABASE: scholarly
      MYSQL_USER: scholarly_app
      MYSQL_PASSWORD: scholarly_app_pass
    volumes:
      - mysql_data:/var/lib/mysql
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "mysqladmin", "ping", "-h", "localhost", "-u", "root", "-pscholarly_root_pass"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 30
    networks:
      - scholarly-net

  # ──────────────────────────────────────────
  # Backend — Rocket API server (Rust)
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────
  backend:
    build:
      context: ./backend
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    container_name: scholarly-backend
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
    environment:
      ROCKET_ADDRESS: "0.0.0.0"
      ROCKET_PORT: 8000
      DATABASE_URL: "mysql://scholarly_app:scholarly_app_pass@mysql:3306/scholarly"
      ATTACHMENT_STORAGE_PATH: "/data/attachments"
      RUST_LOG: "info"
      JWT_SECRET: "CHANGE_ME_IN_PRODUCTION"
    volumes:
      - attachment_storage:/data/attachments
    depends_on:
      mysql:
        condition: service_healthy
    networks:
      - scholarly-net

  # ──────────────────────────────────────────
  # Frontend — Dioxus web app (served via static files)
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────
  frontend:
    build:
      context: ./frontend
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    container_name: scholarly-frontend
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "3000:80"
    depends_on:
      - backend
    networks:
      - scholarly-net

volumes:
  mysql_data:
    driver: local
  attachment_storage:
    driver: local

networks:
  scholarly-net:
    driver: bridge
"}

All 13 domain model files have been created under `/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/backend/src/domain/`. The existing `mod.rs` already had all 13 submodule declarations, so no changes were needed there.

Summary of what each file contains:

1. **user.rs** -- `User` struct (id, email, display_name, password_hash, status, avatar_url, phone, timestamps) and `UserStatus` enum (PendingVerification, Active, Suspended, Deactivated)
2. **role.rs** -- `Role` (name, display_name), `Permission` (colon-delimited key), `UserRole` join (user_id, role_id, assigned_by)
3. **auth.rs** -- `Session` (refresh_token_hash, ip_address, expires_at, revoked_at), `FailedLoginAttempt` (email, ip, reason), `LoginCredentials` (ephemeral value object)
4. **journal.rs** -- `Journal` (author_id, title, is_published, current_version_id) and `JournalVersion` (body, change_summary, version_number)
5. **teaching_resource.rs** -- `TeachingResource` (owner_id, resource_type, tags), `ResourceVersion` (content_url, mime_type, size_bytes), `ResourceType` enum (Document, Video, Presentation, Assessment, ExternalLink, Dataset, Other)
6. **course.rs** -- `Course` (code, title, owner_id) and `CourseVersion` (description, syllabus, credit_hours)
7. **section.rs** -- `Section` (course_id, instructor_id, section_code, term, capacity) and `SectionVersion` (location, schedule, notes)
8. **checkin.rs** -- `CheckinEvent` (user_id, section_id, checked_in_at, lat/lng, is_validated), `CheckinType` enum (QrCode, Geofence, ManualInstructor, NfcBeacon), plus doc-comment describing the anti-duplicate strategy (unique constraint on user_id + section_id + event_date, with a configurable debounce window)
9. **metric.rs** -- `MetricDefinition` (key, display_name, unit, polarity) and `MetricDefinitionVersion` (formula, description)
10. **dashboard.rs** -- `DashboardDefinition` (title, is_shared, layout_json) and `DashboardWidget` (metric_definition_id, widget_type, config_json, position)
11. **report.rs** -- `Report` (query_definition, default_format), `ReportRun` (status, artifact_url, error_message), `ReportSchedule` (cron_expression, notify_recipients), `ReportFormat` enum (Pdf, Csv, Excel, Html, Json)
12. **audit.rs** -- `AuditLog` (actor_id, action, target_entity_type/id, change_payload) and `AuditHashChain` (previous_hash, current_hash, sequence_number) with doc comments explaining the tamper-evident chain
13. **retention.rs** -- `RetentionPolicy` (target_entity_type, retention_days, action, rationale) and `RetentionAction` enum (Delete, Anonymize, Archive, FlagForReview)

All structs use `Uuid` for IDs, `NaiveDateTime` for timestamps, carry `#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]`, and have TODO comments marking fields whose details will be finalised in phase 2.

{"description": "Create frontend Dioxus skeleton", "prompt": "Create a complete Dioxus web frontend skeleton for a scholarly resources system at:
/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/frontend/

1. Create Cargo.toml with:
- name = "scholarly-frontend"
- edition = "2021"
- dependencies: dioxus = { version = "0.6", features = ["web"] }, dioxus-router = "0.6", serde/serde_json, gloo-net (for HTTP), chrono

2. Create Dioxus.toml:
```toml
[application]
name = "scholarly-frontend"

[web.app]
title = "Scholarly"

[web.watcher]
watch_path = ["src"]

[web.resource]
style = ["assets/main.css"]
```

3. Create assets/main.css with a minimal professional CSS reset and layout variables.

4. Create src/main.rs that initializes the Dioxus web app and launches the router.

5. Create src/router.rs with a declarative route enum using dioxus-router. Routes:
- / -> Dashboard
- /library/journals -> JournalList
- /library/resources -> ResourceList
- /courses -> CourseList
- /courses/:id -> CourseDetail
- /courses/:id/sections -> SectionList
- /imports -> ImportExport
- /checkin -> CheckIn
- /reports -> Reports
- /audit -> AuditLogs
- /admin/settings -> AdminSettings
- /admin/retention -> RetentionSettings
- /login -> Login

6. Create src/layouts/mod.rs and src/layouts/main_layout.rs:
- MainLayout component that renders a sidebar nav + content area
- Role-aware navigation using a NavItem struct with required_role field
- Define all nav items declaratively in a NAVIGATION constant/function
- Roles: Admin, Librarian, Instructor, DepartmentHead, Viewer

7. Create stub page components in src/pages/mod.rs and individual files:
- dashboard.rs, journal_list.rs, resource_list.rs, course_list.rs, course_detail.rs, section_list.rs, import_export.rs, checkin.rs, reports.rs, audit_logs.rs, admin_settings.rs, retention_settings.rs, login.rs
- Each page is a simple Dioxus component returning an rsx! block with a heading and "Phase 1 stub" note

8. Create src/components/mod.rs with placeholder submodules:
- header.rs, sidebar.rs, data_table.rs, search_bar.rs, role_badge.rs

9. Create src/hooks/mod.rs with placeholder submodules:
- use_auth.rs (auth state hook), use_api.rs (API client hook)

10. Create src/api/mod.rs with placeholder submodules:
- client.rs (HTTP client config pointing to backend at port 8000)

11. Create src/state/mod.rs:
- AuthState struct (user, role, token)
- AppState struct

12. Create src/types/mod.rs:
- Re-export common types used across the frontend (Role enum, User, etc.)

All files should have doc comments. Components should be real Dioxus components (fn ComponentName() -> Element using rsx!). Mark unimplemented functionality with TODO comments.

Write all files."}

File: 001_create_users_and_auth.sql
Tables:
- users (id CHAR(36) PK, email VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL, display_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, password_hash VARCHAR(512) NOT NULL, status ENUM('pending_verification','active','suspended','deactivated') DEFAULT 'active', phone VARCHAR(50), avatar_url TEXT, department_id CHAR(36), created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)

- permissions (id CHAR(36) PK, key_name VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL, description TEXT, created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)

- user_roles (id CHAR(36) PK, user_id CHAR(36) NOT NULL, role_id CHAR(36) NOT NULL, assigned_by CHAR(36), assigned_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, UNIQUE(user_id, role_id), FKs)
- sessions (id CHAR(36) PK, user_id CHAR(36) NOT NULL FK, refresh_token_hash VARCHAR(512) NOT NULL, ip_address VARCHAR(45), user_agent TEXT, expires_at DATETIME NOT NULL, revoked_at DATETIME, created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)
- failed_login_attempts (id CHAR(36) PK, email VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, ip_address VARCHAR(45), attempted_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, reason VARCHAR(255))
- departments (id CHAR(36) PK, name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, code VARCHAR(50) UNIQUE NOT NULL, parent_department_id CHAR(36) FK self-ref, created_at/updated_at)

Add FK from users.department_id to departments.id via ALTER TABLE at end.

- teaching_resources (id, owner_id FK users, title VARCHAR(500), resource_type ENUM('document','video','presentation','assessment','external_link','dataset','other'), tags JSON, is_published BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE, current_version_id CHAR(36), created_at, updated_at)
- resource_versions (id, resource_id FK, version_number INT, content_url TEXT, mime_type VARCHAR(255), size_bytes BIGINT, description TEXT, change_summary TEXT, created_by FK users, created_at, UNIQUE(resource_id, version_number))

File: 004_create_courses_and_sections.sql
- courses (id, code VARCHAR(50) UNIQUE, title VARCHAR(500), department_id FK departments, owner_id FK users, is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE, current_version_id CHAR(36), created_at, updated_at)
- course_versions (id, course_id FK, version_number INT, description TEXT, syllabus LONGTEXT, credit_hours DECIMAL(3,1), change_summary TEXT, created_by FK users, created_at, UNIQUE(course_id, version_number))
- sections (id, course_id FK, instructor_id FK users, section_code VARCHAR(50), term VARCHAR(50), year INT, capacity INT, is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE, current_version_id CHAR(36), created_at, updated_at, UNIQUE(course_id, section_code, term, year))
- section_versions (id, section_id FK, version_number INT, location VARCHAR(255), schedule_json JSON, notes TEXT, created_by FK users, created_at, UNIQUE(section_id, version_number))

- checkin_events (id, user_id FK users, section_id FK sections, checkin_type ENUM('qr_code','geofence','manual_instructor','nfc_beacon'), checked_in_at DATETIME NOT NULL, event_date DATE NOT NULL, latitude DECIMAL(10,8), longitude DECIMAL(11,8), device_info TEXT, is_validated BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE, validated_by CHAR(36) FK users, created_at, UNIQUE(user_id, section_id, event_date) for anti-duplicate)

- report_runs (id, report_id FK, status ENUM('queued','running','completed','failed','cancelled') DEFAULT 'queued', parameters_json JSON, format ENUM('pdf','csv','excel','html','json'), artifact_path TEXT, error_message TEXT, started_at DATETIME, completed_at DATETIME, triggered_by FK users, created_at)
- report_schedules (id, report_id FK, cron_expression VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL, is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE, parameters_json JSON, format ENUM('pdf','csv','excel','html','json'), notify_recipients JSON, last_run_at DATETIME, next_run_at DATETIME, created_by FK users, created_at, updated_at)

File: 009_create_audit_logs.sql

- audit_hash_chain (id, audit_log_id FK UNIQUE, sequence_number BIGINT UNIQUE NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT-like, previous_hash VARCHAR(128), current_hash VARCHAR(128) NOT NULL, created_at)
Note: For audit_hash_chain, sequence_number should use BIGINT NOT NULL with a UNIQUE constraint (not auto-increment since it's computed by the application)

- retention_policies (id, target_entity_type VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL, retention_days INT NOT NULL, action ENUM('delete','anonymize','archive','flag_for_review') NOT NULL, rationale TEXT, is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE, created_by FK users, last_executed_at DATETIME, created_at, updated_at, UNIQUE(target_entity_type))

Every migration file should start with a comment block describing what it creates. Use InnoDB engine and utf8mb4 charset.

Write all files."}

content.name: Agent
content.arguments:
{"description": "Create Docker and seed files", "prompt": "Create Docker automation files and seed data for the Scholarly project.

1. Backend Dockerfile at /home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/backend/Dockerfile:
- Multi-stage build
- Stage 1 "builder": FROM rust:1.77-bookworm, copy source, cargo build --release
- Stage 2: FROM debian:bookworm-slim, install ca-certificates and default-mysql-client (for mysql CLI used in entrypoint), copy binary from builder, copy migrations/ seeds/ scripts/, expose 8000, entrypoint scripts/entrypoint.sh

2. Backend entrypoint script at /home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/backend/scripts/entrypoint.sh:
- #!/usr/bin/env bash with set -euo pipefail
- Wait for MySQL to be ready (loop checking mysqladmin ping or mysql connection)
- Run all migration .sql files in order from /app/migrations/ using mysql CLI
- Run all seed .sql files in order from /app/seeds/ using mysql CLI  
- Start the backend binary
- Use DATABASE_URL env var parsing to extract host/user/pass/db

3. Frontend Dockerfile at /home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/frontend/Dockerfile:
- Multi-stage build
- Stage 1 "builder": FROM rust:1.77-bookworm, install dioxus-cli (cargo install dioxus-cli), copy source, dx build --release
- Stage 2: FROM nginx:alpine, copy dist/ from builder to /usr/share/nginx/html, copy custom nginx config, expose 80
- Create a minimal nginx.conf that serves static files and proxies /api/* to backend:8000

4. Create the nginx config at /home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/frontend/nginx.conf:
- Listen on port 80
- Serve static files from /usr/share/nginx/html
- try_files for SPA routing (fallback to index.html)
- Proxy /api/ to http://scholarly-backend:8000/api/

5. Create seed files at /home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/backend/seeds/:

001_seed_roles_and_permissions.sql:
- INSERT IGNORE roles: admin, librarian, instructor, department_head, viewer (with UUIDs)
- INSERT IGNORE core permissions (users:read, users:write, journals:read, journals:write, resources:read, resources:write, courses:read, courses:write, checkins:read, checkins:write, reports:read, reports:write, reports:execute, audit:read, admin:config, admin:retention)
- INSERT IGNORE role_permissions mapping (admin gets all, librarian gets journals+resources, instructor gets courses+checkins, department_head gets reports+courses, viewer gets read-only)

002_seed_departments.sql:
- INSERT IGNORE 3 example departments: Computer Science (CS), Library Sciences (LS), Mathematics (MATH)

003_seed_users.sql:
- INSERT IGNORE one user per role with clear placeholder emails
- admin@scholarly.local, librarian@scholarly.local, instructor@scholarly.local, depthead@scholarly.local, viewer@scholarly.local
- password_hash should be a placeholder string with comment: "-- Password: 'changeme' - hash to be generated by application on first run"
- INSERT IGNORE corresponding user_roles entries

004_seed_config_defaults.sql:
- INSERT IGNORE a default retention policy for audit_logs (365 days, archive)
- INSERT IGNORE a default retention policy for sessions (30 days, delete)

All seed files should use INSERT IGNORE for idempotency. Use deterministic UUIDs (like 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001 pattern) for seed data so they're predictable.

Write all files."}

text.text:
All files have been created. Here is a summary:

**Docker files:**

- `/repo/backend/Dockerfile` -- Multi-stage build: `rust:1.77-bookworm` builder stage compiles the release binary, `debian:bookworm-slim` runtime stage installs `ca-certificates` and `default-mysql-client`, copies the binary plus migrations/seeds/scripts directories, exposes port 8000, and uses `scripts/entrypoint.sh` as the entrypoint.

- `/repo/frontend/Dockerfile` -- Multi-stage build: `rust:1.77-bookworm` builder stage installs `dioxus-cli` and runs `dx build --release`, `nginx:alpine` runtime stage serves the built `dist/` directory and uses a custom nginx config. Exposes port 80.

- `/repo/frontend/nginx.conf` -- Serves static files from `/usr/share/nginx/html` with SPA fallback (`try_files` to `index.html`), proxies `/api/` requests to `http://scholarly-backend:8000/api/`, and adds cache headers for static assets.

**Entrypoint script:**

- `/repo/backend/scripts/entrypoint.sh` -- Parses `DATABASE_URL` to extract MySQL connection parameters, waits up to 120 seconds for MySQL to accept connections, runs all migration `.sql` files in lexicographic order (tolerating failures for already-applied migrations), runs all seed `.sql` files in order, then `exec`s the backend binary.

**Seed files (all use INSERT IGNORE for idempotency with deterministic UUIDs):**

- `/repo/backend/seeds/001_seed_roles_and_permissions.sql` -- 5 roles (admin, librarian, instructor, department_head, viewer), 16 permissions covering users/journals/resources/courses/checkins/reports/audit/admin, and complete role-permission mappings (admin gets all 16, librarian gets journals+resources, instructor gets courses+checkins, department_head gets reports+courses, viewer gets all read-only permissions).

- `/repo/backend/seeds/002_seed_departments.sql` -- 3 departments: Computer Science (CS), Library Sciences (LS), Mathematics (MATH).

- `/repo/backend/seeds/003_seed_users.sql` -- 5 users (one per role) with `@scholarly.local` emails and placeholder password hashes, plus corresponding `user_roles` entries.

- `/repo/backend/seeds/004_seed_config_defaults.sql` -- Creates a `retention_policies` table (if not exists) and inserts two default policies: audit_logs (365 days, archive) and sessions (30 days, delete).

agentId: a499d0e52ca255330 (use SendMessage with to: 'a499d0e52ca255330' to continue this agent)
<usage>total_tokens: 23425
tool_uses: 24
duration_ms: 119554</usage>

All 10 migration files have been created at `/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/backend/migrations/`. Here is a summary:

**001_create_users_and_auth.sql** - 8 tables: `departments`, `users`, `roles`, `permissions`, `role_permissions`, `user_roles`, `sessions`, `failed_login_attempts`. Includes the deferred `ALTER TABLE users ADD CONSTRAINT fk_users_department` at the end since `departments` must exist before `users` can reference it, but `users` is defined after `departments`.

**002_create_journals.sql** - `journals` and `journal_versions` with circular FK for `current_version_id` resolved via ALTER TABLE.

**003_create_teaching_resources.sql** - `teaching_resources` and `resource_versions` with the same version-pointer pattern.

**004_create_courses_and_sections.sql** - `courses`, `course_versions`, `sections`, `section_versions`. Both `courses` and `sections` have deferred FKs for their `current_version_id` columns.

**005_create_attachments.sql** - Polymorphic `attachments` table with composite index on `(entity_type, entity_id)`.

**006_create_checkins.sql** - `checkin_events` with UNIQUE constraint on `(user_id, section_id, event_date)` for anti-duplicate protection.

**007_create_metrics_and_dashboards.sql** - `metric_definitions`, `metric_definition_versions`, `dashboard_definitions`, `dashboard_widgets`.

**008_create_reports.sql** - `reports`, `report_runs`, `report_schedules` with ENUM status tracking and cron scheduling support.

**009_create_audit_logs.sql** - `audit_logs` with indexes on actor, target entity, and timestamp. `audit_hash_chain` with `sequence_number BIGINT NOT NULL` (application-managed, not auto-increment) and UNIQUE constraints.

**010_create_retention_policies.sql** - `retention_policies` with UNIQUE on `target_entity_type` and ENUM action types.

All tables use `ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci`, CHAR(36) UUIDs for primary keys, proper foreign keys with ON DELETE/ON UPDATE actions, and relevant indexes.

agentId: aee6bb03eb162cabf (use SendMessage with to: 'aee6bb03eb162cabf' to continue this agent)
<usage>total_tokens: 22383
tool_uses: 13
duration_ms: 147087</usage>

All 35 files created. Here is a summary of what was built:

**Project configuration:**
- `Cargo.toml` -- dioxus 0.6 (web), dioxus-router 0.6, serde, serde_json, gloo-net 0.6, chrono 0.4
- `Dioxus.toml` -- app name "scholarly-frontend", title "Scholarly", watches `src/`, loads `assets/main.css`

**Styling:**
- `assets/main.css` -- CSS reset, design tokens (colors, spacing, radii, shadows, fonts), sidebar + main-content app shell layout, card/surface styles, stub-notice styling

**Application entry and routing:**

Write these 6 files with thorough, production-grade content:

---

**docs/architecture.md** — System architecture document covering:
- High-level overview: offline-only, 3-tier (Dioxus SPA → Rocket API → MySQL)
- Service topology: frontend (nginx, port 3000), backend (Rocket, port 8000), database (MySQL 8.0, port 3306)
- Docker Compose orchestration with health checks and dependency ordering
- Backend architecture: layered (api → application → domain ← infrastructure), clean architecture principles
- Frontend architecture: Dioxus SPA with client-side routing, role-aware navigation
- Data flow: request → auth guard → API handler → application service → repository → MySQL
- Storage: MySQL for structured data, local filesystem (Docker volume) for attachments
- Configuration: environment variables loaded at startup, no runtime config files on host
- Deployment: single `docker compose up` command, no external dependencies

---

**docs/domain_model.md** — Domain model documentation covering:
- Entity relationship diagram (text-based using ASCII/markdown tables)
- Core entities: User, Role, Permission, Department
- Content entities: Journal, TeachingResource, Course, Section — each with versioning pattern
- Versioning strategy: current_version_id pointer on parent, version history table with version_number, immutable version rows
- Operational entities: CheckinEvent, MetricDefinition, DashboardDefinition
- Administrative entities: AuditLog, AuditHashChain, RetentionPolicy, Report, ReportRun
- Attachment model: polymorphic (entity_type + entity_id), file stored on local filesystem
- Relationship map showing all foreign keys
- Department-scoped visibility: users belong to departments, data filtering by department

---

**docs/api_surface.md** — API reference covering:
- Base URL: /api/v1
- Authentication: JWT bearer token in Authorization header
- All route namespaces with HTTP methods and paths:
  - /auth (login, logout, refresh)
  - /users (CRUD)
  - /roles (CRUD)
  - /journals (CRUD + versions)
  - /teaching-resources (CRUD + versions)
  - /courses (CRUD + versions)
  - /sections (CRUD)
  - /attachments (get, upload, delete)
  - /checkins (list, create)
  - /metrics (CRUD + versions)
  - /dashboards (CRUD)
  - /reports (CRUD + run + runs)
  - /audit-logs (list, get, verify-chain)
  - /admin/config (get, update)
  - /admin/retention (list, create, update, execute)
  - /health (healthcheck)
- Standard response format: JSON with error envelope
- Pagination strategy: cursor-based or offset (TBD in phase 2)
- Status: ALL endpoints are Phase 1 stubs returning {"status": "not_implemented"}

---

**docs/requirement_traceability.md** — Maps each requirement to implementation:

Create a table format: | Requirement | Module | Status | Notes |

Cover requirements for:
- User management and RBAC → api::users, api::roles, domain::user, domain::role
- Department scoping → domain::user (department_id), infrastructure::repositories
- Journal management with versioning → api::journals, domain::journal
- Teaching resource management with versioning → api::teaching_resources, domain::teaching_resource
- Course and section management → api::courses, api::sections
- Attachment handling (local storage) → api::attachments, infrastructure::storage
- Check-in with anti-duplicate → api::checkins, domain::checkin (UNIQUE constraint)
- Metric definitions with versioning → api::metrics, domain::metric
- Dashboard configuration → api::dashboards, domain::dashboard
- Report generation and scheduling → api::reports, domain::report
- Audit logging (immutable, chained hash) → api::audit_logs, domain::audit, infrastructure::repositories::audit_repo
- Retention policies → api::retention, domain::retention
- Authentication (JWT + lockout) → api::auth, domain::auth, config (lockout settings)
- Data masking/encryption → TODO Phase 2 (document where it will live)
- Offline-only deployment → docker-compose.yml (no external service dependencies)
- Single-command startup → docker compose up

---

**docs/security_model.md** — Security architecture covering:
- Authentication: JWT-based with refresh tokens, stored as hashed values in sessions table
- Password storage: Argon2id hashing
- Account lockout: configurable max_failed_logins and lockout_duration_minutes, tracked in failed_login_attempts table
- Authorization: RBAC with 5 roles (Admin, Librarian, Instructor, DepartmentHead, Viewer)
- Role hierarchy and permission matrix (table format)
- Permission enforcement: Rocket request guards check JWT validity and role permissions
- Department-scoped data access: queries filtered by user's department_id
- Data masking: sensitive fields masked based on viewer's role (Phase 2 implementation, strategy defined here)
- Encryption at rest: attachment files stored unencrypted on local volume (offline deployment assumption); database-level encryption via MySQL TDE if required
- Audit trail: every state-changing operation logged to audit_logs, immutability via hash chain
- Hash chain strategy: SHA-256, each entry hashes (previous_hash + audit_log_id + action + timestamp), verified via /audit-logs/verify-chain endpoint
- Session management: configurable expiration, revocation support
- CORS: restricted to frontend origin only
- Input validation: at API boundary (Phase 2 implementation)
- SQL injection prevention: parameterized queries via sqlx
- No secrets in source code: all secrets via environment variables

---

**docs/phase_1_summary.md** — Phase 1 completion summary:
- What was created (itemized list of all deliverables)
- What remains intentionally unimplemented (grouped by category)
- Where Phase 2 should continue (ordered priority list)
- Risks and open decisions
- Static review checklist results

Write all 6 files with real, thorough content. No placeholder sections."}

All 6 documentation files have been created. Here is a summary of what was written:

**Files created in `/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/docs/`:**

1. **architecture.md** -- Covers the 3-tier architecture (Dioxus SPA -> Rocket API -> MySQL), service topology with ports, Docker Compose orchestration with health checks and dependency ordering, backend clean architecture layers (api -> application -> domain <- infrastructure), frontend Dioxus SPA with client-side routing and role-aware navigation, complete data flow diagram, storage strategy, all configuration environment variables, and single-command deployment.

2. **domain_model.md** -- Documents all 22+ database tables across 4 entity categories. Includes ASCII relationship diagram, full column-level schemas for every entity, the versioning pattern (current_version_id pointer + immutable version rows), the polymorphic attachment model, the foreign key relationship map (30+ FK relationships), and department-scoped visibility strategy.

3. **api_surface.md** -- Complete API reference for all 60 endpoints across 16 route namespaces. Each endpoint has method, path, handler name, description, and required permissions. Covers authentication flow (JWT bearer), standard error response format with AppError mapping, pagination strategy, and a summary table.

4. **requirement_traceability.md** -- Maps every requirement to specific module paths with a 5-level status system (Scaffolded, Schema Ready, Domain Modeled, Service Stub, Not Started). Organized into Identity/Access, Content, Operations, Security/Compliance, Infrastructure/Deployment, and Frontend categories.

5. **security_model.md** -- Covers JWT authentication flow (login/refresh/logout), Argon2id password storage, account lockout mechanism with configurable thresholds, RBAC with the full 5-role x 16-permission matrix, department-scoped data access, data masking strategy (Phase 2), encryption at rest options, audit trail with SHA-256 hash chain verification, session management, CORS policy, input validation plan, SQL injection prevention via sqlx, secrets management, and network security.

6. **phase_1_summary.md** -- Itemized list of every deliverable (backend: 60 routes, 13 domain modules, 11 services, 13 repos, 10 migrations, 4 seeds; frontend: 13 pages, 5 components, routing, API client; infrastructure: Docker Compose, Dockerfiles, entrypoint script). Lists all unimplemented items grouped by category, a 34-item prioritized Phase 2 plan, 8 risks with mitigations, 10 open design decisions, and a 20-item static review checklist with pass/fail results.

{"file_path": "/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/README.md", "content": "# Scholarly

Offline-first scholarly resources and teaching operations management system.

## Current Phase: Phase 1 — Architecture Foundation

This repository contains the **architectural skeleton** of the Scholarly system. All module boundaries, database schema, Docker automation, and requirement traceability are in place. **No business features are implemented yet** — all API endpoints return stub responses, and all frontend pages are placeholders.

## Stack

| Layer    | Technology          | Version |
|----------|---------------------|---------|
| Frontend | Dioxus (Web)        | 0.6     |
| Backend  | Rocket (Rust)       | 0.5     |
| Database | MySQL               | 8.0     |
| Auth     | JWT + Argon2id      | —       |
| Storage  | Local filesystem    | —       |
| Deploy   | Docker Compose      | 3.9     |

## Architecture

```
┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐
│   Frontend   │───▶│   Backend    │───▶│    MySQL     │
│  Dioxus SPA  │    │  Rocket API  │    │    8.0       │
│  nginx:3000  │    │  :8000       │    │  :3306       │
└──────────────┘    └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘
                           │
                    ┌──────┴──────┐
                    │ Attachments │
                    │ (local vol) │
                    └─────────────┘
```

Backend follows clean architecture: `api → application → domain ← infrastructure`

## Quick Start

```bash
docker compose up
```

That's it. The system:
1. Starts MySQL and waits for it to be healthy
2. Runs all database migrations automatically
3. Seeds default roles, permissions, departments, and users
4. Starts the Rocket API server on port 8000
5. Builds and serves the Dioxus frontend on port 3000

### Exposed Ports

| Service  | Port | Description               |
|----------|------|---------------------------|
| Frontend | 3000 | Dioxus SPA (via nginx)    |
| Backend  | 8000 | Rocket REST API           |
| MySQL    | 3306 | Database (direct access)  |

### Default Seed Users

| Role            | Email                        | Password   |
|-----------------|------------------------------|------------|
| Admin           | admin@scholarly.local        | changeme*  |
| Librarian       | librarian@scholarly.local    | changeme*  |
| Instructor      | instructor@scholarly.local   | changeme*  |
| Department Head | depthead@scholarly.local     | changeme*  |
| Viewer          | viewer@scholarly.local       | changeme*  |

*Password hashing will be implemented in Phase 2. Current seeds use placeholder hashes.

## Folder Structure

```
repo/
├── README.md
├── docker-compose.yml
├── .gitignore
├── .dockerignore
├── run_tests.sh
├── docs/
│   ├── architecture.md
│   ├── domain_model.md
│   ├── api_surface.md
│   ├── requirement_traceability.md
│   ├── security_model.md
│   └── phase_1_summary.md
├── backend/
│   ├── Cargo.toml
│   ├── Dockerfile
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── main.rs
│   │   ├── lib.rs
│   │   ├── api/          # Route handlers (16 modules)
│   │   ├── application/  # Use-case services (11 modules)
│   │   ├── domain/       # Business entities (13 modules)
│   │   ├── infrastructure/ # Repos, DB, storage
│   │   ├── config/       # Environment configuration
│   │   └── errors/       # Unified error types
│   ├── migrations/       # 10 SQL migration files
│   ├── seeds/            # 4 deterministic seed files
│   ├── scripts/          # entrypoint.sh (startup automation)
│   └── tests/            # Integration tests
├── frontend/
│   ├── Cargo.toml
│   ├── Dioxus.toml
│   ├── Dockerfile
│   ├── nginx.conf
│   ├── assets/
│   └── src/
│       ├── main.rs
│       ├── router.rs
│       ├── pages/        # 13 page components
│       ├── layouts/      # Main layout with role-aware nav
│       ├── components/   # Reusable UI components
│       ├── hooks/        # Auth and API hooks
│       ├── api/          # HTTP client
│       ├── state/        # Application state
│       ├── types/        # Shared types
│       └── routes/       # (reserved)
├── unit_tests/
└── API_tests/
```

## What Is Implemented (Phase 1)

- Full repository structure with clean module boundaries
- Docker Compose with MySQL, backend, and frontend services
- Automated startup: DB wait → migrations → seeds → server
- 10 SQL migration files defining 22+ tables
- 4 seed files with deterministic default data
- Backend: 60 API route stubs across 16 namespaces
- Backend: 13 domain models, 11 application services, 13 repositories (all stubs)
- Frontend: 13 page components, role-aware navigation, routing
- Unified error handling structure
- Configuration from environment variables
- Comprehensive documentation and requirement traceability

## What Is NOT Implemented Yet

- Authentication logic (login, JWT issuance, token refresh)
- Authorization guards (role/permission checking)
- Any business logic in API handlers
- Database queries in repositories
- File upload/download for attachments
- Check-in duplicate detection logic
- Audit log hash chain computation
- Report generation and scheduling execution
- Data masking/encryption
- Frontend API integration
- Frontend state management (beyond stubs)
- Input validation

## Running Tests

```bash
./run_tests.sh
```

This runs backend unit tests, integration tests, frontend tests, and API test scripts.

## Constraints and Assumptions

- **Offline-only**: No external network dependencies. All services run locally.
- **Single-command startup**: `docker compose up` is the only required command.
- **Local file storage**: Attachments stored on a Docker volume, not cloud storage.
- **Strict RBAC**: 5 roles with granular permissions. Department-scoped data access.
- **Audit immutability**: Append-only audit log with SHA-256 chained hashes.
- **Versioning**: Journals, resources, courses, sections, and metric definitions are versioned entities.
- **MySQL 8.0**: Chosen for JSON column support, window functions, and CTEs.

## Documentation

| Document | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [Architecture](docs/architecture.md) | System design and service topology |
| [Domain Model](docs/domain_model.md) | Entity relationships and versioning strategy |
| [API Surface](docs/api_surface.md) | All endpoints with methods and permissions |
| [Requirement Traceability](docs/requirement_traceability.md) | Requirement-to-module mapping |
| [Security Model](docs/security_model.md) | Auth, RBAC, audit, encryption strategy |
| [Phase 1 Summary](docs/phase_1_summary.md) | What was built and what's next |
"}

3. Seeds default roles, permissions, departments, and users
4. Starts the Rocket API server on port 8000
5. Builds and serves the Dioxus frontend on port 3000

### Exposed Ports

| Service  | Port | Description               |
|----------|------|---------------------------|
| Frontend | 3000 | Dioxus SPA (via nginx)    |
| Backend  | 8000 | Rocket REST API           |
| MySQL    | 3306 | Database (direct access)  |

### Default Seed Users

| Role            | Email                        | Password   |
|-----------------|------------------------------|------------|
| Admin           | admin@scholarly.local        | changeme*  |
| Librarian       | librarian@scholarly.local    | changeme*  |
| Instructor      | instructor@scholarly.local   | changeme*  |
| Department Head | depthead@scholarly.local     | changeme*  |
| Viewer          | viewer@scholarly.local       | changeme*  |

*Password hashing will be implemented in Phase 2. Current seeds use placeholder hashes.

## Folder Structure

```
repo/
├── README.md
├── docker-compose.yml
├── .gitignore
├── .dockerignore
├── run_tests.sh
├── docs/
│   ├── architecture.md
│   ├── domain_model.md
│   ├── api_surface.md
│   ├── requirement_traceability.md
│   ├── security_model.md
│   └── phase_1_summary.md
├── backend/
│   ├── Cargo.toml
│   ├── Dockerfile
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── main.rs
│   │   ├── lib.rs
│   │   ├── api/          # Route handlers (16 modules)
│   │   ├── application/  # Use-case services (11 modules)
│   │   ├── domain/       # Business entities (13 modules)
│   │   ├── infrastructure/ # Repos, DB, storage
│   │   ├── config/       # Environment configuration
│   │   └── errors/       # Unified error types
│   ├── migrations/       # 10 SQL migration files
│   ├── seeds/            # 4 deterministic seed files
│   ├── scripts/          # entrypoint.sh (startup automation)
│   └── tests/            # Integration tests
├── frontend/
│   ├── Cargo.toml
│   ├── Dioxus.toml
│   ├── Dockerfile
│   ├── nginx.conf
│   ├── assets/
│   └── src/
│       ├── main.rs
│       ├── router.rs
│       ├── pages/        # 13 page components
│       ├── layouts/      # Main layout with role-aware nav
│       ├── components/   # Reusable UI components
│       ├── hooks/        # Auth and API hooks
│       ├── api/          # HTTP client
│       ├── state/        # Application state
│       ├── types/        # Shared types
│       └── routes/       # (reserved)
├── unit_tests/
└── API_tests/
```

## What Is Implemented (Phase 1)

- Full repository structure with clean module boundaries
- Docker Compose with MySQL, backend, and frontend services
- Automated startup: DB wait → migrations → seeds → server
- 10 SQL migration files defining 22+ tables
- 4 seed files with deterministic default data
- Backend: 60 API route stubs across 16 namespaces
- Backend: 13 domain models, 11 application services, 13 repositories (all stubs)
- Frontend: 13 page components, role-aware navigation, routing
- Unified error handling structure
- Configuration from environment variables
- Comprehensive documentation and requirement traceability

## What Is NOT Implemented Yet

- Authentication logic (login, JWT issuance, token refresh)
- Authorization guards (role/permission checking)
- Any business logic in API handlers
- Database queries in repositories
- File upload/download for attachments
- Check-in duplicate detection logic
- Audit log hash chain computation
- Report generation and scheduling execution
- Data masking/encryption
- Frontend API integration
- Frontend state management (beyond stubs)
- Input validation

## Running Tests

```bash
./run_tests.sh
```

This runs backend unit tests, integration tests, frontend tests, and API test scripts.

## Constraints and Assumptions

- **Offline-only**: No external network dependencies. All services run locally.
- **Single-command startup**: `docker compose up` is the only required command.
- **Local file storage**: Attachments stored on a Docker volume, not cloud storage.
- **Strict RBAC**: 5 roles with granular permissions. Department-scoped data access.
- **Audit immutability**: Append-only audit log with SHA-256 chained hashes.
- **Versioning**: Journals, resources, courses, sections, and metric definitions are versioned entities.
- **MySQL 8.0**: Chosen for JSON column support, window functions, and CTEs.

## Documentation

| Document | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [Architecture](docs/architecture.md) | System design and service topology |
| [Domain Model](docs/domain_model.md) | Entity relationships and versioning strategy |
| [API Surface](docs/api_surface.md) | All endpoints with methods and permissions |
| [Requirement Traceability](docs/requirement_traceability.md) | Requirement-to-module mapping |
| [Security Model](docs/security_model.md) | Auth, RBAC, audit, encryption strategy |
| [Phase 1 Summary](docs/phase_1_summary.md) | What was built and what's next |
"}

content.text:
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	set -euo pipefail
3	
4	# ============================================================================
5	# Entrypoint for Scholarly Backend
6	# ============================================================================
7	# Waits for MySQL, runs migrations and seeds, then starts the API server.
8	# ============================================================================
9	
10	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
11	# Parse DATABASE_URL into components
12	# Format: mysql://user:password@host:port/database
13	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
14	parse_database_url() {
15	    local url="${DATABASE_URL:?DATABASE_URL environment variable is required}"
16	
17	    # Strip the protocol prefix
18	    local without_proto="${url#mysql://}"
19	
20	    # Extract user:password
21	    local userpass="${without_proto%%@*}"
22	    DB_USER="${userpass%%:*}"
23	    DB_PASS="${userpass#*:}"
24	
25	    # Extract host:port/database
26	    local hostportdb="${without_proto#*@}"
27	    local hostport="${hostportdb%%/*}"
28	    DB_HOST="${hostport%%:*}"
29	    DB_PORT="${hostport#*:}"
30	    DB_NAME="${hostportdb#*/}"
31	
32	    echo "Database config: host=${DB_HOST} port=${DB_PORT} user=${DB_USER} db=${DB_NAME}"
33	}
34	
35	parse_database_url
36	
37	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
38	# Wait for MySQL to be ready
39	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
40	echo "Waiting for MySQL at ${DB_HOST}:${DB_PORT}..."
41	
42	MAX_RETRIES=60
43	RETRY_COUNT=0
44	
45	until mysql -h "${DB_HOST}" -P "${DB_PORT}" -u "${DB_USER}" -p"${DB_PASS}" -e "SELECT 1" "${DB_NAME}" > /dev/null 2>&1; do
46	    RETRY_COUNT=$((RETRY_COUNT + 1))
47	    if [ "${RETRY_COUNT}" -ge "${MAX_RETRIES}" ]; then
48	        echo "ERROR: MySQL not available after ${MAX_RETRIES} attempts. Exiting."
49	        exit 1
50	    fi
51	    echo "  MySQL not ready yet (attempt ${RETRY_COUNT}/${MAX_RETRIES}). Retrying in 2s..."
52	    sleep 2
53	done
54	
55	echo "MySQL is ready."
56	
57	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
58	# Run migration SQL files in order
59	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
60	echo "Running migrations..."
61	
62	for migration in /app/migrations/*.sql; do
63	    if [ -f "${migration}" ]; then
64	        echo "  Applying migration: $(basename "${migration}")"
65	        mysql -h "${DB_HOST}" -P "${DB_PORT}" -u "${DB_USER}" -p"${DB_PASS}" "${DB_NAME}" < "${migration}" || {
66	            # Migrations use CREATE TABLE which will fail if already applied;
67	            # log and continue so the entrypoint is re-entrant.
68	            echo "  Warning: migration $(basename "${migration}") returned non-zero (may already be applied)."
69	        }
70	    fi
71	done
72	
73	echo "Migrations complete."
74	
75	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
76	# Run seed SQL files in order
77	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
78	echo "Running seeds..."
79	
80	for seed in /app/seeds/*.sql; do
81	    if [ -f "${seed}" ]; then
82	        echo "  Applying seed: $(basename "${seed}")"
83	        mysql -h "${DB_HOST}" -P "${DB_PORT}" -u "${DB_USER}" -p"${DB_PASS}" "${DB_NAME}" < "${seed}" || {
84	            echo "  Warning: seed $(basename "${seed}") returned non-zero."
85	        }
86	    fi
87	done
88	
89	echo "Seeds complete."
90	
91	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
92	# Start the backend server
93	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
94	echo "Starting Scholarly backend..."
95	exec /app/scholarly-backend
96

1	-- ============================================================================
2	-- Migration 001: Users, Authentication, and Authorization
3	-- ============================================================================
4	-- Creates the foundational tables for user management, role-based access
5	-- control, session tracking, failed login monitoring, and departments.
6	-- ============================================================================
7	
8	CREATE TABLE departments (
9	    id              CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
10	    name            VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
11	    code            VARCHAR(50)  NOT NULL,
12	    parent_department_id CHAR(36) DEFAULT NULL,
13	    created_at      DATETIME     NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
14	    updated_at      DATETIME     NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
15	    PRIMARY KEY (id),
16	    UNIQUE KEY uq_departments_code (code),
17	    CONSTRAINT fk_departments_parent FOREIGN KEY (parent_department_id) REFERENCES departments (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE,
18	    INDEX idx_departments_parent (parent_department_id)
19	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
20	
21	CREATE TABLE users (
22	    id              CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
23	    email           VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
24	    display_name    VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
25	    password_hash   VARCHAR(512) NOT NULL,
26	    status          ENUM('pending_verification','active','suspended','deactivated') NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active',
27	    phone           VARCHAR(50)  DEFAULT NULL,
28	    avatar_url      TEXT         DEFAULT NULL,
29	    department_id   CHAR(36)     DEFAULT NULL,
30	    created_at      DATETIME     NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
31	    updated_at      DATETIME     NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
32	    PRIMARY KEY (id),
33	    UNIQUE KEY uq_users_email (email),
34	    INDEX idx_users_status (status),
35	    INDEX idx_users_department (department_id)
36	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
37	
38	CREATE TABLE roles (
39	    id              CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
40	    name            VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
41	    display_name    VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
42	    description     TEXT         DEFAULT NULL,
43	    created_at      DATETIME     NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
44	    PRIMARY KEY (id),
45	    UNIQUE KEY uq_roles_name (name)
46	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
47	
48	CREATE TABLE permissions (
49	    id              CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
50	    key_name        VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
51	    description     TEXT         DEFAULT NULL,
52	    created_at      DATETIME     NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
53	    PRIMARY KEY (id),
54	    UNIQUE KEY uq_permissions_key_name (key_name)
55	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
56	
57	CREATE TABLE role_permissions (
58	    role_id         CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
59	    permission_id   CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
60	    PRIMARY KEY (role_id, permission_id),
61	    CONSTRAINT fk_role_permissions_role FOREIGN KEY (role_id) REFERENCES roles (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
62	    CONSTRAINT fk_role_permissions_permission FOREIGN KEY (permission_id) REFERENCES permissions (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE
63	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
64	
65	CREATE TABLE user_roles (
66	    id              CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
67	    user_id         CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
68	    role_id         CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
69	    assigned_by     CHAR(36)     DEFAULT NULL,
70	    assigned_at     DATETIME     NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
71	    PRIMARY KEY (id),
72	    UNIQUE KEY uq_user_roles_user_role (user_id, role_id),
73	    CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_user FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
74	    CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_role FOREIGN KEY (role_id) REFERENCES roles (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
75	    CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_assigned_by FOREIGN KEY (assigned_by) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE
76	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
77	
78	CREATE TABLE sessions (
79	    id                 CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
80	    user_id            CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
81	    refresh_token_hash VARCHAR(512) NOT NULL,
82	    ip_address         VARCHAR(45)  DEFAULT NULL,
83	    user_agent         TEXT         DEFAULT NULL,
84	    expires_at         DATETIME     NOT NULL,
85	    revoked_at         DATETIME     DEFAULT NULL,
86	    created_at         DATETIME     NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
87	    PRIMARY KEY (id),
88	    CONSTRAINT fk_sessions_user FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
89	    INDEX idx_sessions_user (user_id),
90	    INDEX idx_sessions_expires (expires_at)
91	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
92	
93	CREATE TABLE failed_login_attempts (
94	    id              CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
95	    email           VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
96	    ip_address      VARCHAR(45)  DEFAULT NULL,
97	    attempted_at    DATETIME     NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
98	    reason          VARCHAR(255) DEFAULT NULL,
99	    PRIMARY KEY (id),
100	    INDEX idx_failed_logins_email (email),
101	    INDEX idx_failed_logins_ip (ip_address),
102	    INDEX idx_failed_logins_attempted (attempted_at)
103	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
104	
105	-- Add the deferred foreign key from users to departments
106	ALTER TABLE users
107	    ADD CONSTRAINT fk_users_department FOREIGN KEY (department_id) REFERENCES departments (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE;
108

1	//! Scholarly Frontend — Route Definitions
2	//!
3	//! Declares every navigable route in the application using a `Routable`
4	//! enum. Dioxus-router derives the mapping from URL path to component
5	//! automatically.
6	
7	use dioxus::prelude::*;
8	use dioxus_router::prelude::*;
9	
10	use crate::layouts::main_layout::MainLayout;
11	use crate::pages::{
12	    admin_settings::AdminSettings,
13	    audit_logs::AuditLogs,
14	    checkin::CheckIn,
15	    course_detail::CourseDetail,
16	    course_list::CourseList,
17	    dashboard::Dashboard,
18	    import_export::ImportExport,
19	    journal_list::JournalList,
20	    login::Login,
21	    reports::Reports,
22	    resource_list::ResourceList,
23	    retention_settings::RetentionSettings,
24	    section_list::SectionList,
25	};
26	
27	/// Top-level route enum for the Scholarly application.
28	///
29	/// Each variant maps a URL pattern to a page component. Routes nested
30	/// under `#[layout(MainLayout)]` share the sidebar/header chrome.
31	#[derive(Clone, Routable, Debug, PartialEq)]
32	pub enum AppRoute {
33	    /// Public login page — rendered without the main layout chrome.
34	    #[route("/login")]
35	    Login {},
36	
37	    /// All authenticated routes share the MainLayout wrapper.
38	    #[layout(MainLayout)]
39	        /// Dashboard / home page.
40	        #[route("/")]
41	        Dashboard {},
42	
43	        /// Journal catalogue listing.
44	        #[route("/library/journals")]
45	        JournalList {},
46	
47	        /// General resource listing.
48	        #[route("/library/resources")]
49	        ResourceList {},
50	
51	        /// Course listing.
52	        #[route("/courses")]
53	        CourseList {},
54	
55	        /// Detail view for a single course.
56	        #[route("/courses/:id")]
57	        CourseDetail { id: String },
58	
59	        /// Sections within a course.
60	        #[route("/courses/:id/sections")]
61	        SectionList { id: String },
62	
63	        /// Bulk import / export page.
64	        #[route("/imports")]
65	        ImportExport {},
66	
67	        /// Resource check-in page.
68	        #[route("/checkin")]
69	        CheckIn {},
70	
71	        /// Reports and analytics.
72	        #[route("/reports")]
73	        Reports {},
74	
75	        /// Audit log viewer.
76	        #[route("/audit")]
77	        AuditLogs {},
78	
79	        /// System-wide admin settings.
80	        #[route("/admin/settings")]
81	        AdminSettings {},
82	
83	        /// Retention policy settings.
84	        #[route("/admin/retention")]
85	        RetentionSettings {},
86	    // end layout
87	}
88

Phase 1 is complete. Here's what was delivered:

## Phase 1 Deliverables Summary

**133 files** across the full repository:

### Root Configuration
- `docker-compose.yml` — MySQL + Backend + Frontend with health checks, volumes, ports
- `.gitignore`, `.dockerignore`, `run_tests.sh`, `README.md`

### Backend (64 source files)
- **16 API route modules** — 60 stub endpoints across auth, users, roles, journals, teaching-resources, courses, sections, attachments, checkins, metrics, dashboards, reports, audit-logs, admin/config, admin/retention, health
- **13 domain models** — User, Role, Auth, Journal, TeachingResource, Course, Section, Checkin, Metric, Dashboard, Report, Audit, Retention (all with versioning where needed)
- **11 application services** — orchestration layer stubs
- **13 repositories** + database pool + local file storage stubs
- **Config** with env-var loading and **unified error types**

### Frontend (31 source files)
- **13 page components** with Dioxus `rsx!` blocks
- **Role-aware navigation** — 5 roles (Admin, Librarian, Instructor, DepartmentHead, Viewer) with hierarchical filtering
- **Declarative routing** via `dioxus-router` with layout nesting
- Components, hooks, API client, state management stubs

### Database (10 migrations, 4 seeds)
- **22+ tables** with proper FKs, indexes, constraints, utf8mb4
- Anti-duplicate UNIQUE on checkin_events, hash chain for audit immutability
- Deterministic seeds: 5 roles, 16 permissions, 3 departments, 5 users

### Docker Automation
- Multi-stage Dockerfiles for backend (Rust build) and frontend (Dioxus + nginx)
- `entrypoint.sh` — waits for DB, runs migrations, seeds, starts server
- nginx reverse proxy with SPA fallback

### Documentation (6 files)
- `architecture.md`, `domain_model.md`, `api_surface.md`, `requirement_traceability.md`, `security_model.md`, `phase_1_summary.md`

### Testing
- Backend integration tests, unit test harness, API test scripts, `run_tests.sh`

**Startup command: `docker compose up`** — no other setup required.

Authentication, session management, RBAC, admin controls, and audit logging spine

Goal of This Phase

Implement the security-critical backbone: offline authentication, password policy, lockout, sessions, explicit authorization boundaries, and immutable audit logging foundations. This phase exists because all later domain work depends on trustworthy identity, scope, and audit behavior.

Scope Boundaries

Do in this phase:

real auth endpoints and session handling
password hashing and minimum password policy
lockout policy
role and department-scope authorization framework
route-level and object-level authorization helpers
audit logging infrastructure and hash chaining logic
admin configuration primitives for local-network and retention defaults

Do not do yet:

no full library/course/check-in/report feature implementations
no placeholder auth
no UI beyond auth shell and role-aware layout if needed
no partial masking logic hidden in comments only

Preserve:

offline-only auth
five failed attempts => 15 minute lockout
at least 12 character passwords
immutable application-layer audit behavior

Instructions:

Implement the authentication and authorization backbone for the system. This phase must establish real security logic, not stubs.

Core requirements to satisfy now:
- offline username/password authentication
- salted password hashing
- minimum 12-character passwords
- lockout after 5 failed attempts for 15 minutes
- server-side session management
- role-based access control
- department/object-scope enforcement foundation
- immutable audit logging with tamper detection using chained hashes
- searchable audit log API foundations
- protection of admin/internal/debug routes

Implement the following:

A. Backend auth and session model
Create real backend implementations for:
- login
- logout
- current session/me
- password change/reset path as appropriate for seeded users/admin-managed flows
- lockout enforcement
- session expiration/revocation policy
- auth middleware/guards for Rocket

Use a strong password hasher suitable for offline auth in Rust, preferably Argon2id with per-password salt.

Do not store plaintext passwords anywhere. Seed only hashed passwords.

B. Authorization model
Implement explicit authorization layers:
1. route-level authorization
2. function-level authorization for sensitive service operations
3. object-level authorization helpers for department scope / role scope
4. admin-only protections
5. auditor read-only protections where applicable

Create a clearly reviewable role matrix in code and docs showing which roles can access:
- journal/resource functions
- course/section functions
- import/export
- check-in
- dashboards
- reports
- audit log search/export
- admin configuration

C. Department scope and masking foundation
Implement scope primitives needed later:
- users associated to allowed department scopes where relevant
- helper utilities for scoped filtering
- response masking utilities for sensitive instructor notes and student identifiers
- explicit policy code, not ad hoc filtering in route handlers

At this phase, masking may be partial but the framework must be real and already used where relevant in auth/audit endpoints.

D. Audit logging implementation
Implement immutable application-layer audit logging with chained hashes:
- record auth events
- permission changes
- admin config changes
- failed logins and lockouts
- major security-relevant accesses

Requirements:
- append-only write path from application services
- each log entry includes previous hash reference and current hash
- searchable by user, object, action, and time range
- exportable to CSV endpoint may be scaffolded if not fully completed, but search/read must be real now
- sensitive fields must be masked in responses unless explicitly authorized

E. Admin configuration primitives
Implement admin-config storage and APIs for settings that later features depend on:
- approved SSID names/local-network rule config
- check-in duplicate window default (10 minutes)
- retention policy defaults (7 years audit, 3 years operational events)
- attachment upload constraints
- report schedule definitions scaffold

These endpoints must be admin-protected.

F. Frontend auth shell
Implement Dioxus pages/components for:
- login page
- authenticated app shell
- role-aware navigation visibility
- unauthorized/forbidden states
- session-expired handling

Keep the UI production-grade and minimal but polished. No generic ugly placeholder.

G. Standardized API error schema
Implement a consistent error response contract across backend APIs:
- machine-readable code
- human-readable message
- optional field errors
- request correlation id if appropriate

Use this already in auth and admin endpoints.

H. Database work
Complete the migrations and seeds necessary for:
- users
- roles
- user_roles
- sessions
- departments/scopes
- lockout tracking
- admin settings/config
- audit logs and hash chain support

I. Tests
Add substantial tests now in both required suites:
- unit_tests/
- API_tests/

Must include:
- successful login
- password minimum length rejection
- failed login attempt tracking
- lockout after 5 failures
- unlock after configured duration logic
- unauthenticated access => 401
- unauthorized role => 403
- object/department scope denial where relevant
- audit log entry creation on login/logout/failure/config change
- audit chain integrity logic
- admin endpoint protection

J. Documentation updates
Update:
- README
- docs/security_model.md
- docs/api_surface.md
- docs/requirement_traceability.md

Make the docs exact. Do not claim future protections already exist unless they are implemented in code.

Anti-hallucination constraints:
- do not fake sessions in frontend memory only
- do not put authorization only in the UI
- do not log secrets, passwords, or raw sensitive payloads
- do not leave admin endpoints unguarded
- do not use giant route files with mixed business logic
- do not silently weaken object-level authorization by saying it will be added later; the framework must be active now

At the end, create docs/phase_2_summary.md with implemented security capabilities, routes, tests, and known remaining gaps for later phases.


Deliverables Expected at End of Phase

real auth/session endpoints
password hashing implementation
lockout implementation
RBAC/scoping utilities
admin config endpoints
audit logging with chained hashes
frontend login + protected shell
security-focused migrations and seeds
unit/API tests for security flows
updated README and docs
docs/phase_2_summary.md

Static Review Checklist for This Phase

Are auth entry points real and documented?
Are password hashing and lockout visible in code?
Are route/object/function authorization layers explicit?
Are admin endpoints protected?
Is audit chain logic implemented and tested?
Are 401/403 paths covered?
Do docs reflect actual implemented security boundaries?

Stop Condition / Hand-off Rule

Stop when the security backbone is fully real, test-covered, and statically obvious, before implementing domain workflows.
I'll implement the security backbone for Phase 2. This requires deep coordination between auth, RBAC, audit, and frontend layers. Let me start by tracking tasks and examining the current state.

1	pub mod api;
2	pub mod application;
3	pub mod config;
4	pub mod domain;
5	pub mod errors;
6	pub mod infrastructure;
7	
8	use rocket::Build;
9	
10	/// Assembles the full Rocket instance with all route mounts and managed state.
11	///
12	/// This is the single entry point used by both `main` and integration tests.
13	pub async fn build_rocket() -> rocket::Rocket<Build> {
14	    let app_config = config::AppConfig::from_env();
15	
16	    // TODO: Initialize database connection pool (sqlx::MySqlPool)
17	    // TODO: Initialize attachment storage service
18	    // TODO: Mount fairings (CORS, request guards, etc.)
19	
20	    rocket::build()
21	        .manage(app_config)
22	        // ── Auth ──
23	        .mount("/api/v1/auth", api::auth::routes())
24	        // ── Users & RBAC ──
25	        .mount("/api/v1/users", api::users::routes())
26	        .mount("/api/v1/roles", api::roles::routes())
27	        // ── Core domains ──
28	        .mount("/api/v1/journals", api::journals::routes())
29	        .mount("/api/v1/teaching-resources", api::teaching_resources::routes())
30	        .mount("/api/v1/courses", api::courses::routes())
31	        .mount("/api/v1/sections", api::sections::routes())
32	        .mount("/api/v1/attachments", api::attachments::routes())
33	        // ── Operations ──
34	        .mount("/api/v1/checkins", api::checkins::routes())
35	        .mount("/api/v1/metrics", api::metrics::routes())
36	        .mount("/api/v1/dashboards", api::dashboards::routes())
37	        .mount("/api/v1/reports", api::reports::routes())
38	        // ── Administration ──
39	        .mount("/api/v1/audit-logs", api::audit_logs::routes())
40	        .mount("/api/v1/admin/config", api::admin_config::routes())
41	        .mount("/api/v1/admin/retention", api::retention::routes())
42	        // ── Health ──
43	        .mount("/api/v1", api::health::routes())
44	}
45	
46	#[cfg(test)]
47	mod tests {
48	    use super::*;
49	
50	    #[tokio::test]
51	    async fn test_rocket_builds() {
52	        // Verifies that all routes mount without panic
53	        let _rocket = build_rocket().await;
54	    }
55	}

1	-- ============================================================================
2	-- Migration 009: Audit Logs and Audit Hash Chain
3	-- ============================================================================
4	-- Creates the audit logging infrastructure with a tamper-evident hash chain
5	-- for integrity verification. The actor_id is nullable to support system-
6	-- generated events.
7	-- ============================================================================
8	
9	CREATE TABLE audit_logs (
10	    id                  CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
11	    actor_id            CHAR(36)     DEFAULT NULL,
12	    actor_email         VARCHAR(255) DEFAULT NULL,
13	    action              VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
14	    target_entity_type  VARCHAR(100) DEFAULT NULL,
15	    target_entity_id    CHAR(36)     DEFAULT NULL,
16	    change_payload      JSON         DEFAULT NULL,
17	    ip_address          VARCHAR(45)  DEFAULT NULL,
18	    user_agent          TEXT         DEFAULT NULL,
19	    created_at          DATETIME     NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
20	    PRIMARY KEY (id),
21	    INDEX idx_audit_logs_actor (actor_id),
22	    INDEX idx_audit_logs_target (target_entity_type, target_entity_id),
23	    INDEX idx_audit_logs_created_at (created_at),
24	    INDEX idx_audit_logs_action (action)
25	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
26	
27	CREATE TABLE audit_hash_chain (
28	    id              CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
29	    audit_log_id    CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
30	    sequence_number BIGINT       NOT NULL,
31	    previous_hash   VARCHAR(128) DEFAULT NULL,
32	    current_hash    VARCHAR(128) NOT NULL,
33	    created_at      DATETIME     NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
34	    PRIMARY KEY (id),
35	    UNIQUE KEY uq_audit_hash_chain_log (audit_log_id),
36	    UNIQUE KEY uq_audit_hash_chain_sequence (sequence_number),
37	    CONSTRAINT fk_audit_hash_chain_log FOREIGN KEY (audit_log_id) REFERENCES audit_logs (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
38	    INDEX idx_audit_hash_chain_sequence (sequence_number)
39	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
40

{"file_path": "/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/backend/migrations/011_create_admin_settings.sql", "content": "-- ============================================================================
-- Migration 011: Admin Settings (key-value store)
-- ============================================================================
-- Stores admin-managed configuration: approved SSIDs for check-in, the
-- check-in duplicate window, retention defaults, attachment upload constraints,
-- and report schedule scaffolding values. All values are JSON-encoded so they
-- can carry scalars, objects, or arrays without per-setting table churn.
-- Every mutation is routed through /api/v1/admin/config and written to the
-- audit log with a chained hash.
-- ============================================================================

CREATE TABLE admin_settings (
    setting_key     VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    setting_value   JSON         NOT NULL,
    description     TEXT         DEFAULT NULL,
    updated_by      CHAR(36)     DEFAULT NULL,
    updated_at      DATETIME     NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    created_at      DATETIME     NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    PRIMARY KEY (setting_key),
    CONSTRAINT fk_admin_settings_updated_by FOREIGN KEY (updated_by) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
"}
