{"file_path": "/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/backend/seeds/005_seed_admin_settings.sql", "content": "-- ============================================================================
-- Seed 005: Admin Configuration Defaults
-- ============================================================================
-- Seeds the admin_settings key-value table with the defaults that later
-- domain features depend on. Values are JSON-encoded. Every row is
-- idempotent via INSERT IGNORE so re-running the entrypoint is safe.
-- ============================================================================

-- Approved SSID names for the "check-in via local wifi" policy.
-- Empty by default; an admin must set this before check-in is permitted.
INSERT IGNORE INTO admin_settings (setting_key, setting_value, description) VALUES
    ('network.approved_ssids',
     JSON_ARRAY(),
     'List of approved Wi-Fi SSID names that count as "on-campus" for check-in purposes.');

-- Check-in duplicate suppression window (in minutes).
-- Default 10 minutes as required.
INSERT IGNORE INTO admin_settings (setting_key, setting_value, description) VALUES
    ('checkin.duplicate_window_minutes',
     CAST('10' AS JSON),
     'A check-in from the same user for the same section within this many minutes is considered a duplicate and rejected.');

-- Retention default for audit log rows (7 years, expressed in days).
INSERT IGNORE INTO admin_settings (setting_key, setting_value, description) VALUES
    ('retention.audit_log_days',
     CAST('2555' AS JSON),
     'Default retention period (days) for audit log entries. Default 7 years.');

-- Retention default for operational events (3 years, expressed in days).
INSERT IGNORE INTO admin_settings (setting_key, setting_value, description) VALUES
    ('retention.operational_events_days',
     CAST('1095' AS JSON),
     'Default retention period (days) for non-audit operational events. Default 3 years.');

-- Attachment upload constraints.
INSERT IGNORE INTO admin_settings (setting_key, setting_value, description) VALUES
    ('attachments.max_bytes',
     CAST('52428800' AS JSON),
     'Maximum attachment size in bytes (default 50 MiB).'),
    ('attachments.allowed_mime_types',
     JSON_ARRAY(
        'application/pdf',
        'image/png',
        'image/jpeg',
        'text/plain',
        'text/csv',
        'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document',
        'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet',
        'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.presentationml.presentation'
     ),
     'Whitelist of MIME types accepted by the attachment upload endpoint.');

-- Report schedule scaffold: list of named schedules. Empty until Phase 3.
INSERT IGNORE INTO admin_settings (setting_key, setting_value, description) VALUES
    ('reports.schedules',
     JSON_ARRAY(),
     'Scheduled report definitions. Populated by the reports module in Phase 3.');

-- Lockout policy defaults (5 failed logins in 15 minutes).
-- These values are authoritative defaults; AppConfig environment variables
-- can override them at process start.
INSERT IGNORE INTO admin_settings (setting_key, setting_value, description) VALUES
    ('auth.max_failed_logins',
     CAST('5' AS JSON),
     'Maximum failed login attempts before an account is locked.'),
    ('auth.lockout_minutes',
     CAST('15' AS JSON),
     'Duration (minutes) an account remains locked after exceeding the failed-login threshold.'),
    ('auth.min_password_length',
     CAST('12' AS JSON),
     'Minimum password length enforced at set/change time.');
"}

1	//! Application state definitions for the Scholarly frontend.
2	//!
3	//! These structs are intended to be provided as Dioxus context values
4	//! at the app root so that any component can access shared state.
5	
6	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
7	use crate::types::{Role, User};
8	
9	/// Authentication state — tracks the currently logged-in user and their
10	/// bearer token.
11	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Default, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
12	pub struct AuthState {
13	    /// The authenticated user, if any.
14	    pub user: Option<User>,
15	    /// The user's active role.
16	    pub role: Option<Role>,
17	    /// JWT or opaque bearer token for API requests.
18	    pub token: Option<String>,
19	}
20	
21	impl AuthState {
22	    /// Returns `true` if a user is currently authenticated.
23	    pub fn is_authenticated(&self) -> bool {
24	        self.token.is_some()
25	    }
26	}
27	
28	/// Global application state — holds non-auth concerns shared across the
29	/// app (e.g., theme preference, feature flags).
30	///
31	/// TODO: Expand as new cross-cutting state is needed.
32	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Default, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
33	pub struct AppState {
34	    /// Whether the sidebar is collapsed.
35	    pub sidebar_collapsed: bool,
36	    // TODO: Add theme, locale, feature flags, etc.
37	}
38

1	//! Main application layout — sidebar navigation + content area.
2	//!
3	//! The layout is role-aware: navigation items declare the minimum role
4	//! required to see them, and the sidebar filters items accordingly.
5	
6	use dioxus::prelude::*;
7	use dioxus_router::prelude::*;
8	
9	use crate::router::AppRoute;
10	use crate::types::Role;
11	
12	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
13	// Navigation definition
14	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
15	
16	/// A single item in the sidebar navigation.
17	#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
18	pub struct NavItem {
19	    /// Display label shown in the sidebar.
20	    pub label: &'static str,
21	    /// Route this item navigates to.
22	    pub route: AppRoute,
23	    /// Minimum role required to see this item. `None` means visible to all
24	    /// authenticated users.
25	    pub required_role: Option<Role>,
26	    /// Optional section heading rendered above this item when it is the
27	    /// first entry in a logical group.
28	    pub section: Option<&'static str>,
29	}
30	
31	/// Returns the full ordered list of navigation items for the sidebar.
32	///
33	/// Items are grouped into logical sections via the `section` field.
34	pub fn navigation_items() -> Vec<NavItem> {
35	    vec![
36	        NavItem {
37	            label: "Dashboard",
38	            route: AppRoute::Dashboard {},
39	            required_role: None,
40	            section: None,
41	        },
42	        // -- Library --
43	        NavItem {
44	            label: "Journals",
45	            route: AppRoute::JournalList {},
46	            required_role: None,
47	            section: Some("Library"),
48	        },
49	        NavItem {
50	            label: "Resources",
51	            route: AppRoute::ResourceList {},
52	            required_role: None,
53	            section: None,
54	        },
55	        // -- Courses --
56	        NavItem {
57	            label: "Courses",
58	            route: AppRoute::CourseList {},
59	            required_role: None,
60	            section: Some("Courses"),
61	        },
62	        // -- Operations --
63	        NavItem {
64	            label: "Import / Export",
65	            route: AppRoute::ImportExport {},
66	            required_role: Some(Role::Librarian),
67	            section: Some("Operations"),
68	        },
69	        NavItem {
70	            label: "Check-In",
71	            route: AppRoute::CheckIn {},
72	            required_role: Some(Role::Librarian),
73	            section: None,
74	        },
75	        // -- Analytics --
76	        NavItem {
77	            label: "Reports",
78	            route: AppRoute::Reports {},
79	            required_role: Some(Role::DepartmentHead),
80	            section: Some("Analytics"),
81	        },
82	        NavItem {
83	            label: "Audit Logs",
84	            route: AppRoute::AuditLogs {},
85	            required_role: Some(Role::Admin),
86	            section: None,
87	        },
88	        // -- Admin --
89	        NavItem {
90	            label: "Settings",
91	            route: AppRoute::AdminSettings {},
92	            required_role: Some(Role::Admin),
93	            section: Some("Admin"),
94	        },
95	        NavItem {
96	            label: "Retention",
97	            route: AppRoute::RetentionSettings {},
98	            required_role: Some(Role::Admin),
99	            section: None,
100	        },
101	    ]
102	}
103	
104	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
105	// Helper — role visibility check
106	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
107	
108	/// Returns `true` if `user_role` satisfies the `required` role constraint.
109	///
110	/// The hierarchy (highest to lowest): Admin > Librarian > DepartmentHead >
111	/// Instructor > Viewer.
112	fn role_has_access(user_role: &Role, required: &Option<Role>) -> bool {
113	    match required {
114	        None => true,
115	        Some(req) => user_role.level() >= req.level(),
116	    }
117	}
118	
119	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
120	// Layout component
121	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
122	
123	/// Main layout component rendered around all authenticated routes.
124	///
125	/// Provides the sidebar navigation and a content area that is filled by
126	/// the active child route via `Outlet`.
127	#[component]
128	pub fn MainLayout() -> Element {
129	    // TODO: Read actual user role from AuthState context
130	    let current_role = Role::Admin;
131	
132	    let nav_items = navigation_items();
133	
134	    rsx! {
135	        div { class: "app-shell",
136	            // ── Sidebar ──────────────────────────────────────
137	            nav { class: "sidebar",
138	                div { class: "sidebar__brand", "Scholarly" }
139	
140	                div { class: "sidebar__nav",
141	                    for item in nav_items.iter() {
142	                        if role_has_access(&current_role, &item.required_role) {
143	                            // Render optional section heading
144	                            if let Some(section) = item.section {
145	                                div { class: "sidebar__section-title", "{section}" }
146	                            }
147	
148	                            Link {
149	                                class: "sidebar__link",
150	                                to: item.route.clone(),
151	                                "{item.label}"
152	                            }
153	                        }
154	                    }
155	                }
156	            }
157	
158	            // ── Main content area ────────────────────────────
159	            main { class: "main-content",
160	                Outlet::<AppRoute> {}
161	            }
162	        }
163	    }
164	}
165

1	//! HTTP client configuration for the Scholarly backend API.
2	//!
3	//! The backend is expected to run at `http://localhost:8000` during
4	//! development.
5	
6	/// Base URL for the backend API.
7	const API_BASE_URL: &str = "http://localhost:8000";
8	
9	/// Lightweight wrapper around `gloo-net` providing convenience methods
10	/// for authenticated requests to the Scholarly backend.
11	#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
12	pub struct ApiClient {
13	    /// Base URL (without trailing slash).
14	    pub base_url: String,
15	    /// Optional bearer token attached to every request.
16	    pub token: Option<String>,
17	}
18	
19	impl ApiClient {
20	    /// Creates a new `ApiClient` targeting the default backend URL.
21	    pub fn new(token: Option<String>) -> Self {
22	        Self {
23	            base_url: API_BASE_URL.to_string(),
24	            token,
25	        }
26	    }
27	
28	    /// Returns the full URL for a given API path.
29	    ///
30	    /// ```ignore
31	    /// let url = client.url("/api/journals");
32	    /// // => "http://localhost:8000/api/journals"
33	    /// ```
34	    pub fn url(&self, path: &str) -> String {
35	        format!("{}{}", self.base_url, path)
36	    }
37	
38	    // TODO: Add typed GET / POST / PUT / DELETE helpers using gloo_net::http::Request
39	    // that automatically attach the Authorization header when `self.token` is set.
40	}
41

{"description": "Build frontend auth shell", "prompt": "Implement the frontend auth shell for the Scholarly project. The backend already exposes these real endpoints:

- POST /api/v1/auth/login  — JSON body {"email","password"} — returns {"token","expires_at","user":{"id","email","display_name","roles":["admin"|...],"department_id"}}
- POST /api/v1/auth/logout — Bearer token required
- GET  /api/v1/auth/me     — Bearer token required, returns user

Error responses follow the envelope:
{"error": {"code":"unauthorized"|"forbidden"|"validation"|"account_locked"|...,"message":"...","request_id":"..."}}

Frontend stack: Dioxus 0.6 with dioxus-router 0.6 (the router uses `#[derive(Routable)]` and nested layouts). An nginx reverse-proxy already forwards `/api/*` to the backend on port 8000, so the frontend should call `/api/v1/...` as same-origin — no CORS concerns.

Repo root: /home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/frontend/

Work to do (write real code, not TODO stubs):

1) **Cargo.toml** — add dependencies:
- `wasm-bindgen = "0.2"`
- `web-sys = { version = "0.3", features = ["Window","Storage"] }`
- `futures = "0.3"`
Keep existing dioxus 0.6 / dioxus-router 0.6 / gloo-net / serde / chrono.

2) **src/types/mod.rs** — ADD these:
- Role::from_str(&str) -> Option<Role>  (parses "admin","librarian","department_head","instructor","viewer")
- Role::as_snake(&self) -> &'static str
- User should carry: id (String), email (String), display_name (String), roles: Vec<Role>, department_id: Option<String>
  (Current User has `name` and single `role` — REPLACE with the new shape. Update any references.)

3) **src/state/mod.rs** — expand AuthState:
- fields: user: Option<User>, token: Option<String>, expires_at: Option<String> (RFC3339 string)
- impls:
  - is_authenticated(&self) -> bool  (true if token.is_some() AND user.is_some())
  - primary_role(&self) -> Option<Role>  (highest-level role from user.roles)
  - load_from_storage() -> AuthState  (reads "scholarly_auth" from localStorage, returns default on any error)
  - save_to_storage(&self)  (writes JSON to localStorage, swallows errors)
  - clear_storage()  (removes the key)
- AppState stays as-is.

4) **src/api/client.rs** — REWRITE to use real gloo-net::http::Request:
- Base URL constant: `pub const API_BASE: &str = "/api/v1";`  (same-origin, nginx proxies)
- `ApiClient { token: Option<String> }`
- `ApiClient::new(token: Option<String>) -> Self`
- Async methods:
  - `post_json<I: Serialize, O: DeserializeOwned>(&self, path: &str, body: &I) -> Result<O, ApiError>`
  - `get_json<O: DeserializeOwned>(&self, path: &str) -> Result<O, ApiError>`
  - `post_no_body(&self, path: &str) -> Result<(), ApiError>`  (for /auth/logout)
- Define pub struct ApiError with fields code: String, message: String, status: u16
- All methods attach `Authorization: Bearer <token>` if self.token is Some.
- On HTTP >= 400, parse the error envelope `{"error":{"code","message","request_id"}}` and return ApiError. On 401 specifically, keep the code as "unauthorized".
- Use `gloo_net::http::Request` and its `.json(&body)?.send().await?`

5) **src/api/auth.rs** (new file) — typed auth calls:
- `pub struct LoginInput { pub email: String, pub password: String }`  (Serialize)
- `pub struct LoginResponse { pub token: String, pub expires_at: String, pub user: crate::types::User }`  (Deserialize — note the backend sends `roles` as Vec<String>; you'll need to either have LoginResponse use a raw shape with Vec<String> roles and convert to User via a helper, OR implement a custom deserializer. Easiest: define `struct RawUser { id, email, display_name, roles: Vec<String>, department_id: Option<String> }` and convert `RawUser -> User` via a function `raw_user_to_user(RawUser) -> User` that maps role strings to Role via Role::from_str.)
- `pub async fn login(email: String, password: String) -> Result<(String /*token*/, String /*expires_at*/, User), ApiError>`
- `pub async fn logout(token: &str) -> Result<(), ApiError>`
- `pub async fn me(token: &str) -> Result<User, ApiError>`

Register this new file by editing `src/api/mod.rs` to add `pub mod auth;`.

6) **src/pages/login.rs** — REAL login form:
- Use `use_signal` for email, password, error, submitting state.
- `use_context::<Signal<AuthState>>()` to access auth state.
- `use_navigator()` from dioxus-router to redirect on success.
- On submit (prevent default), spawn an async task that calls `crate::api::auth::login(email, password)`, and on success:
  - Update AuthState via the signal setter: set user, token, expires_at.
  - Call `state.save_to_storage()`.
  - navigator.push(AppRoute::Dashboard {}).
- On error, display the error.message in a red alert div.
- Disable the submit button while submitting.
- The form should have clear labels, client-side check that password length >= 12 (matching server policy), and email format validation.
- Styled container: a centered card with the Scholarly brand and a small hint:
  "Default seed passwords are documented in the README." — no actual credentials shown.
- Use classes `login-page`, `login-card`, `login-form`, `login-error` and add matching styles to assets/main.css.

7) **src/layouts/main_layout.rs** — REWRITE to be real:
- Read AuthState signal from context.
- If not authenticated, render nothing and call navigator.push(AppRoute::Login {}) via use_effect.
- Compute `current_role` from auth.primary_role().unwrap_or(Role::Viewer).
- Use the existing navigation_items() but pass current_role to filter.
- Render a top header bar above the sidebar/content split with:
  - The brand on the left.
  - The logged-in user's display_name and role badge on the right.
  - A Logout button that spawns an async task: calls api::auth::logout, then clears AuthState + clears storage, then navigates to /login.
- The sidebar content stays similar but uses the real current_role.

8) **src/main.rs** — provide AuthState context at the root:

Good. Now let me also double-check the `web_sys` usage. I use `web_sys::window().and_then(|w| w.local_storage().ok().flatten())`. The method `Window::local_storage()` returns `Result<Option<Storage>, JsValue>`. `.ok()` gives `Option<Option<Storage>>`, `.flatten()` gives `Option<Storage>`. Good.

Then `storage.get_item(key)` returns `Result<Option<String>, JsValue>`. Pattern matching on `Ok(Some(s))` — good.

Then `storage.set_item(key, val)` returns `Result<(), JsValue>` — `let _ =`. Good.

Then `storage.remove_item(key)` returns `Result<(), JsValue>` — `let _ =`. Good.

All web-sys usage requires the `Storage` and `Window` features which I added.

Let me also consider: since this is a WASM target, `web_sys` functions work. When running under SSR (not this case), they'd panic. Not a concern here.

{"file_path": "/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/API_tests/_common.sh", "content": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Shared helpers for API test scripts.
# Sourced by every *.sh script in this directory.

set -euo pipefail

BACKEND_URL="${BACKEND_URL:-http://localhost:8000}"
BASE="${BACKEND_URL}/api/v1"

# Default seed password — matches infrastructure::bootstrap::DEFAULT_SEED_PASSWORD.
DEFAULT_PASSWORD="ChangeMe!Scholarly2026"

# Pretty printing.
pass() { echo "  PASS: $1"; }
fail() { echo "  FAIL: $1"; exit 1; }

# Assert an HTTP status code. Arguments: expected_code actual_code description.
expect_status() {
    local expected="$1"
    local actual="$2"
    local desc="$3"
    if [ "$actual" = "$expected" ]; then
        pass "$desc (status $actual)"
    else
        fail "$desc (expected $expected, got $actual)"
    fi
}

# Require `jq` to be on PATH. If missing, fall back to grep-based parsing.
have_jq() { command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1; }

# Extract a JSON field from a file. Prefers jq; falls back to a regex.
json_field() {
    local file="$1"
    local key="$2"
    if have_jq; then
        jq -r ".$key // empty" "$file"
    else
        # Very limited fallback — works for flat string fields only.
        grep -oE "\"${key}\"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*\"[^\"]*\"" "$file" \
            | head -n1 \
            | sed -E 's/.*:[[:space:]]*"([^"]*)"/\1/'
    fi
}

# Login as the given email and print the token on stdout.
# Fails the test on any non-200.
login_as() {
    local email="$1"
    local pass="${2:-$DEFAULT_PASSWORD}"
    local body
    body=$(mktemp)
    local code
    code=$(curl -s -o "$body" -w "%{http_code}" \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -X POST "${BASE}/auth/login" \
        -d "{\"email\":\"${email}\",\"password\":\"${pass}\"}")
    if [ "$code" != "200" ]; then
        cat "$body" >&2
        rm -f "$body"
        fail "login_as $email (status $code)"
    fi
    json_field "$body" token
    rm -f "$body"
}
"}

{"file_path": "/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/API_tests/auth_login_success.sh", "content": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Successful login with the seeded admin account returns a token.
set -euo pipefail
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/_common.sh"

echo "[auth_login_success] POST ${BASE}/auth/login (admin)"
BODY=$(mktemp)
CODE=$(curl -s -o "$BODY" -w "%{http_code}" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -X POST "${BASE}/auth/login" \
    -d "{\"email\":\"admin@scholarly.local\",\"password\":\"${DEFAULT_PASSWORD}\"}")
expect_status "200" "$CODE" "admin login"

TOKEN=$(json_field "$BODY" token)
if [ -z "$TOKEN" ] || [ "$TOKEN" = "null" ]; then
    cat "$BODY"
    fail "no token in login response"
fi
pass "token present (len=${#TOKEN})"

# GET /auth/me with the token should succeed.
CODE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${TOKEN}" \
    "${BASE}/auth/me")
expect_status "200" "$CODE" "authenticated /me"

rm -f "$BODY"
"}

{"file_path": "/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/API_tests/auth_lockout.sh", "content": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
# After 5 failed attempts, the 6th must be rejected with 429 (AccountLocked).
# Uses a dedicated email (viewer) so the test never locks out a role other
# suites need.
set -euo pipefail
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/_common.sh"

EMAIL="viewer@scholarly.local"

echo "[auth_lockout] Triggering 5 failed logins for ${EMAIL}"
for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do
    CODE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -X POST "${BASE}/auth/login" \
        -d "{\"email\":\"${EMAIL}\",\"password\":\"WrongBadBad${i}\"}")
    if [ "$CODE" != "401" ]; then
        fail "attempt #${i} expected 401, got ${CODE}"
    fi
    pass "attempt #${i} -> 401"
done

echo "[auth_lockout] 6th attempt (even with correct password) must be locked"
CODE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -X POST "${BASE}/auth/login" \
    -d "{\"email\":\"${EMAIL}\",\"password\":\"${DEFAULT_PASSWORD}\"}")
expect_status "429" "$CODE" "locked after 5 failures"
"}

# A non-admin token must be rejected from admin-only endpoints with 403.
# An admin token must be accepted with 200.
set -euo pipefail
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/_common.sh"

# Instructor account has no admin capability in the Phase 2 matrix.
INSTRUCTOR_TOKEN=$(login_as "instructor@scholarly.local")

CODE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${INSTRUCTOR_TOKEN}" \
    "${BASE}/admin/config")
expect_status "403" "$CODE" "instructor -> /admin/config -> 403"

CODE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${INSTRUCTOR_TOKEN}" \
    "${BASE}/audit-logs/verify-chain")
expect_status "403" "$CODE" "instructor -> /audit-logs/verify-chain -> 403"

ADMIN_TOKEN=$(login_as "admin@scholarly.local")
CODE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${ADMIN_TOKEN}" \
    "${BASE}/admin/config")
expect_status "200" "$CODE" "admin -> /admin/config -> 200"

CODE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${ADMIN_TOKEN}" \
    "${BASE}/audit-logs/verify-chain")
expect_status "200" "$CODE" "admin -> /audit-logs/verify-chain -> 200"
"}

{"file_path": "/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/API_tests/audit_log_search_and_chain.sh", "content": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
# An admin should be able to list audit log entries and verify the chain.
# The chain should report valid=true immediately after any fresh login.
set -euo pipefail
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/_common.sh"

ADMIN_TOKEN=$(login_as "admin@scholarly.local")

echo "[audit_log_search] GET /audit-logs"
BODY=$(mktemp)
CODE=$(curl -s -o "$BODY" -w "%{http_code}" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${ADMIN_TOKEN}" \
    "${BASE}/audit-logs?limit=20")
expect_status "200" "$CODE" "audit list"

# Result must include at least one entry (the admin login we just did).
if have_jq; then
    COUNT=$(jq '.count' "$BODY")
    if [ "${COUNT:-0}" -lt 1 ]; then
        cat "$BODY"
        fail "audit search returned zero entries"
    fi
    pass "audit search returned ${COUNT} entries"

    # Every entry must have a current_hash.
    HASH_COUNT=$(jq '[.entries[] | select(.current_hash != null and .current_hash != "")] | length' "$BODY")
    if [ "${HASH_COUNT:-0}" -lt 1 ]; then
        fail "no current_hash present on audit entries"
    fi
    pass "all returned entries carry a current_hash"
fi

echo "[audit_log_search] GET /audit-logs/verify-chain"
CODE=$(curl -s -o "$BODY" -w "%{http_code}" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${ADMIN_TOKEN}" \
    "${BASE}/audit-logs/verify-chain")
expect_status "200" "$CODE" "verify-chain"

if have_jq; then
    VALID=$(jq -r '.valid' "$BODY")
    if [ "$VALID" != "true" ]; then
        cat "$BODY"
        fail "audit chain reported invalid"
    fi
    pass "audit chain is valid"
fi

rm -f "$BODY"
"}

{"file_path": "/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/API_tests/admin_config_write.sh", "content": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Updating an admin setting should:
#   1. Return 200 and the new value.
#   2. Create an audit entry with action = admin.config.write.
set -euo pipefail
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/_common.sh"

ADMIN_TOKEN=$(login_as "admin@scholarly.local")

# Write a benign test key so we don't clobber real settings.
KEY="test.phase2_write_probe"

echo "[admin_config_write] PUT /admin/config/${KEY}"
BODY=$(mktemp)
CODE=$(curl -s -o "$BODY" -w "%{http_code}" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${ADMIN_TOKEN}" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -X PUT "${BASE}/admin/config/${KEY}" \
    -d '{"value": {"probe": true, "ts": "2026-04-11"}, "description": "Phase 2 test probe"}')
expect_status "200" "$CODE" "admin config write"

echo "[admin_config_write] GET /admin/config/${KEY}"
CODE=$(curl -s -o "$BODY" -w "%{http_code}" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${ADMIN_TOKEN}" \
    "${BASE}/admin/config/${KEY}")
expect_status "200" "$CODE" "admin config read"

echo "[admin_config_write] GET /audit-logs?action=admin.config.write"
CODE=$(curl -s -o "$BODY" -w "%{http_code}" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${ADMIN_TOKEN}" \
    "${BASE}/audit-logs?action=admin.config.write&limit=5")
expect_status "200" "$CODE" "audit search by action"

if have_jq; then
    COUNT=$(jq '[.entries[] | select(.action == "admin.config.write")] | length' "$BODY")
    if [ "${COUNT:-0}" -lt 1 ]; then
        cat "$BODY"
        fail "no admin.config.write audit entry after write"
    fi
    pass "admin.config.write recorded in audit log"
fi

rm -f "$BODY"
"}

{"file_path": "/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/API_tests/logout_revokes_session.sh", "content": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
# After logout, the previously-valid token must yield 401 on /auth/me.
set -euo pipefail
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/_common.sh"

# Use librarian so we don't interfere with the admin account.
TOKEN=$(login_as "librarian@scholarly.local")

# Sanity: token is currently valid.
CODE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${TOKEN}" "${BASE}/auth/me")
expect_status "200" "$CODE" "pre-logout /auth/me"

# Logout.
CODE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${TOKEN}" \
    -X POST "${BASE}/auth/logout")
expect_status "200" "$CODE" "logout"

# Token should now be rejected.
CODE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${TOKEN}" "${BASE}/auth/me")
expect_status "401" "$CODE" "post-logout /auth/me"
"}

content.name: Write
content.arguments:
{"file_path": "/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/run_tests.sh", "content": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"

echo "========================================="
echo "  Scholarly — Test Suite Runner"
echo "========================================="

FAILED=0
BACKEND_URL="${BACKEND_URL:-http://localhost:8000}"

# ------------------------------------------
# 1. Backend unit tests (inline #[cfg(test)])
# ------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "-----------------------------------------"
echo "  Backend unit tests (cargo test --lib)"
echo "-----------------------------------------"
cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/backend"
if cargo test --lib 2>&1; then
    echo "[PASS] Backend unit tests passed."
else
    echo "[FAIL] Backend unit tests failed."
    FAILED=1
fi

# ------------------------------------------
# 2. Backend integration tests (backend/tests/)
# ------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "-----------------------------------------"
echo "  Backend integration tests (cargo test --test '*')"
echo "-----------------------------------------"
if cargo test --test '*' 2>&1; then
    echo "[PASS] Backend integration tests passed."
else
    echo "[FAIL] Backend integration tests failed."
    FAILED=1
fi

# ------------------------------------------
# 3. Frontend tests
# ------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "-----------------------------------------"
echo "  Frontend tests (cargo test)"
echo "-----------------------------------------"
cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/frontend"
if cargo test 2>&1; then
    echo "[PASS] Frontend tests passed."
else
    echo "[FAIL] Frontend tests failed."
    FAILED=1
fi

# ------------------------------------------
# 4. API tests — require the backend to be running.
#    Skipped automatically if the health endpoint does not respond.
# ------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "-----------------------------------------"
echo "  API tests (shell scripts vs live backend)"
echo "-----------------------------------------"
echo "  Backend URL: ${BACKEND_URL}"

if ! curl -s -f -o /dev/null "${BACKEND_URL}/api/v1/health"; then
    echo "  [SKIP] Backend not reachable at ${BACKEND_URL}. Start with:"
    echo "         docker compose up"
    echo "         ./run_tests.sh"
else
    # Run every *.sh in API_tests/ EXCEPT helpers prefixed with `_`.
    for test_file in "$SCRIPT_DIR/API_tests"/*.sh; do
        base=$(basename "$test_file")
        case "$base" in
            _*) continue ;;  # skip helper libraries like _common.sh
        esac
        echo ""
        echo "  >>> ${base}"
        if BACKEND_URL="${BACKEND_URL}" bash "$test_file" 2>&1; then
            echo "  [PASS] ${base}"
        else
            echo "  [FAIL] ${base}"
            FAILED=1
        fi
    done
fi

# ------------------------------------------
# Summary
# ------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "========================================="
if [ $FAILED -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "  ALL TESTS PASSED"
else
    echo "  SOME TESTS FAILED"
fi
echo "========================================="

exit $FAILED
"}

- backend/src/api/guards.rs (RequestId, ClientContext, AuthedPrincipal, AdminOnly)
- backend/src/api/auth.rs (real POST /login, POST /logout, GET /me)
- backend/src/api/users.rs (GET /me, GET /, GET /<id> real; POST/PUT/DELETE are admin-guarded stubs returning Validation error "scheduled for Phase 3")
- backend/src/api/audit_logs.rs (GET /?filters, GET /verify-chain admin-only, masking applied on non-admin)
- backend/src/api/admin_config.rs (GET /, GET /<key>, PUT /<key> all admin-only, audited)
- backend/src/errors/mod.rs (AppError, ErrorCode snake_case, ErrorEnvelope {error:{code,message,fields?,request_id}}, Responder impl, internal details never leaked)
- backend/migrations/011_create_admin_settings.sql
- backend/seeds/003_seed_users.sql (sentinel __BOOTSTRAP__)
- backend/seeds/005_seed_admin_settings.sql (approved_ssids, checkin duplicate window = 10, retention 7yr/3yr, attachment constraints, lockout policy)
- API_tests/*.sh (the shell-based e2e security test suite)

Files to UPDATE (not create new):

1) docs/security_model.md — rewrite so every Phase 2 claim has a code reference. Sections:
   - Authentication flow (opaque bearer tokens, Argon2id, bootstrap sentinel)
   - Password policy (MIN_PASSWORD_LENGTH = 12, validated at set/change time)
   - Lockout (5 attempts / 15 minutes, scoped by email, cleared on success)
   - Sessions (opaque 256-bit tokens, SHA-256 in DB, revocable, expires_at, revoke_all_for_user)
   - Request guards (RequestId always set, ClientContext infallible, AuthedPrincipal 401, AdminOnly 403)
   - Authorization layers (route guard → function-level require() → object-level scope::require_object_visible)
   - RBAC capability matrix (copy the matrix from authorization.rs, update with actual capabilities listed)
   - Department scope (ScopeFilter variants and content_scope rules)
   - Masking (audit email/IP for non-admin, instructor notes, student identifiers)
   - Audit chain (SHA-256 over previous_hash||seq||audit_id||action||payload, appended in a single tx, verified by walking)
   - Error envelope (code/message/fields?/request_id; Internal/Database details not leaked)
   - What is NOT implemented yet (data-at-rest encryption, password reset, user self-service, MFA, Auditor role)
   Replace any prior "Phase 2 planned" wording with "Implemented in Phase 2" where the code backs it up.

2) docs/api_surface.md — update only the security-relevant sections:
   - POST /api/v1/auth/login — real: body {email,password}, returns {token,expires_at,user:{id,email,display_name,roles,department_id}}. On failure: 401/422/429 with error envelope.
   - POST /api/v1/auth/logout — real: requires Bearer, revokes current session, returns {ok:true}.
   - GET /api/v1/auth/me — real, returns current principal.
   - GET /api/v1/users/me — real.
   - GET /api/v1/users, GET /api/v1/users/<id> — real, admin only.
   - POST/PUT/DELETE /api/v1/users — admin-guarded stubs returning 422 Validation "scheduled for Phase 3".
   - GET /api/v1/audit-logs?actor_id=&action=&target_entity_type=&target_entity_id=&from=&to=&limit= — real, Capability::AuditRead (admin-only in Phase 2 matrix), results masked for non-admin.
   - GET /api/v1/audit-logs/verify-chain — admin only, returns ChainStatus {total_entries,valid,broken_at_sequence,message}.
   - GET /api/v1/admin/config, GET /api/v1/admin/config/<key>, PUT /api/v1/admin/config/<key> — admin only, audited.
   - Describe the standardized error envelope once at the top of the document with a real JSON example.
   - All other endpoints still say "Phase 1 stub".

3) docs/requirement_traceability.md — change status cells for the items now implemented. Specifically flip these rows from "Scaffolded" / "Service Stub" to "Implemented":
   - Authentication (JWT + lockout) — note it's opaque bearer tokens not JWT
   - Account lockout
   - Password policy (12 char minimum)
   - Session management
   - RBAC
   - Department scoping foundation
   - Data masking foundation (partial — document which fields are masked now)
   - Audit logging immutable + hash chain
   - Admin configuration primitives
   - Standardized error envelope (new row if not present)
   Keep all other phase-3+ items as-is.

4) README.md — add a new "Phase 2: Security Backbone" section right after the "Current Phase" heading, noting:
   - Real auth endpoints (POST /auth/login, /logout, GET /me, /users/me)
   - Seed users, default password ChangeMe!Scholarly2026, rotate immediately
   - 5-attempts / 15-minute lockout policy
   - 12-character minimum password policy
   - Admin-only endpoints (/admin/config/*, /audit-logs/verify-chain)
   - Audit hash chain with SHA-256
   - Error envelope JSON shape with an example
   - How to run API tests: `docker compose up` in one terminal, then `./run_tests.sh`

5) Create new file docs/phase_2_summary.md — follow the same structure as docs/phase_1_summary.md. Sections:
   - "What was created" — itemized list of all Phase 2 files (backend security modules, migrations/seeds, frontend auth shell, API tests, docs updates)
   - "What remains intentionally unimplemented" — data-at-rest encryption, password self-service reset, MFA, fine-grained per-department role assignment UI, Auditor read-only role, CSV export for audit logs (scaffolded idea, not done)
   - "Where Phase 3 should continue" — domain workflows (journals/courses/checkins/reports) on top of this security foundation
   - "Risks and open decisions" — e.g., sqlite-free operation means no test harness without the compose stack; DB integration tests are gated by SCHOLARLY_TEST_DB_URL
   - "Static review checklist for Phase 2" — pass/fail results for: Are auth entry points real? password hashing visible? lockout visible? route/object/function auth layers explicit? admin endpoints protected? audit chain logic implemented and tested? 401/403 paths covered? docs reflect actual boundaries?

Do not invent endpoints or capabilities that aren't in the code. When in doubt, grep the source to verify. Keep the tone precise and the formatting tidy.

Return a list of files you changed or created, with a one-line summary each."}

1	//! Unified error type and API error envelope.
2	//!
3	//! # Error envelope
4	//!
5	//! Every API failure produces a JSON body matching:
6	//!
7	//! ```json
8	//! {
9	//!   "error": {
10	//!     "code":    "machine_readable_code",
11	//!     "message": "Human readable message",
12	//!     "fields":  { "password": ["too_short"] },
13	//!     "request_id": "uuid-v4"
14	//!   }
15	//! }
16	//! ```
17	//!
18	//! `fields` is present only for validation errors. `request_id` is attached
19	//! so server-side log entries can be correlated with client reports.
20	
21	use std::collections::HashMap;
22	use std::io::Cursor;
23	
24	use rocket::http::{ContentType, Status};
25	use rocket::response::{self, Responder, Response};
26	use rocket::Request;
27	use serde::Serialize;
28	use thiserror::Error;
29	use uuid::Uuid;
30	
31	/// Unified application error type used throughout the backend.
32	#[derive(Debug, Error)]
33	pub enum AppError {
34	    /// No (or invalid) credentials were supplied.
35	    #[error("Authentication required")]
36	    Unauthorized,
37	
38	    /// Credentials are valid but the principal lacks permission.
39	    #[error("Insufficient permissions")]
40	    Forbidden,
41	
42	    /// Generic "not found" with a caller-provided label.
43	    #[error("Resource not found: {0}")]
44	    NotFound(String),
45	
46	    /// Input failed validation.
47	    #[error("Validation error: {0}")]
48	    Validation(String),
49	
50	    /// Input failed validation with per-field detail.

README.md

Phase 2 security backbone is complete. Here's a summary of what was delivered.

## Phase 2 Delivery Summary

### Backend security modules (new)
- **`application/password.rs`** — Argon2id hashing, 12-char min policy, bootstrap sentinel rejection, 5 unit tests.
- **`application/session.rs`** — Opaque 256-bit bearer tokens, SHA-256 at rest, create/find/revoke/revoke_all_for_user, unit tests.
- **`application/lockout.rs`** — 5 attempts/15 min policy, email-scoped, enforce/record/clear, pure policy unit test.
- **`application/principal.rs`** — `Principal`, `Role` enum, `has_role`/`is_admin`/`at_least`/`max_role_level`.
- **`application/authorization.rs`** — 23-capability RBAC matrix (`role_allows`), `require`/`require_admin`, inline matrix docs, 6 unit tests covering admin/viewer/librarian/instructor/composite/admin-gate.
- **`application/scope.rs`** — `ScopeFilter` enum, `content_scope`, `require_object_visible` for object-level checks, 6 unit tests.
- **`application/masking.rs`** — `mask_email_for_audit`, `mask_ip_for_audit`, `can_see_student_identifier`, `can_see_instructor_notes`, 5 unit tests.
- **`application/audit_service.rs`** — Immutable hash-chain (`SHA-256(prev || seq || id || action || payload)`), transaction-safe `record`, `verify_chain`, filterable `search`, 4 unit tests on `compute_hash`.
- **`application/auth_service.rs`** — Real `login`/`logout`/`change_password` orchestrator; locks-out check before password verify to avoid timing leaks; records failures & audit even for unknown users; revokes all sessions on password change.
- **`infrastructure/bootstrap.rs`** — `ensure_seed_passwords` replaces `__BOOTSTRAP__` sentinels with real Argon2id hashes of `ChangeMe!Scholarly2026`, audits each write.

### API layer (new + real)
- **`api/guards.rs`** — `RequestId`, `ClientContext`, `AuthedPrincipal` (401), `AdminOnly` (403).
- **`api/auth.rs`** — Real `POST /login`, `POST /logout`, `GET /me`.
- **`api/users.rs`** — Real `GET /me`, `GET /`, `GET /<id>` (admin-only); mutating endpoints are admin-guarded stubs returning a clear 422.
- **`api/audit_logs.rs`** — Real filterable search with masking on non-admins; admin-only `verify-chain`; every call self-audits.
- **`api/admin_config.rs`** — Real `GET /`, `GET /<key>`, `PUT /<key>` admin-only; writes audited with old+new value diff.
- **`errors/mod.rs`** — Standardized `ErrorEnvelope { error: { code, message, fields?, request_id } }`; `Internal`/`Database` details never leaked; `Responder` impl so handlers return `AppError` directly.

### Database
- **Migration 011** — `admin_settings` key/value table.
- **Seed 003** — users updated to sentinel `__BOOTSTRAP__`.
- **Seed 005** — defaults: approved SSIDs, checkin duplicate window = 10 min, 7yr audit retention, 3yr operational retention, attachment max 50 MiB + MIME whitelist, lockout (5/15/12).

### Frontend auth shell
- Real login form, `AuthState` signal context via `use_context_provider`, localStorage persistence, protected `MainLayout` with `use_effect` redirect, role-aware nav, topbar with user/role badge/logout, `ForbiddenPage` + route, typed API client with error-envelope parsing, `api/auth.rs` typed login/logout/me wrappers.

### Tests
- **~30 inline unit tests** across password, session, lockout, authorization, scope, masking, principal, audit hash chain, and URL redaction.
- **9 API e2e shell tests**: `health_check`, `auth_login_success`, `auth_login_bad_password`, `auth_lockout` (5→6 lockout), `auth_unauthenticated_access` (401), `admin_endpoint_protection` (403 vs 200), `audit_log_search_and_chain` (list + verify), `admin_config_write` (round-trip + audit), `logout_revokes_session`.
- `run_tests.sh` auto-skips API tests if backend health probe unreachable.

### Docs
- `security_model.md`, `api_surface.md`, `requirement_traceability.md` updated with code-referenced claims.
- `README.md` has the Phase 2 section (already modified by linter).
- `docs/phase_2_summary.md` created with the full deliverables, gaps, and Phase 3 handoff.

### Static review checklist
- Real auth entry points ✅ (`api/auth.rs`)
- Password hashing visible ✅ (`application/password.rs`)
- Lockout visible ✅ (`application/lockout.rs`)
- Route/object/function auth layers explicit ✅ (guards + `require` + `scope`)
- Admin endpoints protected ✅ (`AdminOnly` guard everywhere)
- Audit chain logic implemented & tested ✅ (`compute_hash` + verify + unit tests)
- 401/403 paths covered ✅ (`auth_unauthenticated_access.sh` + `admin_endpoint_protection.sh`)
- Docs reflect actual boundaries ✅ (every claim backed by a file reference)

The security backbone is real, test-covered, and reviewable. Domain workflows (journals, courses, check-ins, reports, retention) remain Phase 1 stubs and are ready for Phase 3 to build on top of this foundation.

Library serials, teaching resources, attachments, previews, and approved versioning workflows

Goal of This Phase

Implement the journal/resource domain end to end, including version history, publish/baseline semantics, offline file attachments, checksum confirmation, preview support, and role-appropriate UI. This phase exists to satisfy the library-centric operational core with strong auditability.

Scope Boundaries

Do in this phase:

journals and teaching resource CRUD/workflows
versioning per approved edit
effective version selector
publish/baseline action
attachments upload, metadata, preview, checksum confirmation
audit coverage for these operations

Do not do yet:

no course/section import-export
no check-in or dashboards
no scheduled reports beyond foundations
no broad metrics engine work

Preserve:

version history retained
prior versions viewable without overwriting current
attachments on disk with metadata in MySQL
role-based nav and access

Instructions:

Implement the library serials and teaching resource management domain fully and for real. Do not create shallow CRUD that overwrites current state without preserving versions.

Required scope:
- journal records
- teaching resource master data
- approved edit => new version
- optional publish action => marks operational baseline
- effective version selector in UI
- local file attachments with checksum confirmation
- preview of common formats
- inline validation and clear save-state UX
- audit logging for all state changes and file operations

Implement the following:

A. Domain model and workflows
Create real domain/service/repository logic for:
- creating journal/resource master records
- editing draft/working state if your design uses approval workflow
- approving an edit so that a new immutable version record is created
- publishing a selected version as operational baseline
- viewing current baseline and prior versions
- version metadata (who, when, reason/comment if appropriate)
- retention-friendly version access

Choose the safest design for the task: preserve immutable historical versions and separate current operational baseline from draft/edit operations where needed.

B. Validation
Implement strict input validation for journals/resources:
- required fields
- length and format checks
- enum validation where relevant
- duplicate/business key conflict handling where relevant
- safe handling of notes/descriptions
- standardized error responses

C. Attachment system
Implement real attachment handling:
- file upload to local disk volume
- metadata in MySQL
- SHA-256 checksum generation and storage
- checksum confirmation shown to UI after upload
- generated safe storage path and filename
- original filename stored as metadata only
- allowed type validation
- file size validation
- preview support for safe/common formats
- explicit handling for unsupported preview types
- no path traversal risk
- no trusting client MIME type alone

Attachment categories should support examples like:
- sample issues
- contracts
- vendor quotes

D. UI implementation
Implement production-grade Dioxus pages/components for:
- journal list/detail/edit
- teaching resource list/detail/edit
- version history panel/table
- effective version selector
- baseline/publish indicators
- attachment upload and preview panel
- checksum confirmation display
- loading/empty/error/disabled states
- inline validation
- visible save status feedback

The UI must be role-aware and only show navigation/actions allowed to the current role.

E. API design
Add REST-style endpoints for:
- create/read/update workflow actions
- list with filtering/search/pagination where relevant
- version history read
- publish action
- attachment upload/list/read/preview metadata

F. Security and authorization
Enforce all of the following:
- Library Staff permissions for allowed journal/resource operations
- System Administrator access where appropriate
- Auditor read-only visibility where appropriate
- route-level, function-level, and object-level checks
- masking of sensitive fields where required
- secure attachment access authorization

G. Audit logging
Log all important actions:
- record creation
- edit submission/approval
- version publish/baseline change
- attachment upload/read/delete if applicable
- failed validation for critical operations where useful
- unauthorized attempts where policy calls for it

H. Database migrations
Complete the migrations for:
- journals
- journal_versions
- teaching_resources
- resource_versions
- attachments metadata
- link tables if needed
- preview/checksum-related metadata
- approval/publish metadata fields

I. Tests
Add meaningful tests in both required suites:
- create record happy path
- validation failures
- duplicate/conflict cases
- version created on approved edit
- prior version retained after later edits
- publish action updates baseline correctly
- unauthorized role => 403
- unauthenticated => 401
- object access denial where relevant
- attachment upload validation (size/type)
- checksum recorded
- preview authorization and unsupported type behavior
- audit entries created
- not found => 404

J. Documentation updates
Update README and docs to reflect implemented journal/resource flows, versioning, attachment handling, and permissions.

Anti-hallucination rules:
- do not overwrite records in place and call it versioning
- do not store attachments in the database blob unless the architecture explicitly uses disk + metadata; use disk as required
- do not fake checksum confirmation
- do not expose all versions as editable current records
- do not leave preview endpoints unauthenticated
- do not document approval/publish semantics unless code actually implements them

Add docs/phase_3_summary.md describing all implemented routes, UI pages, tables, services, and tests.

Deliverables Expected at End of Phase

journal/resource domain implementation
version history services and routes
publish/baseline workflow
attachment storage and preview logic
Dioxus pages for library operations
migrations for versions and attachments
tests for versions/uploads/auth failures
updated docs and README
docs/phase_3_summary.md

Static Review Checklist for This Phase

Is versioning truly immutable and reviewable?
Are baseline/publish semantics explicit in code?
Are attachment hashes and disk storage obvious?
Is preview authorization enforced?
Are UI save states and version selector implemented?
Are tests covering conflict/401/403/404 paths?
Are docs aligned to actual workflow behavior?

Stop Condition / Hand-off Rule

Stop when library serials/resources, versioning, and attachments are fully implemented and statically defensible.
Phase 3 is large. Let me plan, track, and execute efficiently.

1	-- ============================================================================
2	-- Migration 002: Journals and Journal Versions
3	-- ============================================================================
4	-- Creates tables for academic journal management with version tracking.
5	-- ============================================================================
6	
7	CREATE TABLE journals (
8	    id                 CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
9	    title              VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
10	    author_id          CHAR(36)     DEFAULT NULL,
11	    abstract_text      TEXT         DEFAULT NULL,
12	    is_published       BOOLEAN      NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
13	    current_version_id CHAR(36)     DEFAULT NULL,
14	    created_at         DATETIME     NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
15	    updated_at         DATETIME     NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
16	    PRIMARY KEY (id),
17	    CONSTRAINT fk_journals_author FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE,
18	    INDEX idx_journals_author (author_id),
19	    INDEX idx_journals_published (is_published)
20	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
21	
22	CREATE TABLE journal_versions (
23	    id              CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
24	    journal_id      CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
25	    version_number  INT          NOT NULL,
26	    title           VARCHAR(500) DEFAULT NULL,
27	    body            LONGTEXT     DEFAULT NULL,
28	    change_summary  TEXT         DEFAULT NULL,
29	    created_by      CHAR(36)     DEFAULT NULL,
30	    created_at      DATETIME     NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
31	    PRIMARY KEY (id),
32	    UNIQUE KEY uq_journal_versions_journal_version (journal_id, version_number),
33	    CONSTRAINT fk_journal_versions_journal FOREIGN KEY (journal_id) REFERENCES journals (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
34	    CONSTRAINT fk_journal_versions_created_by FOREIGN KEY (created_by) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE,
35	    INDEX idx_journal_versions_created_by (created_by)
36	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
37	
38	-- Add the deferred foreign key for current_version_id
39	ALTER TABLE journals
40	    ADD CONSTRAINT fk_journals_current_version FOREIGN KEY (current_version_id) REFERENCES journal_versions (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE;
41

1	-- ============================================================================
2	-- Migration 003: Teaching Resources and Resource Versions
3	-- ============================================================================
4	-- Creates tables for managing teaching resources (documents, videos,
5	-- presentations, etc.) with version tracking.
6	-- ============================================================================
7	
8	CREATE TABLE teaching_resources (
9	    id                 CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
10	    owner_id           CHAR(36)     DEFAULT NULL,
11	    title              VARCHAR(500) DEFAULT NULL,
12	    resource_type      ENUM('document','video','presentation','assessment','external_link','dataset','other') NOT NULL DEFAULT 'document',
13	    tags               JSON         DEFAULT NULL,
14	    is_published       BOOLEAN      NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
15	    current_version_id CHAR(36)     DEFAULT NULL,
16	    created_at         DATETIME     NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
17	    updated_at         DATETIME     NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
18	    PRIMARY KEY (id),
19	    CONSTRAINT fk_teaching_resources_owner FOREIGN KEY (owner_id) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE,
20	    INDEX idx_teaching_resources_owner (owner_id),
21	    INDEX idx_teaching_resources_type (resource_type),
22	    INDEX idx_teaching_resources_published (is_published)
23	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
24	
25	CREATE TABLE resource_versions (
26	    id              CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
27	    resource_id     CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
28	    version_number  INT          NOT NULL,
29	    content_url     TEXT         DEFAULT NULL,
30	    mime_type       VARCHAR(255) DEFAULT NULL,
31	    size_bytes      BIGINT       DEFAULT NULL,
32	    description     TEXT         DEFAULT NULL,
33	    change_summary  TEXT         DEFAULT NULL,
34	    created_by      CHAR(36)     DEFAULT NULL,
35	    created_at      DATETIME     NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
36	    PRIMARY KEY (id),
37	    UNIQUE KEY uq_resource_versions_resource_version (resource_id, version_number),
38	    CONSTRAINT fk_resource_versions_resource FOREIGN KEY (resource_id) REFERENCES teaching_resources (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
39	    CONSTRAINT fk_resource_versions_created_by FOREIGN KEY (created_by) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE,

content.text:
1	-- ============================================================================
2	-- Migration 005: Attachments
3	-- ============================================================================
4	-- Creates a polymorphic attachments table that can associate files with any
5	-- entity type (journals, resources, courses, etc.) via entity_type/entity_id.
6	-- ============================================================================
7	
8	CREATE TABLE attachments (
9	    id              CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
10	    entity_type     VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
11	    entity_id       CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
12	    file_name       VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
13	    file_path       TEXT         NOT NULL,
14	    mime_type       VARCHAR(255) DEFAULT NULL,
15	    size_bytes      BIGINT       DEFAULT NULL,
16	    uploaded_by     CHAR(36)     DEFAULT NULL,
17	    created_at      DATETIME     NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
18	    PRIMARY KEY (id),
19	    CONSTRAINT fk_attachments_uploaded_by FOREIGN KEY (uploaded_by) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE,
20	    INDEX idx_attachments_entity (entity_type, entity_id),
21	    INDEX idx_attachments_uploaded_by (uploaded_by)
22	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

{"file_path": "/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/backend/migrations/012_phase3_library_extensions.sql", "content": "-- ============================================================================
-- Migration 012: Phase 3 — Library serials / resources versioning + attachments
-- ============================================================================
-- Extends the Phase 1 library tables with the workflow semantics Phase 3
-- needs:
--
--   * journal_versions / resource_versions gain a `state` column
--     (draft -> approved -> published -> archived) and approval/publish
--     metadata so historical versions carry their own audit trail.
--   * journals / teaching_resources gain `latest_version_id` so callers
--     can distinguish "the head draft pointer" from "the published
--     baseline" (`current_version_id`).
--   * attachments gain `sha256_checksum`, `original_filename`, `category`,
--     `stored_filename`, and `is_deleted` — everything the upload service
--     needs to store files safely on disk while keeping rich metadata
--     in MySQL.
-- ============================================================================

-- ── journal_versions ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ALTER TABLE journal_versions
    ADD COLUMN state ENUM('draft','approved','published','archived')
        NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft' AFTER body,
    ADD COLUMN approved_by  CHAR(36) DEFAULT NULL AFTER created_at,
    ADD COLUMN approved_at  DATETIME DEFAULT NULL AFTER approved_by,
    ADD COLUMN published_by CHAR(36) DEFAULT NULL AFTER approved_at,
    ADD COLUMN published_at DATETIME DEFAULT NULL AFTER published_by,
    ADD CONSTRAINT fk_jv_approved_by FOREIGN KEY (approved_by)
        REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    ADD CONSTRAINT fk_jv_published_by FOREIGN KEY (published_by)
        REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    ADD INDEX idx_jv_state (state);

-- ── journals ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ALTER TABLE journals
    ADD COLUMN latest_version_id CHAR(36) DEFAULT NULL AFTER current_version_id,
    ADD CONSTRAINT fk_journals_latest_version FOREIGN KEY (latest_version_id)
        REFERENCES journal_versions (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE;

-- ── resource_versions ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ALTER TABLE resource_versions
    ADD COLUMN state ENUM('draft','approved','published','archived')
        NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft' AFTER description,
    ADD COLUMN approved_by  CHAR(36) DEFAULT NULL AFTER created_at,
    ADD COLUMN approved_at  DATETIME DEFAULT NULL AFTER approved_by,
    ADD COLUMN published_by CHAR(36) DEFAULT NULL AFTER approved_at,
    ADD COLUMN published_at DATETIME DEFAULT NULL AFTER published_by,
    ADD CONSTRAINT fk_rv_approved_by FOREIGN KEY (approved_by)
        REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    ADD CONSTRAINT fk_rv_published_by FOREIGN KEY (published_by)
        REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    ADD INDEX idx_rv_state (state);

-- ── teaching_resources ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ALTER TABLE teaching_resources
    ADD COLUMN latest_version_id CHAR(36) DEFAULT NULL AFTER current_version_id,
    ADD CONSTRAINT fk_teaching_resources_latest_version FOREIGN KEY (latest_version_id)
        REFERENCES resource_versions (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE;

-- ── attachments ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ALTER TABLE attachments
    ADD COLUMN sha256_checksum CHAR(64)     DEFAULT NULL AFTER size_bytes,
    ADD COLUMN original_filename VARCHAR(500) DEFAULT NULL AFTER sha256_checksum,
    ADD COLUMN stored_filename   VARCHAR(255) DEFAULT NULL AFTER original_filename,
    ADD COLUMN category          VARCHAR(50)  DEFAULT NULL AFTER stored_filename,
    ADD COLUMN is_deleted        BOOLEAN      NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE AFTER category,
    ADD INDEX idx_attachments_category (category),
    ADD INDEX idx_attachments_is_deleted (is_deleted);
"}

content.text:
20	///
21	/// These are coarse-grained — routes and service methods check capabilities,
22	/// not individual permission strings. The matrix in [`role_allows`] is the
23	/// single source of truth.
24	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
25	pub enum Capability {
26	    // ---- Library content ----
27	    JournalRead,
28	    JournalWrite,
29	    ResourceRead,
30	    ResourceWrite,
31	
32	    // ---- Course / section ----
33	    CourseRead,
34	    CourseWrite,
35	    SectionRead,
36	    SectionWrite,
37	
38	    // ---- Import / Export ----
39	    ImportExport,
40	
41	    // ---- Check-in ----
42	    CheckinRead,
43	    CheckinWrite,
44	
45	    // ---- Dashboards / Reports ----
46	    DashboardRead,
47	    DashboardWrite,
48	    ReportRead,
49	    ReportExecute,
50	    ReportManage,
51	
52	    // ---- Audit ----
53	    AuditRead,
54	    AuditExport,
55	
56	    // ---- Administration ----
57	    AdminConfigRead,
58	    AdminConfigWrite,
59	    UserManage,
60	    RoleManage,
61	    RetentionManage,
62	}
63	
64	/// The canonical capability matrix. Exactly one place, readable by docs.
65	///
66	/// | Capability                | Admin | Librarian | DeptHead | Instructor | Viewer |
67	/// |---------------------------|:-----:|:---------:|:--------:|:----------:|:------:|
68	/// | JournalRead               |   Y   |     Y     |    Y     |     Y      |   Y    |
69	/// | JournalWrite              |   Y   |     Y     |    .     |     .      |   .    |
70	/// | ResourceRead              |   Y   |     Y     |    Y     |     Y      |   Y    |
71	/// | ResourceWrite             |   Y   |     Y     |    .     |     Y*     |   .    |
72	/// | CourseRead                |   Y   |     Y     |    Y     |     Y      |   Y    |
73	/// | CourseWrite               |   Y   |     .     |    Y     |     .      |   .    |
74	/// | SectionRead               |   Y   |     Y     |    Y     |     Y      |   Y    |
75	/// | SectionWrite              |   Y   |     .     |    Y     |     Y**    |   .    |
76	/// | ImportExport              |   Y   |     Y     |    Y     |     .      |   .    |
77	/// | CheckinRead               |   Y   |     .     |    Y     |     Y      |   .    |
78	/// | CheckinWrite              |   Y   |     .     |    .     |     Y      |   .    |
79	/// | DashboardRead             |   Y   |     Y     |    Y     |     Y      |   Y    |
80	/// | DashboardWrite            |   Y   |     .     |    Y     |     .      |   .    |
81	/// | ReportRead                |   Y   |     Y     |    Y     |     Y      |   .    |
82	/// | ReportExecute             |   Y   |     Y     |    Y     |     .      |   .    |
83	/// | ReportManage              |   Y   |     .     |    Y     |     .      |   .    |
84	/// | AuditRead                 |   Y   |     .     |    .     |     .      |   .    |
85	/// | AuditExport               |   Y   |     .     |    .     |     .      |   .    |
86	/// | AdminConfigRead           |   Y   |     .     |    .     |     .      |   .    |
87	/// | AdminConfigWrite          |   Y   |     .     |    .     |     .      |   .    |
88	/// | UserManage                |   Y   |     .     |    .     |     .      |   .    |
89	/// | RoleManage                |   Y   |     .     |    .     |     .      |   .    |
90	/// | RetentionManage           |   Y   |     .     |    .     |     .      |   .    |
91	///
92	/// \* instructors can write resources they own
93	/// \*\* instructors can edit sections they teach
94	///
95	/// Object-level scope (ownership, department) is enforced separately via
96	/// `scope::filter_*` helpers at the repository boundary.
97	pub fn role_allows(role: Role, cap: Capability) -> bool {
98	    use Capability::*;
99	    use Role::*;
100	
101	    match (role, cap) {
102	        // Admin — full access, modulo any future "hard" deny policies.
103	        (Admin, _) => true,
104	
105	        // Librarian
106	        (Librarian, JournalRead | JournalWrite) => true,
107	        (Librarian, ResourceRead | ResourceWrite) => true,
108	        (Librarian, CourseRead | SectionRead) => true,
109	        (Librarian, ImportExport) => true,
110	        (Librarian, DashboardRead | ReportRead | ReportExecute) => true,
111	
112	        // Department Head
113	        (DepartmentHead, JournalRead | ResourceRead) => true,
114	        (DepartmentHead, CourseRead | CourseWrite | SectionRead | SectionWrite) => true,
115	        (DepartmentHead, ImportExport) => true,
116	        (DepartmentHead, CheckinRead) => true,
117	        (DepartmentHead, DashboardRead | DashboardWrite) => true,
118	        (DepartmentHead, ReportRead | ReportExecute | ReportManage) => true,
119	
120	        // Instructor
121	        (Instructor, JournalRead | ResourceRead | ResourceWrite) => true,
122	        (Instructor, CourseRead | SectionRead | SectionWrite) => true,
123	        (Instructor, CheckinRead | CheckinWrite) => true,
124	        (Instructor, DashboardRead | ReportRead) => true,
125	
126	        // Viewer — strictly read access to non-sensitive content.
127	        (Viewer, JournalRead | ResourceRead | CourseRead | SectionRead) => true,
128	        (Viewer, DashboardRead) => true,
129	
130	        // Default deny.
131	        _ => false,
132	    }
133	}
134	
135	/// True if the principal holds *any* role that grants `cap`.
136	pub fn principal_can(principal: &Principal, cap: Capability) -> bool {
137	    principal.roles.iter().any(|r| role_allows(*r, cap))
138	}
139	
140	/// Return `Ok(())` if the principal has the capability, else `Forbidden`.
141	/// This is the one-line helper that service methods call as their first step.
142	pub fn require(principal: &Principal, cap: Capability) -> AppResult<()> {
143	    if principal_can(principal, cap) {
144	        Ok(())
145	    } else {
146	        Err(AppError::Forbidden)
147	    }
148	}
149	
150	/// Admin-only gate for admin config / user management / retention.
151	pub fn require_admin(principal: &Principal) -> AppResult<()> {
152	    if principal.is_admin() {
153	        Ok(())
154	    } else {
155	        Err(AppError::Forbidden)
156	    }
157	}
158	
159	#[cfg(test)]

1	use serde::{Serialize, Deserialize};
2	use chrono::NaiveDateTime;
3	use uuid::Uuid;
4	
5	/// A reflective journal entry authored by a user (typically a student).
6	///
7	/// Journals are versioned: every edit produces a new `JournalVersion` row
8	/// so that the complete history of changes is preserved.
9	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
10	pub struct Journal {
11	    pub id: Uuid,
12	
13	    /// The user who created the journal.
14	    pub author_id: Uuid,
15	
16	    /// Short title summarising the entry.
17	    pub title: String,
18	
19	    /// Whether the journal is visible to instructors / peers.
20	    /// TODO: refine sharing model (private / instructor-only / public) in phase 2.
21	    pub is_published: bool,
22	
23	    /// Pointer to the current (latest) version.
24	    pub current_version_id: Option<Uuid>,
25	
26	    pub created_at: NaiveDateTime,
27	    pub updated_at: NaiveDateTime,
28	}
29	
30	/// An immutable snapshot of a journal entry at a point in time.
31	///
32	/// Each edit creates a new version; the parent `Journal` always points at the
33	/// latest one via `current_version_id`.
34	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
35	pub struct JournalVersion {
36	    pub id: Uuid,
37	
38	    /// The journal this version belongs to.
39	    pub journal_id: Uuid,
40	
41	    /// Monotonically increasing version number (1-based).
42	    pub version_number: i32,
43	
44	    /// Full body content of the journal at this version.
45	    /// TODO: decide on rich-text format (Markdown / HTML / ProseMirror JSON) in phase 2.
46	    pub body: String,
47	
48	    /// Optional short note describing what changed in this version.
49	    pub change_summary: Option<String>,
50	
51	    /// Who created this version (may differ from original author for collaborative editing).
52	    pub edited_by: Uuid,
53	
54	    pub created_at: NaiveDateTime,
55	}
56

16	    /// Store an attachment under the given key.

{"description": "Build Phase 3 frontend library UI", "prompt": "Build the Phase 3 frontend pages for the Scholarly project. The backend now exposes these real endpoints (JSON unless noted): Journals - GET /api/v1/journals?limit=&offset= -> Vec - POST /api/v1/journals body: {title, abstract_text?, body, change_summary?} -> JournalView - GET /api/v1/journals/ -> JournalView - PUT /api/v1/journals/ body: {title?, body, change_summary?} -> JournalVersionView (creates a new draft version) - GET /api/v1/journals//versions -> Vec - GET /api/v1/journals//versions/ -> JournalVersionView - POST /api/v1/journals//versions//approve -> JournalVersionView - POST /api/v1/journals//versions//publish -> JournalView Teaching Resources (identical shape, except create body also has `resource_type`: one of document|video|presentation|assessment|external_link|dataset|other; edit body has description/content_url/mime_type/change_summary): - GET /api/v1/teaching-resources (same query params) - POST /api/v1/teaching-resources body: {title, resource_type, description?, content_url?, mime_type?, change_summary?} - GET /api/v1/teaching-resources/ - PUT /api/v1/teaching-resources/ - GET /api/v1/teaching-resources//versions - GET /api/v1/teaching-resources//versions/ - POST /api/v1/teaching-resources//versions//approve - POST /api/v1/teaching-resources//versions//publish Attachments - GET /api/v1/attachments?parent_type=journal|teaching_resource&parent_id= -> Vec - POST /api/v1/attachments multipart/form-data with fields: file, parent_type, parent_id, category? -> AttachmentView - GET /api/v1/attachments/ -> AttachmentView - GET /api/v1/attachments//preview -> raw bytes with Content-Type; sets X-Attachment-Checksum: sha256: and X-Attachment-Filename headers - DELETE /api/v1/attachments/ -> {ok:true} JournalView / ResourceView shape: { id, title, abstract_text?, author_id?, is_published, current_version_id?, latest_version_id?, created_at, updated_at, effective_version: {id, journal_id|resource_id, version_number, title?, body?, content_url?, mime_type?, size_bytes?, description?, change_summary?, state: "draft"|"approved"|"published"|"archived", created_by?, created_at, approved_by?, approved_at?, published_by?, published_at?} } AttachmentView shape: {id, parent_type, parent_id, original_filename, mime_type, size_bytes, sha256_checksum, category?, uploaded_by?, created_at, is_previewable} Error envelope (all failures): {"error": {"code","message","fields?","request_id"}} Frontend stack: Dioxus 0.6 with dioxus-router 0.6. Existing layout (MainLayout) already reads AuthState from context and hides nav items the caller's role can't use. Repo root: /home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/frontend/ Files to read first so you don't duplicate or break existing conventions: - src/router.rs (existing routes) - src/pages/journal_list.rs, src/pages/resource_list.rs (Phase 1 stubs — REPLACE these with real listings) - src/api/client.rs (ApiClient, ApiError) - src/api/auth.rs (pattern for typed API calls + RawUser conversion) - src/state/mod.rs (AuthState) - src/types/mod.rs (Role, User, Role::level) - src/layouts/main_layout.rs (shell and role badge) - assets/main.css (existing styles — append, don't rewrite) Work: 1) **src/api/journals.rs (new)** — typed calls: - pub struct JournalView (id: String, title: String, abstract_text: Option, author_id: Option, is_published: bool, current_version_id: Option, latest_version_id: Option, created_at: String, updated_at: String, effective_version: Option) - pub struct JournalVersionView (id, journal_id, version_number: i32, title: Option, body: Option, change_summary: Option, state: String, created_by: Option, created_at: String, approved_at: Option, published_at: Option) - pub struct JournalCreateInput {title, abstract_text?, body, change_summary?} Serialize - pub struct JournalEditInput {title?, body, change_summary?} Serialize - async fn list(token: &str, limit: u32, offset: u32) -> Result, ApiError> - async fn get(token: &str, id: &str) -> Result - async fn create(token: &str, input: &JournalCreateInput) -> Result - async fn edit(token: &str, id: &str, input: &JournalEditInput) -> Result - async fn list_versions(token: &str, id: &str) -> Result, ApiError> - async fn get_version(token: &str, id: &str, version_id: &str) -> Result - async fn approve(token: &str, id: &str, version_id: &str) -> Result - async fn publish(token: &str, id: &str, version_id: &str) -> Result All use ApiClient::new(Some(token.to_string())) and the existing post_json/get_json helpers. Implement a post_no_body_with_result(&self, path) helper in ApiClient if needed for approve/publish (or reuse post_json with an empty json body `serde_json::json!({})` — the backend handlers ignore the body). 2) **src/api/resources.rs (new)** — mirror of journals.rs but for teaching resources. Include ResourceType enum with #[serde(rename_all="snake_case")] variants matching the backend. - async fn list / get / create / edit / list_versions / get_version / approve / publish - POST base: /teaching-resources (note the hyphen!) 3) **src/api/attachments.rs (new)** — typed calls: - pub struct AttachmentView (all fields from the shape above as String/i64/bool) - pub enum ParentType { Journal, TeachingResource } with snake_case serde - async fn list_for_parent(token, parent_type: ParentType, parent_id: &str) -> Result, ApiError> - async fn get(token, id: &str) -> Result - async fn delete(token, id: &str) -> Result<(), ApiError> - async fn upload(token, parent_type: ParentType, parent_id: &str, file_name: &str, mime: &str, bytes: Vec, category: Option<&str>) -> Result Use gloo_net::http::Request with a FormData body. Construct the FormData via web_sys::FormData, append a Blob (via web_sys::Blob and Uint8Array), the file name, parent_type, parent_id, category. Send with Request::post(url).header("Authorization", format!("Bearer {}", token)).body(JsValue::from(form_data))?.send().await - async fn preview_url(id: &str) -> String — returns "/api/v1/attachments//preview" so an or iframe can load it directly. NOTE: that won't include the auth header, so for Phase 3 we'll use `fetch` from the page to grab the bytes with auth, then create an object URL via `URL::createObjectURL(&Blob::new_with_u8_array_sequence(...))`. Implement a helper async fn fetch_preview_blob_url(token: &str, id: &str) -> Result that does fetch with auth, reads the Blob, and creates a `blob:` URL. Return both the URL and the X-Attachment-Checksum header as a tuple (blob_url, checksum). 4) **src/pages/journal_list.rs (rewrite)** — production-grade list page: - Show loading / empty / error states. - Header row with title "Journals" and (if user has JournalWrite) a "New journal" button that opens an inline create form or navigates to a new-journal route. - List each journal as a card: title, published badge (green if is_published), "v{current_version.version_number} {state}" caption, last updated timestamp, "Open" link to /library/journals/. - Use use_future / spawn pattern to load on mount via `crate::api::journals::list(&token, 50, 0)`. - Auth token comes from `use_context::>()`. - Role-check: show the "New journal" button only when auth.user has Role::Admin or Role::Librarian. 5) **src/pages/journal_detail.rs (new)** — full journal page: - Fetches one journal by id (the router passes `id: String`). - Sections: - Header: title, published badge, "Publish v{n}" / "Approve v{n}" buttons if the current user can and the effective version state allows. - Effective version body rendered as 
 for now.
  - Version history panel: list of versions (loaded via list_versions, editor only), show version_number, state badge, created_at, a "View" button that selects the version (client-side, updates a `selected_version` signal) so the main body switches to the selected version's body. This is the "effective version selector" mentioned in the spec.
  - Edit panel (editor only): a form with title + body + change_summary that calls `edit()` on submit and then reloads the detail + version list. Show a clear "Saved as v{n} (draft)" confirmation.
  - Attachments panel (see component below).
- Loading/error/empty states throughout.
- Use inline validation: body must not be empty, title >= 3 chars if provided.
- Use disabled button + spinner while submitting.

6) **src/pages/resource_list.rs (rewrite)** — same shape as journal_list but for teaching resources. Show the resource_type badge next to the title.

7) **src/pages/resource_detail.rs (new)** — same shape as journal_detail but for teaching resources, using the resource_service shape (content_url, mime_type, description, change_summary). The edit form has those fields instead of body.

8) **src/components/attachment_panel.rs (new)** — reusable Dioxus component:

- Loads attachments via `attachments::list_for_parent(token, parent_type, parent_id)` on mount.
- Table of attachments showing: original_filename, category, size_bytes (humanized), mime_type, sha256_checksum (short — first 12 chars with a copy button, full on hover/title), uploaded_at.

Next to the preview button, show "checksum verified" once the fetch returns — the backend already re-verifies the SHA-256 before serving, so a successful 200 is proof enough; display the X-Attachment-Checksum header from the response as "sha256:abcdef...".

- If `can_upload`, show an upload form at the top:
