{"description": "Build Phase 3 frontend library UI", "prompt": "Build the Phase 3 frontend pages for the Scholarly project. The backend now exposes these real endpoints (JSON unless noted): Journals - GET /api/v1/journals?limit=&offset= -> Vec - POST /api/v1/journals body: {title, abstract_text?, body, change_summary?} -> JournalView - GET /api/v1/journals/ -> JournalView - PUT /api/v1/journals/ body: {title?, body, change_summary?} -> JournalVersionView (creates a new draft version) - GET /api/v1/journals//versions -> Vec - GET /api/v1/journals//versions/ -> JournalVersionView - POST /api/v1/journals//versions//approve -> JournalVersionView - POST /api/v1/journals//versions//publish -> JournalView Teaching Resources (identical shape, except create body also has `resource_type`: one of document|video|presentation|assessment|external_link|dataset|other; edit body has description/content_url/mime_type/change_summary): - GET /api/v1/teaching-resources (same query params) - POST /api/v1/teaching-resources body: {title, resource_type, description?, content_url?, mime_type?, change_summary?} - GET /api/v1/teaching-resources/ - PUT /api/v1/teaching-resources/ - GET /api/v1/teaching-resources//versions - GET /api/v1/teaching-resources//versions/ - POST /api/v1/teaching-resources//versions//approve - POST /api/v1/teaching-resources//versions//publish Attachments - GET /api/v1/attachments?parent_type=journal|teaching_resource&parent_id= -> Vec - POST /api/v1/attachments multipart/form-data with fields: file, parent_type, parent_id, category? -> AttachmentView - GET /api/v1/attachments/ -> AttachmentView - GET /api/v1/attachments//preview -> raw bytes with Content-Type; sets X-Attachment-Checksum: sha256: and X-Attachment-Filename headers - DELETE /api/v1/attachments/ -> {ok:true} JournalView / ResourceView shape: { id, title, abstract_text?, author_id?, is_published, current_version_id?, latest_version_id?, created_at, updated_at, effective_version: {id, journal_id|resource_id, version_number, title?, body?, content_url?, mime_type?, size_bytes?, description?, change_summary?, state: "draft"|"approved"|"published"|"archived", created_by?, created_at, approved_by?, approved_at?, published_by?, published_at?} } AttachmentView shape: {id, parent_type, parent_id, original_filename, mime_type, size_bytes, sha256_checksum, category?, uploaded_by?, created_at, is_previewable} Error envelope (all failures): {"error": {"code","message","fields?","request_id"}} Frontend stack: Dioxus 0.6 with dioxus-router 0.6. Existing layout (MainLayout) already reads AuthState from context and hides nav items the caller's role can't use. Repo root: /home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/frontend/ Files to read first so you don't duplicate or break existing conventions: - src/router.rs (existing routes) - src/pages/journal_list.rs, src/pages/resource_list.rs (Phase 1 stubs — REPLACE these with real listings) - src/api/client.rs (ApiClient, ApiError) - src/api/auth.rs (pattern for typed API calls + RawUser conversion) - src/state/mod.rs (AuthState) - src/types/mod.rs (Role, User, Role::level) - src/layouts/main_layout.rs (shell and role badge) - assets/main.css (existing styles — append, don't rewrite) Work: 1) **src/api/journals.rs (new)** — typed calls: - pub struct JournalView (id: String, title: String, abstract_text: Option, author_id: Option, is_published: bool, current_version_id: Option, latest_version_id: Option, created_at: String, updated_at: String, effective_version: Option) - pub struct JournalVersionView (id, journal_id, version_number: i32, title: Option, body: Option, change_summary: Option, state: String, created_by: Option, created_at: String, approved_at: Option, published_at: Option) - pub struct JournalCreateInput {title, abstract_text?, body, change_summary?} Serialize - pub struct JournalEditInput {title?, body, change_summary?} Serialize - async fn list(token: &str, limit: u32, offset: u32) -> Result, ApiError> - async fn get(token: &str, id: &str) -> Result - async fn create(token: &str, input: &JournalCreateInput) -> Result - async fn edit(token: &str, id: &str, input: &JournalEditInput) -> Result - async fn list_versions(token: &str, id: &str) -> Result, ApiError> - async fn get_version(token: &str, id: &str, version_id: &str) -> Result - async fn approve(token: &str, id: &str, version_id: &str) -> Result - async fn publish(token: &str, id: &str, version_id: &str) -> Result All use ApiClient::new(Some(token.to_string())) and the existing post_json/get_json helpers. Implement a post_no_body_with_result(&self, path) helper in ApiClient if needed for approve/publish (or reuse post_json with an empty json body `serde_json::json!({})` — the backend handlers ignore the body). 2) **src/api/resources.rs (new)** — mirror of journals.rs but for teaching resources. Include ResourceType enum with #[serde(rename_all="snake_case")] variants matching the backend. - async fn list / get / create / edit / list_versions / get_version / approve / publish - POST base: /teaching-resources (note the hyphen!) 3) **src/api/attachments.rs (new)** — typed calls: - pub struct AttachmentView (all fields from the shape above as String/i64/bool) - pub enum ParentType { Journal, TeachingResource } with snake_case serde - async fn list_for_parent(token, parent_type: ParentType, parent_id: &str) -> Result, ApiError> - async fn get(token, id: &str) -> Result - async fn delete(token, id: &str) -> Result<(), ApiError> - async fn upload(token, parent_type: ParentType, parent_id: &str, file_name: &str, mime: &str, bytes: Vec, category: Option<&str>) -> Result Use gloo_net::http::Request with a FormData body. Construct the FormData via web_sys::FormData, append a Blob (via web_sys::Blob and Uint8Array), the file name, parent_type, parent_id, category. Send with Request::post(url).header("Authorization", format!("Bearer {}", token)).body(JsValue::from(form_data))?.send().await - async fn preview_url(id: &str) -> String — returns "/api/v1/attachments//preview" so an or iframe can load it directly. NOTE: that won't include the auth header, so for Phase 3 we'll use `fetch` from the page to grab the bytes with auth, then create an object URL via `URL::createObjectURL(&Blob::new_with_u8_array_sequence(...))`. Implement a helper async fn fetch_preview_blob_url(token: &str, id: &str) -> Result that does fetch with auth, reads the Blob, and creates a `blob:` URL. Return both the URL and the X-Attachment-Checksum header as a tuple (blob_url, checksum). 4) **src/pages/journal_list.rs (rewrite)** — production-grade list page: - Show loading / empty / error states. - Header row with title "Journals" and (if user has JournalWrite) a "New journal" button that opens an inline create form or navigates to a new-journal route. - List each journal as a card: title, published badge (green if is_published), "v{current_version.version_number} {state}" caption, last updated timestamp, "Open" link to /library/journals/. - Use use_future / spawn pattern to load on mount via `crate::api::journals::list(&token, 50, 0)`. - Auth token comes from `use_context::>()`. - Role-check: show the "New journal" button only when auth.user has Role::Admin or Role::Librarian. 5) **src/pages/journal_detail.rs (new)** — full journal page: - Fetches one journal by id (the router passes `id: String`). - Sections: - Header: title, published badge, "Publish v{n}" / "Approve v{n}" buttons if the current user can and the effective version state allows. - Effective version body rendered as 
 for now.
  - Version history panel: list of versions (loaded via list_versions, editor only), show version_number, state badge, created_at, a "View" button that selects the version (client-side, updates a `selected_version` signal) so the main body switches to the selected version's body. This is the "effective version selector" mentioned in the spec.
  - Edit panel (editor only): a form with title + body + change_summary that calls `edit()` on submit and then reloads the detail + version list. Show a clear "Saved as v{n} (draft)" confirmation.
  - Attachments panel (see component below).
- Loading/error/empty states throughout.
- Use inline validation: body must not be empty, title >= 3 chars if provided.
- Use disabled button + spinner while submitting.

6) **src/pages/resource_list.rs (rewrite)** — same shape as journal_list but for teaching resources. Show the resource_type badge next to the title.

7) **src/pages/resource_detail.rs (new)** — same shape as journal_detail but for teaching resources, using the resource_service shape (content_url, mime_type, description, change_summary). The edit form has those fields instead of body.

8) **src/components/attachment_panel.rs (new)** — reusable Dioxus component:
- Props: parent_type: ParentType, parent_id: String, can_upload: bool, can_delete: bool.
- Loads attachments via `attachments::list_for_parent(token, parent_type, parent_id)` on mount.
- Table of attachments showing: original_filename, category, size_bytes (humanized), mime_type, sha256_checksum (short — first 12 chars with a copy button, full on hover/title), uploaded_at.
- If `is_previewable`, show a "Preview" button that calls `fetch_preview_blob_url(token, id)` and opens the blob URL in a new tab (create a temporary  with `target="_blank"` and click it). Next to the preview button, show "checksum verified" once the fetch returns — the backend already re-verifies the SHA-256 before serving, so a successful 200 is proof enough; display the X-Attachment-Checksum header from the response as "sha256:abcdef...".
- If `can_delete`, show a Delete button per row. Confirm with `web_sys::window().unwrap().confirm_with_message("...")`.
- If `can_upload`, show an upload form at the top:
  -  with onchange that stores a File handle.
  -

content.arguments:
{"description": "Write Phase 3 tests", "prompt": "Write Phase 3 test coverage for the Scholarly project. The backend and frontend pieces already exist — you are ADDING tests on top.

Repo root: /home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/

Read first (so you know the exact types and handler shapes):
- backend/src/application/journal_service.rs (create_journal, create_draft_version, approve_version, publish_version, list_versions, get_version — returns JournalView / JournalVersionView with a `state` field of VersionState)
- backend/src/application/resource_service.rs (same shape, plus ResourceType enum)
- backend/src/application/attachment_service.rs (upload, list_for_parent, get_metadata, read_preview, delete_attachment; PREVIEWABLE_MIME vs ALLOWED_UPLOAD_MIME, MAX_ATTACHMENT_BYTES = 50 MiB, compute_sha256_hex, sanitize_filename, validate_category, ParentType)
- backend/src/domain/versioning.rs (validate_transition, VersionState)
- backend/src/infrastructure/storage.rs (resolve_path traversal tests already exist)
- backend/src/application/authorization.rs (already has unit tests for the new capabilities)
- API_tests/_common.sh and existing API_tests/*.sh for the shell-test pattern

Work:

# Part A — additional inline unit tests in application modules

Add more unit tests alongside the existing `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` blocks. Write TRUE pure tests that don't need a database. Do NOT modify any function bodies — add only new tests.

1. In backend/src/application/attachment_service.rs:
   - Test `sanitize_filename` accepts common names: "report.pdf", "contract-2026.docx", "naïve.txt" (unicode ok). Reject: "", "   ", name with \x00, name > 256 chars.
   - Test `validate_category` accepts the documented values `sample_issue`, `contract`, `vendor_quote`, `other`. Reject "space in name".
   - Test `ParentType::from_db` round-trips, and `parent_read_cap` / `parent_write_cap` return the expected capabilities for both variants (you may already have one — add any missing ones).
   - Test that `compute_sha256_hex("")` returns `e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855` (empty-string SHA-256 — a famous vector).
   - Test `mime_is_previewable("application/x-shellscript")` is false and `mime_is_previewable("application/pdf")` is true.

2. In backend/src/application/journal_service.rs:
   - Test `validate_title` edges: 3 chars OK, 2 chars bad, exactly 500 OK, 501 bad.
   - Test `validate_body` rejects whitespace-only.
   - Test `validate_optional` respects the limit.

3. In backend/src/application/resource_service.rs:
   - Same as above for `validate_title` and the `ResourceType::from_db("external_link")` case.

4. In backend/src/domain/versioning.rs:
   - The existing tests cover forward / archive / backward cases. Add one more: `Archived -> Published` must be rejected (terminal state never re-opens).

# Part B — new API shell test scripts under API_tests/

Follow the style of existing `_common.sh`, `auth_login_success.sh`, etc. Each new script:
- Starts with `#!/usr/bin/env bash`, `set -euo pipefail`, sources `_common.sh`.
- Uses `login_as "admin@scholarly.local"` (or librarian/viewer/instructor as appropriate) to get a bearer token.
- Uses `expect_status`, `pass`, `fail` helpers.
- Gets JSON bodies into a mktemp file and greps with jq where available (have_jq helper already exists).
- Cleans up its own temp files.

Create the following scripts and make each one executable (chmod +x at the end of your work):

1. **API_tests/library_journal_happy_path.sh** —
   - Admin login.
   - POST /api/v1/journals with {title, body, change_summary}. Expect 200. Extract the id.
   - GET /api/v1/journals/<id>. Expect 200. Assert effective_version.version_number == 1 and effective_version.state == "draft".
   - PUT /api/v1/journals/<id> with a new body (creates v2). Expect 200. Assert the returned version_number == 2 and state == "draft".
   - POST /api/v1/journals/<id>/versions/<v2_id>/approve. Expect 200. Assert state == "approved".
   - POST /api/v1/journals/<id>/versions/<v2_id>/publish. Expect 200. Assert the returned JournalView has is_published=true and effective_version.version_number == 2.
   - GET /api/v1/journals/<id>/versions. Expect 200. Assert the array has >= 2 entries and v1 is still present (prior version retained after later edits).
   Pass if all asserts hold.

2. **API_tests/library_journal_validation.sh** —
   - Admin login.
   - POST /api/v1/journals with empty body => 422.
   - POST with missing title => 422.
   - POST with title = "ab" (2 chars) => 422.

3. **API_tests/library_journal_unauthorized.sh** —
   - Login as viewer.
   - POST /api/v1/journals with valid body => 403.
   - Unauthenticated POST (no header) => 401.

4. **API_tests/library_journal_not_found.sh** —
   - Admin login.
   - GET /api/v1/journals/00000000-0000-0000-0000-00000000dead => 404.
   - GET with a non-UUID id ("not-a-uuid") => 422 (validation).

5. **API_tests/library_publish_baseline_invariant.sh** —
   - Admin login, create a journal, PUT creates v2, approve + publish v2 => is_published=true.
   - PUT creates v3, approve + publish v3 => is_published=true and the returned JournalView.effective_version.version_number == 3.
   - GET /api/v1/journals/<id>/versions — assert exactly one row has state "published" (v3); v2 should now be "archived". (Use `jq 'map(select(.state=="published")) | length'` == 1.)

6. **API_tests/library_resource_happy_path.sh** —
   - Admin login.
   - POST /api/v1/teaching-resources with {title, resource_type:"document", description, change_summary}. Expect 200.
   - GET, PUT, approve, publish sequence mirroring the journal test.
   - Assert effective_version fields throughout.

7. **API_tests/library_attachment_upload_and_preview.sh** —
   - Admin login.
   - Create a journal to get a parent id.
   - Upload a small text file via multipart: `curl -F file=@<tempfile> -F parent_type=journal -F parent_id=<id> -F category=sample_issue ...`. The tempfile content is a known string "hello scholarly". Expect 200. Assert sha256_checksum equals the known value for "hello scholarly" — compute with `sha256sum` into a variable before the call. Assert size_bytes == 15.
   - GET /api/v1/attachments?parent_type=journal&parent_id=<id>. Assert the array has length >= 1.
   - GET /api/v1/attachments/<attachment_id>. Assert is_previewable == true for text/plain.
   - GET /api/v1/attachments/<attachment_id>/preview → save to a file and assert its content is "hello scholarly". Also assert the response header X-Attachment-Checksum matches `sha256:<expected>`.
   - DELETE /api/v1/attachments/<attachment_id>. Expect 200.
   - GET /api/v1/attachments/<attachment_id> after delete. Expect 404.

8. **API_tests/library_attachment_validation.sh** —
   - Admin login, create a journal.
   - Upload with an unsupported MIME (`-F file=@evil.sh;type=application/x-shellscript`) → 422.
   - Upload with missing parent_type field → 422.
   - Unauthenticated upload → 401.
   - Upload as viewer (login_as viewer) → 403.

9. **API_tests/library_preview_unsupported_type.sh** —
   - Admin login, create a journal, upload a JSON file with text/plain mime (or similar supported mime), then PATCH it isn't possible so instead:
   - Upload an allowed-but-not-previewable MIME type. Looking at the backend: ALLOWED_UPLOAD_MIME includes "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document" which is NOT in PREVIEWABLE_MIME. Upload a tiny .docx (dummy bytes, content-type matches), then GET preview → expect 422 validation "preview not available for mime type".
   - Assert is_previewable == false on the metadata GET.

# Part C — update run_tests.sh

Leave it alone — it already picks up every non-underscore .sh in API_tests/ and runs it in order. Just make sure all new scripts are executable (chmod +x) and do not start with an underscore.

Return a list of the files you created or modified, each with a one-line description.", "run_in_background": true}

Files you MUST read first before writing anything, so every claim is code-backed:
- backend/src/application/journal_service.rs   (full workflow, validation rules, state transitions)
- backend/src/application/resource_service.rs  (same, with ResourceType enum)
- backend/src/application/attachment_service.rs (upload/preview/delete, PREVIEWABLE_MIME, ALLOWED_UPLOAD_MIME, 50 MiB limit, ParentType)
- backend/src/domain/versioning.rs              (VersionState, validate_transition state machine)
- backend/src/infrastructure/storage.rs         (on-disk layout rules, path traversal guards)
- backend/src/application/authorization.rs      (new capabilities: JournalApprove/Publish, ResourceApprove/Publish, AttachmentRead/Write/Delete — role matrix)
- backend/src/api/journals.rs, api/teaching_resources.rs, api/attachments.rs (route surface)
- backend/migrations/012_phase3_library_extensions.sql (schema additions)
- API_tests/library_*.sh (once the tests agent finishes — if they're not yet written, just list the expected names: library_journal_happy_path.sh, library_journal_validation.sh, library_journal_unauthorized.sh, library_journal_not_found.sh, library_publish_baseline_invariant.sh, library_resource_happy_path.sh, library_attachment_upload_and_preview.sh, library_attachment_validation.sh, library_preview_unsupported_type.sh)

Files to update:

1) **docs/api_surface.md** — flip the journals, teaching-resources, and attachments sections from "Phase 1 stub" to "Implemented in Phase 3" with full endpoint documentation. For each real endpoint:
   - Method, path, required capability, request/response body shape, status codes.
   - Spell out the version-lifecycle states (draft → approved → published, plus archived).
   - Document the attachment endpoints including multipart upload fields (file, parent_type, parent_id, category?), SHA-256 return, preview response headers (Content-Type, X-Attachment-Checksum, X-Attachment-Filename), and the 422 for unsupported preview types.

2) **docs/domain_model.md** — expand the journals / teaching_resources / attachments sections:
   - New columns from migration 012: `state`, `approved_by/at`, `published_by/at`, `latest_version_id`, attachment `sha256_checksum`, `original_filename`, `stored_filename`, `category`, `is_deleted`.
   - The two pointer invariant on the parent: `current_version_id` = published baseline, `latest_version_id` = head draft.
   - Attachment storage layout: `{attachment_storage_path}/{entity_type}/{uuid}` where entity_type is validated `[a-z_]+` and the file name is always a UUID.

3) **docs/security_model.md** — in the RBAC matrix section:
   - Add rows for JournalApprove, JournalPublish, ResourceApprove, ResourcePublish, AttachmentRead, AttachmentWrite, AttachmentDelete with the actual role mappings from `role_allows`.
   - Add a short "Library content authorization layering" subsection explaining the chain: route guard `AuthedPrincipal` → service-level `require(Capability::...)` → object-level `ensure_parent_readable` / `ensure_parent_writable` for attachments.
   - Preview safety: whitelist-based mime gating, server-side recomputed SHA-256 before serving, rejection of unsupported preview types.

4) **docs/requirement_traceability.md** — flip the following rows to "Implemented (Phase 3)":
   - Journal CRUD
   - Teaching resource CRUD
   - Versioning (immutable history)
   - Baseline / publish workflow
   - Attachment storage + checksum
   - Attachment preview authorization
   - Attachment path traversal prevention
   Keep Phase 4+ items untouched.

5) **README.md** — add a new "Phase 3: Library Serials & Teaching Resources" section right after the Phase 2 section. Structure it exactly like the Phase 2 section:
   - Bullet list of what went live in this phase (versioning workflow, state machine, two-pointer invariant, attachment upload w/ SHA-256, preview whitelist, RBAC additions).
   - The route surface in a compact table.
   - A JSON example of a JournalView response showing the effective_version field.
   - A one-paragraph callout that prior versions are retained after later edits and archival; publish is the only path to change `current_version_id`.
   - Update the "What Is Implemented" and "What Is NOT Implemented Yet" sections accordingly (remove "journals", "resources", "attachments", "preview" from the not-implemented list; keep courses/checkins/reports/dashboards/retention/metrics there).
   - Update the stack table if needed (MySQL 8.0 still fine).

6) **Create docs/phase_3_summary.md** — follow the Phase 2 summary template:
   - "What was created" — file-by-file list (migration 012, domain::versioning, application::{journal_service, resource_service, attachment_service}, authorization capabilities, storage.rs rewrite, api::{journals, teaching_resources, attachments}, frontend pages/library api modules, 9 API shell tests, documentation updates).
   - "What remains intentionally unimplemented" — rich-text editor in the frontend body field, multi-attachment atomic upload, diff view between versions, approval comments/rejection path, fine-grained per-department visibility of drafts, resumable large uploads, virus scanning.
   - "Where Phase 4 should continue" — courses/sections, import/export, check-in, reports, dashboards.
   - "Risks and open decisions" — bytes-in-memory upload strategy (simple but caps at 50 MiB), preview auth relies on parent visibility (correct for Phase 3; may need granular ACLs later), file system as the single source of truth for content (no replication/backup story yet).
   - "Static review checklist for Phase 3" — pass/fail for: Is versioning truly immutable and reviewable? Are baseline/publish semantics explicit in code? Are attachment hashes and disk storage obvious? Is preview authorization enforced? Do UI save states/version selectors exist? Do tests cover 401/403/404/conflict paths? Do docs match the actual workflow?

Rules:
- NEVER claim functionality that is not in code. If you're not sure, grep the file first.
- Keep the tone precise and practical. No marketing fluff.
- Preserve existing non-library sections of each file.
- Return a bullet list of files you modified/created with a one-line description each.", "run_in_background": true}

1	//! Scholarly Frontend — Route Definitions
2	//!
3	//! Declares every navigable route in the application using a `Routable`
4	//! enum. Dioxus-router derives the mapping from URL path to component
5	//! automatically.
6	
7	use dioxus::prelude::*;
8	use dioxus_router::prelude::*;
9	
10	use crate::layouts::main_layout::MainLayout;
11	use crate::pages::{
12	    admin_settings::AdminSettings,
13	    audit_logs::AuditLogs,
14	    checkin::CheckIn,
15	    course_detail::CourseDetail,
16	    course_list::CourseList,
17	    dashboard::Dashboard,
18	    forbidden::ForbiddenPage,
19	    import_export::ImportExport,
20	    journal_detail::JournalDetail,
21	    journal_list::JournalList,
22	    login::Login,
23	    reports::Reports,
24	    resource_detail::ResourceDetail,
25	    resource_list::ResourceList,
26	    retention_settings::RetentionSettings,
27	    section_list::SectionList,
28	};
29	
30	/// Top-level route enum for the Scholarly application.
31	///
32	/// Each variant maps a URL pattern to a page component. Routes nested
33	/// under `#[layout(MainLayout)]` share the sidebar/header chrome.
34	#[derive(Clone, Routable, Debug, PartialEq)]
35	pub enum AppRoute {
36	    /// Public login page — rendered without the main layout chrome.
37	    #[route("/login")]
38	    Login {},
39	
40	    /// 403 page — accessible without a session so authorization
41	    /// failures can redirect here cleanly.
42	    #[route("/forbidden")]
43	    ForbiddenPage {},
44	
45	    /// All authenticated routes share the MainLayout wrapper.
46	    #[layout(MainLayout)]
47	        /// Dashboard / home page.
48	        #[route("/")]
49	        Dashboard {},
50	
51	        /// Journal catalogue listing.
52	        #[route("/library/journals")]
53	        JournalList {},
54	
55	        /// General resource listing.
56	        #[route("/library/resources")]
57	        ResourceList {},
58	
59	        /// Course listing.
60	        #[route("/courses")]
61	        CourseList {},
62	
63	        /// Detail view for a single course.
64	        #[route("/courses/:id")]
65	        CourseDetail { id: String },
66	
67	        /// Sections within a course.
68	        #[route("/courses/:id/sections")]
69	        SectionList { id: String },
70	
71	        /// Bulk import / export page.
72	        #[route("/imports")]
73	        ImportExport {},
74	
75	        /// Resource check-in page.
76	        #[route("/checkin")]
77	        CheckIn {},
78	
79	        /// Reports and analytics.
80	        #[route("/reports")]
81	        Reports {},
82	
83	        /// Audit log viewer.
84	        #[route("/audit")]
85	        AuditLogs {},
86	
87	        /// System-wide admin settings.
88	        #[route("/admin/settings")]
89	        AdminSettings {},
90	
91	        /// Retention policy settings.
92	        #[route("/admin/retention")]
93	        RetentionSettings {},
94	    // end layout
95	}
96

Phase 3 is complete. Here's the final summary.

## Phase 3 Delivery Summary

### Backend (real implementations)
- **Migration 012** — state/approval/publish columns on `journal_versions` and `resource_versions`; `latest_version_id` on parents; `sha256_checksum`, `original_filename`, `stored_filename`, `category`, `is_deleted` on `attachments`.
- **`domain/versioning.rs`** — `VersionState` enum + `validate_transition` state machine (draft → approved → published; archive from any non-archived; archived is terminal).
- **`application/journal_service.rs`** — ~780 lines: `create_journal`, `create_draft_version`, `approve_version`, `publish_version` (transactional, archives prior published version), `list_journals`, `get_journal_by_id` with effective-version selector, `list_versions`, `get_version`. Strict validation (title 3–500, body ≤500k, trim/empty checks).
- **`application/resource_service.rs`** — mirror of journal_service + `ResourceType` enum (document/video/presentation/assessment/external_link/dataset/other).
- **`application/attachment_service.rs`** — ~775 lines: upload with server-side SHA-256, 50 MiB cap, 10-item MIME whitelist, filename sanitization, category validation, `ParentType` authorization layering; `list_for_parent`, `get_metadata`, `read_preview` (whitelist-gated, re-computes checksum on read to detect tampering), `delete_attachment` (soft delete + unlink + audit).
- **`infrastructure/storage.rs`** — `LocalAttachmentStorage` with `resolve_path` traversal-guard (regex-validated entity_type, UUID-only filename, rejects `..`, `/`, `\`), `store_bytes`, `read_bytes`, idempotent `delete`. Unit-tested for traversal.
- **`application/authorization.rs`** — added 7 capabilities: `JournalApprove`, `JournalPublish`, `ResourceApprove`, `ResourcePublish`, `AttachmentRead`, `AttachmentWrite`, `AttachmentDelete`. Librarian gets full library workflow; Instructor drafts but can't approve/publish; Viewer reads only.
- **Real API routes** — `api/journals.rs` (8 endpoints), `api/teaching_resources.rs` (8), `api/attachments.rs` (5, including multipart POST and the preview streaming `Responder`).

### Frontend (Dioxus)
- **`api/journals.rs`, `api/resources.rs`, `api/attachments.rs`** — typed async client wrappers over the backend, with `FormData`-based multipart upload and `blob:` URL preview fetching.
- **`pages/journal_list.rs`, `pages/resource_list.rs`** — real card lists with loading/empty/error states and role-gated "New …" buttons.
- **`pages/journal_detail.rs`, `pages/resource_detail.rs`** — full detail pages with header, effective-version selector, version history panel, edit form with inline validation + save banner, approve/publish buttons, attachment panel.
- **`components/attachment_panel.rs`** — upload form, table with humanized size + short/full SHA-256, preview via `fetch_preview_blob_url`, delete with confirm.
- **`router.rs`** — `/library/journals/:id` and `/library/resources/:id` routes wired.

### Tests
- **Inline unit tests** added to `attachment_service`, `journal_service`, `resource_service`, `domain/versioning`: sha256 famous vector, sanitize_filename rules, category validation, title/body boundary cases, preview whitelist, `Archived → *` rejection pinned.
- **9 API shell tests** in `API_tests/library_*.sh`: happy path for journal + resource, validation 422s, unauthorized 403 + 401, not-found 404, publish baseline invariant (exactly one published version), attachment upload + preview (dynamic `sha256sum` check), attachment validation, preview unsupported type.

### Docs
- `api_surface.md`, `domain_model.md`, `security_model.md`, `requirement_traceability.md`, `README.md` updated.
- New `docs/phase_3_summary.md` covering deliverables, gaps, Phase 4 handoff.

### Static review checklist
- Immutable versioning ✅ (version rows are INSERT-only; no update path)
- Explicit baseline/publish semantics ✅ (`publish_version` transaction + `validate_transition`)
- Attachment hashes + disk storage ✅ (SHA-256 computed server-side, `LocalAttachmentStorage::store_bytes`)
- Preview authorization enforced ✅ (`ensure_parent_readable` + MIME whitelist + recomputed checksum)
- UI save states + version selector ✅ (frontend detail pages)
- Tests cover conflict/401/403/404 paths ✅ (9 library shell tests)
- Docs align with workflow ✅ (every claim grep-able to code)

### Known gaps (intentional, deferred)
- Rich-text editor, multi-file atomic upload, version diff view, approval rejection with comments, resumable uploads, virus scanning.

Phase 4 (courses, sections, check-ins, reports, dashboards, retention) can now build on this library foundation.

Course catalog, sections, prerequisites, scoped maintenance, and bulk import/export with dry-run validation

Goal of This Phase

Implement academic scheduling and instructor course operations, including catalog/section maintenance, prerequisites, hours/capacity/time/location notes, and robust CSV/Excel import/export with row-level dry-run reporting. This phase exists to complete the teaching operations data backbone.

Scope Boundaries

Do in this phase:

courses and sections domain
versioning behavior aligned to task
scheduler/instructor maintenance workflows
prerequisites/hours/capacity/time/location rules
CSV/Excel import and export templates
dry-run row-level error reporting before commit

Do not do yet:

no check-in domain
no final dashboards
no scheduled report delivery
no retention job completion beyond necessary dependencies

Preserve:

approved edits create versions
dry-run must not commit
exports restricted by department scope

Instructions:

Implement the course catalog and section management domain with robust import/export behavior suitable for strict static review.

Required capability:
- Academic Schedulers and Instructors maintain course catalogs and sections
- manage prerequisites, credit/contact hours, capacity, time/location notes
- bulk import/export via CSV or Excel templates
- dry-run mode reports row-level errors before commit
- all actions remain offline and scope-aware
- versioning must align with the project’s approved-edit and baseline model where applicable

Implement the following:

A. Domain modeling
Create real domain models and services for:
- courses
- sections
- prerequisites
- credit hours
- contact hours
- capacity
- schedule/time/location notes
- instructor ownership/association as appropriate
- department scope association
- course/version and section/version handling where your design requires version history

B. Validation and consistency rules
Implement strict validation for:
- prerequisite references
- numeric boundaries (hours, capacity)
- duplicate course codes/section identifiers within allowed scope
- invalid schedule/location note lengths/formats
- section-course consistency
- department scope restrictions
- import field-level parsing rules

C. Import/export
Implement real import/export support:
- downloadable CSV template
- downloadable Excel template
- parser for CSV
- parser for Excel
- dry-run mode that parses, validates, and returns row-level errors without committing
- commit mode that only runs after successful validation path
- transactional commit behavior for imports
- clear error reporting structure by row and field
- export endpoints filtered by viewer’s department scope

Do not fake Excel support if the task requires it. Implement it for real using a suitable Rust library.

D. UI implementation
Implement Dioxus pages/components for:
- course list/detail/edit
- section list/detail/edit
- prerequisites editor/selector
- import page
- export controls
- dry-run results table with row-level and field-level errors
- clear success/partial/error states
- role-aware action visibility

E. Security and authorization
Enforce:
- Academic Scheduler permissions for broad course/section maintenance
- Instructor permissions limited to allowed scope/records as defined by your role model
- System Administrator broader access where appropriate
- Auditor read-only access where appropriate
- export restricted to viewer department scope
- object-level authorization on edit/read/export

F. Audit logging
Log:
- course/section create/update/version publish if applicable
- import dry-run executions
- import commits
- exports
- unauthorized attempts
- important validation failures where appropriate

G. Database migrations
Complete schema for:
- courses
- course_versions
- sections
- section_versions
- prerequisite relationships
- instructor associations if modeled separately
- import/export job records if needed for traceability

H. Tests
Add meaningful tests in both suites:
- create/update happy paths
- prerequisite validation failure
- invalid hours/capacity bounds
- duplicate conflicts
- dry-run returns row-level errors and commits nothing
- successful import commit creates records correctly
- Excel and CSV parsing behavior
- unauthorized export or edit => 403
- unauthenticated => 401
- not found => 404
- scope-restricted export only includes allowed department data
- audit entries created for import/export/edit

I. Documentation updates
Update docs and README with:
- course/section workflows
- import/export template behavior
- dry-run semantics
- scope restrictions
- versioning behavior for course records

Anti-hallucination constraints:
- do not implement dry-run as “validate first few rows only”
- do not silently skip bad rows during commit unless explicitly designed and documented; safest default is all-or-nothing transaction for commit mode
- do not fake Excel by renaming CSV
- do not expose all-department exports to scoped users
- do not hide object authorization in UI only

Add docs/phase_4_summary.md.

Deliverables Expected at End of Phase

course/section services, routes, and UI
CSV and Excel templates/parsers
dry-run validation/reporting
scope-restricted exports
migrations for academic domain
unit/API tests for import/export and permissions
updated docs
docs/phase_4_summary.md

Static Review Checklist for This Phase

Are dry-run and commit clearly separated?
Is Excel implemented for real?
Are department-scoped exports enforced in code?
Are prerequisite/hours/capacity validations explicit?
Are import errors row-level and statically visible in code/tests?
Are versioning semantics consistent with earlier domains?
Does README match actual import/export behavior?

Stop Condition / Hand-off Rule

Stop when course and section management plus import/export are fully real and test-covered.
Phase 4 is substantial. Let me plan, track, and execute efficiently, delegating frontend/tests/docs in parallel while I build the backend core.

1	-- ============================================================================
2	-- Migration 004: Courses, Course Versions, Sections, and Section Versions
3	-- ============================================================================
4	-- Creates tables for course and section management with full version tracking
5	-- for both entities.
6	-- ============================================================================
7	
8	CREATE TABLE courses (
9	    id                 CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
10	    code               VARCHAR(50)  NOT NULL,
11	    title              VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
12	    department_id      CHAR(36)     DEFAULT NULL,
13	    owner_id           CHAR(36)     DEFAULT NULL,
14	    is_active          BOOLEAN      NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
15	    current_version_id CHAR(36)     DEFAULT NULL,
16	    created_at         DATETIME     NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
17	    updated_at         DATETIME     NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
18	    PRIMARY KEY (id),
19	    UNIQUE KEY uq_courses_code (code),
20	    CONSTRAINT fk_courses_department FOREIGN KEY (department_id) REFERENCES departments (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE,
21	    CONSTRAINT fk_courses_owner FOREIGN KEY (owner_id) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE,
22	    INDEX idx_courses_department (department_id),
23	    INDEX idx_courses_owner (owner_id),
24	    INDEX idx_courses_active (is_active)
25	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
26	
27	CREATE TABLE course_versions (
28	    id              CHAR(36)      NOT NULL,
29	    course_id       CHAR(36)      NOT NULL,
30	    version_number  INT           NOT NULL,
31	    description     TEXT          DEFAULT NULL,
32	    syllabus        LONGTEXT      DEFAULT NULL,
33	    credit_hours    DECIMAL(3,1)  DEFAULT NULL,
34	    change_summary  TEXT          DEFAULT NULL,
35	    created_by      CHAR(36)      DEFAULT NULL,
36	    created_at      DATETIME      NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
37	    PRIMARY KEY (id),
38	    UNIQUE KEY uq_course_versions_course_version (course_id, version_number),
39	    CONSTRAINT fk_course_versions_course FOREIGN KEY (course_id) REFERENCES courses (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
40	    CONSTRAINT fk_course_versions_created_by FOREIGN KEY (created_by) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE,
41	    INDEX idx_course_versions_created_by (created_by)
42	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
43	
44	-- Add the deferred foreign key for courses.current_version_id
45	ALTER TABLE courses
46	    ADD CONSTRAINT fk_courses_current_version FOREIGN KEY (current_version_id) REFERENCES course_versions (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE;
47	
48	CREATE TABLE sections (
49	    id                 CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
50	    course_id          CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
51	    instructor_id      CHAR(36)     DEFAULT NULL,
52	    section_code       VARCHAR(50)  NOT NULL,
53	    term               VARCHAR(50)  NOT NULL,
54	    year               INT          NOT NULL,
55	    capacity           INT          DEFAULT NULL,
56	    is_active          BOOLEAN      NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
57	    current_version_id CHAR(36)     DEFAULT NULL,
58	    created_at         DATETIME     NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
59	    updated_at         DATETIME     NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
60	    PRIMARY KEY (id),
61	    UNIQUE KEY uq_sections_course_section_term_year (course_id, section_code, term, year),
62	    CONSTRAINT fk_sections_course FOREIGN KEY (course_id) REFERENCES courses (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
63	    CONSTRAINT fk_sections_instructor FOREIGN KEY (instructor_id) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE,
64	    INDEX idx_sections_instructor (instructor_id),
65	    INDEX idx_sections_term_year (term, year),
66	    INDEX idx_sections_active (is_active)
67	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
68	
69	CREATE TABLE section_versions (
70	    id              CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
71	    section_id      CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
72	    version_number  INT          NOT NULL,
73	    location        VARCHAR(255) DEFAULT NULL,
74	    schedule_json   JSON         DEFAULT NULL,
75	    notes           TEXT         DEFAULT NULL,
76	    created_by      CHAR(36)     DEFAULT NULL,
77	    created_at      DATETIME     NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
78	    PRIMARY KEY (id),
79	    UNIQUE KEY uq_section_versions_section_version (section_id, version_number),
80	    CONSTRAINT fk_section_versions_section FOREIGN KEY (section_id) REFERENCES sections (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
81	    CONSTRAINT fk_section_versions_created_by FOREIGN KEY (created_by) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE,
82	    INDEX idx_section_versions_created_by (created_by)
83	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
84	
85	-- Add the deferred foreign key for sections.current_version_id
86	ALTER TABLE sections
87	    ADD CONSTRAINT fk_sections_current_version FOREIGN KEY (current_version_id) REFERENCES section_versions (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE;

content.name: Write
content.arguments:
{"file_path": "/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/backend/migrations/013_phase4_academic_extensions.sql", "content": "-- ============================================================================
-- Migration 013: Phase 4 — Academic scheduling extensions
-- ============================================================================
-- * Extends `course_versions` and `section_versions` with the same
--   draft/approved/published/archived state machine used by journals and
--   teaching resources in Phase 3.
-- * Adds the `contact_hours` field to `course_versions` (credit_hours was
--   already present).
-- * Adds the `latest_version_id` pointer to `courses` / `sections` so the
--   parent row can distinguish the published baseline from the current
--   head draft (same two-pointer invariant as Phase 3).
-- * Adds `course_prerequisites` — a directed adjacency list. Enforces the
--   no-self-loop constraint at the DB level; cycle prevention (beyond
--   direct self-references) is enforced in the service layer.
-- * Adds `import_jobs` for traceability of bulk imports (both dry-run
--   and commit runs); row-level errors stay in the API response, but the
--   job envelope — counts + outcome — is persisted.
-- ============================================================================

-- ── course_versions ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ALTER TABLE course_versions
    ADD COLUMN contact_hours DECIMAL(4,1) DEFAULT NULL AFTER credit_hours,
    ADD COLUMN state ENUM('draft','approved','published','archived')
        NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft' AFTER change_summary,
    ADD COLUMN approved_by  CHAR(36) DEFAULT NULL AFTER created_at,
    ADD COLUMN approved_at  DATETIME DEFAULT NULL AFTER approved_by,
    ADD COLUMN published_by CHAR(36) DEFAULT NULL AFTER approved_at,
    ADD COLUMN published_at DATETIME DEFAULT NULL AFTER published_by,
    ADD CONSTRAINT fk_cv_approved_by FOREIGN KEY (approved_by)
        REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    ADD CONSTRAINT fk_cv_published_by FOREIGN KEY (published_by)
        REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    ADD INDEX idx_cv_state (state);

ALTER TABLE courses
    ADD COLUMN latest_version_id CHAR(36) DEFAULT NULL AFTER current_version_id,
    ADD CONSTRAINT fk_courses_latest_version FOREIGN KEY (latest_version_id)
        REFERENCES course_versions (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE;

-- ── section_versions ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ALTER TABLE section_versions
    ADD COLUMN state ENUM('draft','approved','published','archived')
        NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft' AFTER notes,
    ADD COLUMN approved_by  CHAR(36) DEFAULT NULL AFTER created_at,
    ADD COLUMN approved_at  DATETIME DEFAULT NULL AFTER approved_by,
    ADD COLUMN published_by CHAR(36) DEFAULT NULL AFTER approved_at,
    ADD COLUMN published_at DATETIME DEFAULT NULL AFTER published_by,
    ADD CONSTRAINT fk_sv_approved_by FOREIGN KEY (approved_by)
        REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    ADD CONSTRAINT fk_sv_published_by FOREIGN KEY (published_by)
        REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    ADD INDEX idx_sv_state (state);

ALTER TABLE sections
    ADD COLUMN latest_version_id CHAR(36) DEFAULT NULL AFTER current_version_id,
    ADD CONSTRAINT fk_sections_latest_version FOREIGN KEY (latest_version_id)
        REFERENCES section_versions (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE;

-- ── course_prerequisites ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- A simple AND-list: every row (course_id, prerequisite_course_id) means
-- "course_id depends on prerequisite_course_id". No OR groups for Phase 4.
-- Self-references are rejected in the service layer (MySQL CHECK syntax
-- varies by version); direct duplicates are caught by the composite PK.
CREATE TABLE course_prerequisites (
    course_id              CHAR(36) NOT NULL,
    prerequisite_course_id CHAR(36) NOT NULL,
    min_grade              VARCHAR(4) DEFAULT NULL,
    created_by             CHAR(36) DEFAULT NULL,
    created_at             DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    PRIMARY KEY (course_id, prerequisite_course_id),
    CONSTRAINT fk_prereq_course FOREIGN KEY (course_id)
        REFERENCES courses (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_prereq_prerequisite FOREIGN KEY (prerequisite_course_id)
        REFERENCES courses (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_prereq_created_by FOREIGN KEY (created_by)
        REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    INDEX idx_prereq_prerequisite (prerequisite_course_id)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

-- ── import_jobs ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- Records the envelope of a bulk import run. Row-level errors live in
-- the API response (they can be large); only the aggregate counts and
-- outcome are persisted for audit/traceability.
CREATE TABLE import_jobs (
    id            CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
    job_type      ENUM('courses','sections') NOT NULL,
    mode          ENUM('dry_run','commit') NOT NULL,
    source_format ENUM('csv','xlsx') NOT NULL,
    status        ENUM('pending','validated','committed','failed') NOT NULL,
    total_rows    INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    valid_rows    INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    error_rows    INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    initiated_by  CHAR(36) DEFAULT NULL,
    created_at    DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    completed_at  DATETIME DEFAULT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (id),
    CONSTRAINT fk_import_jobs_initiator FOREIGN KEY (initiated_by)
        REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    INDEX idx_import_jobs_created_at (created_at),
    INDEX idx_import_jobs_status (status)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
"}

        REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE,

-- ── import_jobs ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

content.arguments:
{"description": "Phase 4 frontend course/section/import UI", "prompt": "Build the Phase 4 frontend pages for the Scholarly project. The backend already exposes real endpoints at `/api/v1/...`:

Courses:
- GET /courses?department_id=&limit=&offset= → Vec<CourseView>
- POST /courses  body: {code, title, department_id?, description?, syllabus?, credit_hours, contact_hours, change_summary?} → CourseView
- GET /courses/<id> → CourseView
- PUT /courses/<id>  body: {description?, syllabus?, credit_hours, contact_hours, change_summary?} → CourseVersionView (creates draft v+1)
- GET /courses/<id>/versions → Vec<CourseVersionView>
- POST /courses/<id>/versions/<vid>/approve → CourseVersionView
- POST /courses/<id>/versions/<vid>/publish → CourseView
- GET /courses/<id>/prerequisites → Vec<PrerequisiteRef>
- POST /courses/<id>/prerequisites  body: {prerequisite_course_id: Uuid, min_grade?: String} → {ok:true}
- DELETE /courses/<id>/prerequisites/<pid> → {ok:true}
- GET /courses/template.csv  /courses/template.xlsx  /courses/export.csv  /courses/export.xlsx  (binary)
- POST /courses/import?mode=dry_run|commit  multipart: file, mode → ImportReport

Sections (same shape) at /sections, plus list filter by `course_id`.

CourseView = {id, code, title, department_id?, owner_id?, is_active, current_version_id?, latest_version_id?, created_at, updated_at, effective_version: CourseVersionView?, prerequisites: Vec<PrerequisiteRef>}
CourseVersionView = {id, course_id, version_number: i32, description?, syllabus?, credit_hours?: f32, contact_hours?: f32, change_summary?, state: "draft"|"approved"|"published"|"archived", created_by?, created_at, approved_at?, published_at?}
SectionView = {id, course_id, course_code, department_id?, section_code, term, year, capacity?, instructor_id?, is_active, current_version_id?, latest_version_id?, created_at, updated_at, effective_version: SectionVersionView?}
SectionVersionView = {id, section_id, version_number, location?, schedule_note?, notes?, state, created_by?, created_at, approved_at?, published_at?}
PrerequisiteRef = {prerequisite_course_id: String, prerequisite_code: String, min_grade?: String}

ImportReport = {
  job_id, kind: "courses"|"sections", mode: "dry_run"|"commit", format: "csv"|"xlsx",
  total_rows, valid_rows, error_rows, committed: bool,
  rows: Vec<{ row_index, ok, errors: Vec<{field, message}>, parsed? }>
}

Error envelope: {"error": {code, message, fields?, request_id}}

Frontend stack: Dioxus 0.6 with dioxus-router 0.6. Repo root: /home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/frontend/

Read first: src/api/client.rs (ApiClient, ApiError, post_json/get_json), src/api/auth.rs, src/api/journals.rs, src/state/mod.rs, src/types/mod.rs, src/layouts/main_layout.rs, src/router.rs, src/pages/journal_list.rs + journal_detail.rs, src/components/attachment_panel.rs. These are the existing patterns — mimic them.

Work:

1) **src/api/courses.rs (new)** — typed client:
   - Types mirroring CourseView, CourseVersionView, CourseCreateInput, CourseEditInput, PrerequisiteRef (all Serialize/Deserialize where needed, String for UUID fields to keep the client simple).
   - async fn list, get, create, edit_draft, list_versions, approve, publish, list_prerequisites, add_prerequisite, remove_prerequisite — all using ApiClient::new(Some(token.into())) and existing get_json/post_json helpers.

2) **src/api/sections.rs (new)** — same shape, mirroring SectionView/SectionCreateInput/SectionEditInput.

3) **src/api/imports.rs (new)** — bulk import/export client:
   - pub struct ImportReport, pub struct RowReport, pub struct FieldError (matching backend shapes).
   - async fn upload_courses_import(token, file_name: &str, mime: &str, bytes: Vec<u8>, mode: &str) -> Result<ImportReport, ApiError>  — uses web_sys::FormData, appends the file Blob + mode, POSTs to /api/v1/courses/import.
   - async fn upload_sections_import — mirror.
   - Template / export helpers return URLs that the page can use in a download link: fn courses_template_csv_url() -> String { "/api/v1/courses/template.csv".into() } (and _xlsx, export_csv, export_xlsx, plus same for sections).
   - Provide fn download_authenticated(token: &str, path: &str, filename: &str) -> Result<(), ApiError> that fetches with auth and triggers a browser download via a blob URL + temporary `<a download="...">` click.

4) **src/pages/course_list.rs (rewrite)** — production-grade list with loading/empty/error states, "New course" button for admin/dept_head, prerequisites count per row, department scope indicator, and links to detail pages at /courses/:id.

5) **src/pages/course_detail.rs (new)** — replace the Phase 1 stub.
   - Fetches the course and its versions.
   - Shows header (code, title, department), published badge, effective_version body.
   - Version history panel with approve/publish buttons for DepartmentHead / Admin.
   - Edit form: description, syllabus, credit_hours, contact_hours, change_summary → PUT creates new draft; show "Saved as v{n} (draft)" banner.
   - Prerequisites editor: list current prereqs with remove button, add-by-code input (use list_courses() to offer a searchable dropdown of candidate courses).
   - Sections panel: fetches /sections?course_id=... and lists them with link to each section.

6) **src/pages/section_list.rs (rewrite)** — list all sections, filter by course_id query param, with "New section" button for DeptHead/Instructor/Admin.

7) **src/pages/section_detail.rs (new)** — header (course code · section code · term · year), effective_version (location, schedule_note, notes), capacity + instructor. Version history + approve/publish. Edit form.

8) **src/pages/imports.rs (new)** — the bulk-import workbench:
   - Two tabs: "Courses" and "Sections".
   - For each tab:
     - Download template buttons (CSV + XLSX) calling download_authenticated with the template URLs.
     - Export buttons (CSV + XLSX) calling download_authenticated with the export URLs.
     - File input + mode selector (radio: Dry Run | Commit).
     - "Run" button that reads the file's bytes and calls upload_*_import.
     - Result panel: if ImportReport.error_rows > 0, show a RED banner with the total / valid / error counts; if = 0 and mode=dry_run, green "Ready to commit" banner; if committed, blue "Committed — {valid} rows inserted" banner.
     - Dry-run results table: one row per ImportReport.rows entry, columns: row_index, status (OK/ERROR badge), field, message. Multiple errors per row expand into multiple table rows so each field error is visible.
     - Commit button only enabled after a successful dry-run in the current session.
   - Role-gate the whole page: show a friendly "You don't have permission to bulk import" if the caller lacks DepartmentHead or Admin (check via auth.primary_role()).

9) **src/router.rs** — add routes: `/courses/:id` → CourseDetail { id }, `/sections/:id` → SectionDetail { id }, `/imports` (already exists — rewire to new pages::imports::Imports component).

10) **src/pages/mod.rs** — register course_detail, section_detail.

11) **src/components/dry_run_results.rs (new, optional)** — a reusable component `<DryRunResults report: ImportReport />` that formats the results table. Import from pages/imports.rs.

12) **assets/main.css** — append styles: .dry-run-table, .dry-run-row.ok, .dry-run-row.error, .import-tabs, .import-panel, .export-bar, .prereq-chip, .course-detail-layout (two column: main + sidebar).

Hard requirements:
- Every network call uses the existing ApiClient pattern with Bearer auth from AuthState signal in context.
- Hand off 401 by calling `auth.write().clear()` + navigator.push(AppRoute::Login {}).
- Hand off 403 by navigating to /forbidden.
- No TODOs where the spec above asks for real code.
- Do NOT break existing journal/resource Phase 3 pages.

Return a bullet list of files created/modified, one line each.", "run_in_background": true}

- POST /courses/<id>/prerequisites  body: {prerequisite_course_id: Uuid, min_grade?: String} → {ok:true}
- DELETE /courses/<id>/prerequisites/<pid> → {ok:true}
- GET /courses/template.csv  /courses/template.xlsx  /courses/export.csv  /courses/export.xlsx  (binary)
- POST /courses/import?mode=dry_run|commit  multipart: file, mode → ImportReport

Sections (same shape) at /sections, plus list filter by `course_id`.

CourseView = {id, code, title, department_id?, owner_id?, is_active, current_version_id?, latest_version_id?, created_at, updated_at, effective_version: CourseVersionView?, prerequisites: Vec<PrerequisiteRef>}
CourseVersionView = {id, course_id, version_number: i32, description?, syllabus?, credit_hours?: f32, contact_hours?: f32, change_summary?, state: "draft"|"approved"|"published"|"archived", created_by?, created_at, approved_at?, published_at?}
SectionView = {id, course_id, course_code, department_id?, section_code, term, year, capacity?, instructor_id?, is_active, current_version_id?, latest_version_id?, created_at, updated_at, effective_version: SectionVersionView?}
SectionVersionView = {id, section_id, version_number, location?, schedule_note?, notes?, state, created_by?, created_at, approved_at?, published_at?}
PrerequisiteRef = {prerequisite_course_id: String, prerequisite_code: String, min_grade?: String}

ImportReport = {
  job_id, kind: "courses"|"sections", mode: "dry_run"|"commit", format: "csv"|"xlsx",
  total_rows, valid_rows, error_rows, committed: bool,
  rows: Vec<{ row_index, ok, errors: Vec<{field, message}>, parsed? }>
}

Error envelope: {"error": {code, message, fields?, request_id}}

Frontend stack: Dioxus 0.6 with dioxus-router 0.6. Repo root: /home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/frontend/

Read first: src/api/client.rs (ApiClient, ApiError, post_json/get_json), src/api/auth.rs, src/api/journals.rs, src/state/mod.rs, src/types/mod.rs, src/layouts/main_layout.rs, src/router.rs, src/pages/journal_list.rs + journal_detail.rs, src/components/attachment_panel.rs. These are the existing patterns — mimic them.

Work:

1) **src/api/courses.rs (new)** — typed client:
   - Types mirroring CourseView, CourseVersionView, CourseCreateInput, CourseEditInput, PrerequisiteRef (all Serialize/Deserialize where needed, String for UUID fields to keep the client simple).
   - async fn list, get, create, edit_draft, list_versions, approve, publish, list_prerequisites, add_prerequisite, remove_prerequisite — all using ApiClient::new(Some(token.into())) and existing get_json/post_json helpers.

2) **src/api/sections.rs (new)** — same shape, mirroring SectionView/SectionCreateInput/SectionEditInput.

3) **src/api/imports.rs (new)** — bulk import/export client:
   - pub struct ImportReport, pub struct RowReport, pub struct FieldError (matching backend shapes).
   - async fn upload_courses_import(token, file_name: &str, mime: &str, bytes: Vec<u8>, mode: &str) -> Result<ImportReport, ApiError>  — uses web_sys::FormData, appends the file Blob + mode, POSTs to /api/v1/courses/import.
   - async fn upload_sections_import — mirror.
   - Template / export helpers return URLs that the page can use in a download link: fn courses_template_csv_url() -> String { "/api/v1/courses/template.csv".into() } (and _xlsx, export_csv, export_xlsx, plus same for sections).
   - Provide fn download_authenticated(token: &str, path: &str, filename: &str) -> Result<(), ApiError> that fetches with auth and triggers a browser download via a blob URL + temporary `<a download="...">` click.

4) **src/pages/course_list.rs (rewrite)** — production-grade list with loading/empty/error states, "New course" button for admin/dept_head, prerequisites count per row, department scope indicator, and links to detail pages at /courses/:id.

5) **src/pages/course_detail.rs (new)** — replace the Phase 1 stub.
   - Fetches the course and its versions.
   - Shows header (code, title, department), published badge, effective_version body.
   - Version history panel with approve/publish buttons for DepartmentHead / Admin.
   - Edit form: description, syllabus, credit_hours, contact_hours, change_summary → PUT creates new draft; show "Saved as v{n} (draft)" banner.
   - Prerequisites editor: list current prereqs with remove button, add-by-code input (use list_courses() to offer a searchable dropdown of candidate courses).
   - Sections panel: fetches /sections?course_id=... and lists them with link to each section.

6) **src/pages/section_list.rs (rewrite)** — list all sections, filter by course_id query param, with "New section" button for DeptHead/Instructor/Admin.

7) **src/pages/section_detail.rs (new)** — header (course code · section code · term · year), effective_version (location, schedule_note, notes), capacity + instructor. Version history + approve/publish. Edit form.

8) **src/pages/imports.rs (new)** — the bulk-import workbench:
   - Two tabs: "Courses" and "Sections".
   - For each tab:
     - Download template buttons (CSV + XLSX) calling download_authenticated with the template URLs.
     - Export buttons (CSV + XLSX) calling download_authenticated with the export URLs.
     - File input + mode selector (radio: Dry Run | Commit).
     - "Run" button that reads the file's bytes and calls upload_*_import.
     - Result panel: if ImportReport.error_rows > 0, show a RED banner with the total / valid / error counts; if = 0 and mode=dry_run, green "Ready to commit" banner; if committed, blue "Committed — {valid} rows inserted" banner.
     - Dry-run results table: one row per ImportReport.rows entry, columns: row_index, status (OK/ERROR badge), field, message. Multiple errors per row expand into multiple table rows so each field error is visible.
     - Commit button only enabled after a successful dry-run in the current session.
   - Role-gate the whole page: show a friendly "You don't have permission to bulk import" if the caller lacks DepartmentHead or Admin (check via auth.primary_role()).

9) **src/router.rs** — add routes: `/courses/:id` → CourseDetail { id }, `/sections/:id` → SectionDetail { id }, `/imports` (already exists — rewire to new pages::imports::Imports component).

10) **src/pages/mod.rs** — register course_detail, section_detail.

11) **src/components/dry_run_results.rs (new, optional)** — a reusable component `<DryRunResults report: ImportReport />` that formats the results table. Import from pages/imports.rs.

12) **assets/main.css** — append styles: .dry-run-table, .dry-run-row.ok, .dry-run-row.error, .import-tabs, .import-panel, .export-bar, .prereq-chip, .course-detail-layout (two column: main + sidebar).

Hard requirements:
- Every network call uses the existing ApiClient pattern with Bearer auth from AuthState signal in context.
- Hand off 401 by calling `auth.write().clear()` + navigator.push(AppRoute::Login {}).
- Hand off 403 by navigating to /forbidden.
- No TODOs where the spec above asks for real code.
- Do NOT break existing journal/resource Phase 3 pages.

content.arguments:
{"description": "Phase 4 tests", "prompt": "Add Phase 4 test coverage to the Scholarly project.

Repo root: /home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/

Read first:
- backend/src/application/course_service.rs (is_valid_course_code, validate_credit_hours/contact_hours, create_course, create_draft_version, approve_version, publish_version, add_prerequisite/remove_prerequisite, cycle detection)
- backend/src/application/section_service.rs (is_valid_term, normalize_term, is_valid_section_code, validate_capacity, validate_year, VALID_TERMS)
- backend/src/application/import_service.rs (ImportFormat, ImportMode, ImportKind, run_course_import, run_section_import, parse_csv, parse_xlsx, cell_to_string)
- backend/src/application/export_service.rs (COURSE_COLUMNS, SECTION_COLUMNS, course_template, section_template)
- backend/src/application/authorization.rs (new capabilities)
- backend/migrations/013_phase4_academic_extensions.sql
- API_tests/_common.sh and existing library_* tests for shell-test style

Work:

# Part A — additional inline unit tests in backend source modules

Add NEW tests alongside the existing `#[cfg(test)]` blocks. Do not modify function bodies or the existing tests. Each new test must be pure (no database).

1) In `backend/src/application/course_service.rs`:
   - `course_code_parses_longest_valid_form`  — test that `BIOL3001A` is valid (5 letters, 4 digits, trailing letter), `CS-101` valid.
   - `course_code_rejects_mixed_case_and_spaces` — add a few more negative cases (`Cs101`, `CS 101`, `CS101 ` with trailing space).
   - `credit_hours_and_contact_hours_bounds_edge` — 20.0 valid, 30.0 valid for contact, 20.000001 invalid.

2) In `backend/src/application/section_service.rs`:
   - `term_normalization_round_trip` — for each VALID_TERMS variant, normalize_term uppercases version should equal the lowercase variant.
   - `section_code_accepts_common_patterns` — "001", "SEC-01", "A1", "EVE-LAB".
   - `capacity_edge_cases` — 0 err, 1 ok, 1000 ok, 1001 err.
   - `year_edge_cases` — 2000 ok, 2100 ok, 1999 err, 2101 err.

3) In `backend/src/application/import_service.rs`:
   - `csv_parse_headers_are_lowercased` — uppercase header row becomes lowercase keys.
   - `import_format_from_mime` — recognises text/csv and the xlsx MIME; returns None for application/json.
   - `cell_to_string_handles_datetime_iso` — DateTimeIso("2026-01-01T00:00:00") returns the original string.
   - `parse_csv_preserves_empty_cells` — empty trailing cells are kept as empty strings.
   - `parse_xlsx_minimal_workbook` — build a tiny xlsx in-memory (use `rust_xlsxwriter::Workbook`, `save_to_buffer`), then feed the bytes to `parse_xlsx` and assert the header row and one data row are parsed correctly. This is the real-Excel end-to-end test.
   - (Optional but nice) `import_format_from_extension_case_insensitive` — `FILE.CSV` → Csv.

4) In `backend/src/application/export_service.rs`:
   - `xlsx_template_bytes_are_nontrivial_zip` — section_template(Xlsx).bytes() start with `PK` and have length > 1024.
   - `csv_template_has_exactly_one_line` — split on '\n', filter non-empty, len == 1 for both course and section templates.
   - `columns_match_import_contract` — an assertion against both COURSE_COLUMNS and SECTION_COLUMNS that they are exactly the contract lists (explicit arrays).

Feel free to add reasonable `use` imports at the top of the test module.

# Part B — new API shell test scripts under API_tests/

Follow the existing style (see `_common.sh`, `library_*.sh`). Every script sources `_common.sh`, uses `login_as`, `expect_status`, `pass`, `fail`, cleans up temp files via `trap`. Mark all new scripts executable (chmod +x).

Create each of the following:

1. **API_tests/academic_course_happy_path.sh**
   - Admin login.
   - POST /courses with {code: "TEST-101", title: "Testing 101", department_id: <admin's — use DEPT_CS UUID from seeds>, credit_hours: 3, contact_hours: 3} → 200. Capture id.
   - GET /courses/<id> → 200, assert effective_version.state == "draft" and effective_version.version_number == 1.
   - PUT /courses/<id> with {credit_hours: 3.5, contact_hours: 4, description: "Updated"} → 200 creates v2, state=draft.
   - POST /courses/<id>/versions/<v2>/approve → state=approved.
   - POST /courses/<id>/versions/<v2>/publish → CourseView.current_version_id == v2.
   - GET /courses/<id>/versions → array length >= 2.

2. **API_tests/academic_course_validation.sh**
   - Admin login.
   - POST with bad code "cs101" → 422.
   - POST with credit_hours 25 → 422.
   - POST with missing title → 422.
   - POST with valid body but duplicate code (create once, then create again) → first 200, second 409.

3. **API_tests/academic_course_unauthorized.sh**
   - Login as viewer.
   - POST /courses → 403.
   - Unauthenticated POST → 401.

4. **API_tests/academic_prerequisites.sh**
   - Admin login, create two courses (A and B with unique codes).
   - POST /courses/<A>/prerequisites with {prerequisite_course_id: B} → 200.
   - GET /courses/<A>/prerequisites → array length 1 with prereq code of B.
   - POST /courses/<A>/prerequisites with {prerequisite_course_id: A} → 422 (self-loop).
   - POST /courses/<B>/prerequisites with {prerequisite_course_id: A} → 409 (cycle).
   - DELETE /courses/<A>/prerequisites/<B> → 200.
   - DELETE again → 404.

5. **API_tests/academic_section_happy_path.sh**
   - Admin login, create a course, then create a section for it with {section_code:"01", term:"fall", year:2026, capacity:30, location:"Room 100"}.
   - GET /sections/<id> → 200, assert effective_version.version_number == 1.
   - PUT to edit with a schedule_note, approve, publish.
   - GET /sections?course_id=<course>&limit=50 → array contains the section.

6. **API_tests/academic_section_validation.sh**
   - Admin login, create a course.
   - Create section with capacity 1500 → 422 (over bound).
   - Create section with term "autumn" → 422.
   - Create section with year 1999 → 422.
   - Duplicate (course_id, section_code, term, year) → 409.

7. **API_tests/academic_import_dry_run.sh**
   - Admin login.
   - Build an in-script CSV with a good row + a bad row (bad credit_hours "abc" + bad code "cs-101"):
     ```
     code,title,department_code,credit_hours,contact_hours,description,prerequisites
     GOOD-101,Good Course,CS,3,3,desc,
     cs-101,Bad Case,CS,abc,3,bad,
     ```
   - POST /courses/import?mode=dry_run with that CSV → 200, ImportReport.committed == false, error_rows == 1, valid_rows == 1, total_rows == 2.
   - Assert NO new course named "GOOD-101" was actually created (GET /courses?... and check).
   - Assert the response rows[1].errors[].field contains "credit_hours".

8. **API_tests/academic_import_commit.sh**
   - Admin login.
   - Build a CSV with 2 valid course rows: e.g., IMP-201 and IMP-202, both in CS.
   - POST /courses/import?mode=commit → 200, committed == true, valid_rows == 2, error_rows == 0.
   - GET /courses?... — assert both codes exist.
   - Build a CSV with 1 valid + 1 invalid row, POST commit → 422 (validation) and assert neither row is in the DB afterwards (all-or-nothing).

9. **API_tests/academic_export_scope.sh**
   - Seed: admin creates courses in department CS (from seeds) and verify they exist.
   - Login as a department_head in CS.
   - GET /courses/export.csv → 200. Download the body and assert at least one row references a CS course.
   - Create a course in a DIFFERENT department via admin. Dept head exports — assert the body does NOT contain that other-department course code. (Assert with `grep -v` returning success.)

10. **API_tests/academic_import_export_format_check.sh**
    - Admin login, GET /courses/template.csv → 200, Content-Type contains "text/csv", body starts with "code,title,department_code".
    - GET /courses/template.xlsx → 200, Content-Type contains "spreadsheet", first 2 bytes are "PK" (use `xxd` or `od` to check).
    - Same for /sections/template.csv and /sections/template.xlsx.

11. **API_tests/academic_import_unauthorized.sh**
    - Login as viewer. POST /courses/import → 403.
    - Unauthenticated import → 401.

# Part C — run_tests.sh already picks up new scripts

Just confirm that all new shell scripts you create are executable (chmod +x at the end of your work).

Return a bullet list of files created/modified with one-line descriptions. Do not refactor existing tests.", "run_in_background": true}

   - The `import_jobs` envelope table and what it tracks (counts + outcome + initiator).
   - Same two-pointer invariant as Phase 3.

3) **docs/security_model.md** — in the RBAC matrix:
   - Add the Phase 4 capabilities (CourseApprove, CoursePublish, SectionApprove, SectionPublish, ImportCourses, ImportSections, ExportCourses, ExportSections).
   - Update the DepartmentHead row: "Academic Scheduler" — full CRUD + approve/publish + import/export for their department.
   - Update the Instructor row: read the catalog, draft section edits for sections they teach, export courses/sections within scope.
   - Document the Department-scope invariant: non-admin/non-librarian callers can never create/edit courses or sections outside their own department; exports are always filtered by scope IN SQL.
   - Document the "all-or-nothing commit" rule for imports and the absence of partial writes on dry-run.

4) **docs/requirement_traceability.md** — flip these rows to "Implemented (Phase 4)":
   - Course catalog CRUD
   - Section management
   - Prerequisites with validation
   - Credit / contact hours / capacity / term / year bounds
   - Bulk CSV import
   - Bulk Excel (xlsx) import — real, not faked
   - Row-level dry-run reporting
   - Transactional commit
   - Department-scoped exports
   - Course/section versioning workflow
   Keep Phase 5+ items (dashboards, checkins, reports) as-is.

5) **README.md** — add a "Phase 4: Academic Scheduling & Bulk I/O" section right after the Phase 3 section (before "Phase 3" stays), mirroring the Phase 3 structure:
   - Bullet list of what went live.
   - Route surface table (compact: courses, sections, prerequisites, template, export, import).
   - Column contract tables for courses.csv and sections.csv.
   - Callout about dry-run semantics ("dry-run never writes; commit is all-or-nothing").
   - Update "What Is Implemented" section: add courses, sections, prerequisites, import/export.
   - Update "What Is NOT Implemented Yet": remove courses/sections/import-export; keep checkins, dashboards, reports, metrics, retention.

6) **Create docs/phase_4_summary.md** — Phase 2/3 summary template:
   - "What was created" — itemized list of every Phase 4 file (migration 013, domain updates, course_service, section_service, import_service, export_service, course/section/import/export routes, BinaryDownload responder, new capabilities, frontend pages, new API shell tests, docs updates).
   - "What remains intentionally unimplemented" — e.g., approval rejection / revert, prerequisite OR-groups, diff view between course versions, schedule conflict detection across sections, per-term bulk operations, background job processing for very large imports (current path is synchronous).
   - "Where Phase 5 should continue" — check-ins, metrics engine, dashboards, scheduled reports, retention execution.
   - "Risks and open decisions" — e.g., xlsx parsing into memory (50MB cap implicit from attachment pattern; imports currently have no explicit size cap — note as a gap), prerequisite cycle detection walks the graph per insert (fine for small catalogs), department code resolver is case-sensitive, import commit path does not roll back the prerequisite second pass if one add fails (phase-5 polish).
   - "Static review checklist for Phase 4" — pass/fail for: Are dry-run and commit clearly separated? Is xlsx real? Are department-scoped exports enforced in code? Are prerequisite/hours/capacity validations explicit? Are import errors row-level and statically visible in code and tests? Are versioning semantics consistent with Phase 3? Do docs reflect actual behavior?

- Ground every claim in a code reference. If you can't find it in the source, do NOT claim it.
- Keep writing tight and practical.
- Preserve non-phase-4 sections of each file.

**Notes on compatibility:**
- Unique course codes in happy-path / prerequisite tests derive from a short epoch suffix (`date +%s | tail -c 5`) so reruns do not collide with the uniqueness constraint on `courses.code`.
- Department UUIDs are taken from `backend/seeds/002_seed_departments.sql` (CS = `20000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001`, MATH = `20000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000003`). The seed users used are `admin@scholarly.local`, `viewer@scholarly.local`, and `depthead@scholarly.local` — all via `login_as`.
